  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactures the device (e.g., Apple, Samsung, Nokia, Huawei, Cisco, Ericsson).']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Commercial marketing name of the device model as presented to customers (e.g., iPhone 14 Pro, Galaxy S23 Ultra, Nokia ONT G-010S-A).']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned model number or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) used for inventory and catalog management. Unique identifier within manufacturer\'s product line.']
  nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports NFC (Near Field Communication) for contactless payments and data exchange. Relevant for mobile payment services and digital wallet integration.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system platform and version running on the device (e.g., iOS 17, Android 14, HarmonyOS, proprietary firmware). Impacts application compatibility and OTT (Over-The-Top) service delivery.']
  processor_chipset varchar [note: 'Processor or chipset model powering the device (e.g., Apple A17 Pro, Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3, MediaTek Dimensity 9300). Indicates device performance tier.']
  ram_capacity_gb varchar [note: 'RAM memory capacity in gigabytes. Impacts device performance and ability to run advanced applications and services.']
  retail_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manufacturer suggested retail price (MSRP) or standard retail price in the base currency. Used for pricing strategies, promotional offers, and revenue forecasting (ARPU - Average Revenue Per User calculations).']
  sar_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) value in watts per kilogram (W/kg), measuring radio frequency energy absorption. Required for regulatory compliance (FCC, ICNIRP guidelines) for mobile devices.']
  storage_capacity_gb varchar [note: 'Internal storage capacity of the device in gigabytes. Relevant for smartphones, tablets, and STB (Set-Top Box) devices with local recording capability.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the primary supplier or distributor for this device model. Used for procurement, inventory management, and supply chain tracking in ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) systems.']
  tac_code varchar [note: '8-digit Type Allocation Code forming the first 8 digits of the IMEI. Uniquely identifies the device model and manufacturer. Used by network systems for device identification and policy enforcement.']
  technology_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of supported network technologies and standards (e.g., 4G LTE, 5G NR, VoLTE, Wi-Fi 6, Wi-Fi 6E, GPON, Bluetooth 5.2). Critical for network compatibility and service provisioning.']
  usb_ports varchar [note: 'Number of USB ports available on the device. Used for charging, data transfer, and peripheral connectivity.']
  video_codec_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of supported video codecs (e.g., H.264, H.265/HEVC, VP9, AV1). Essential for IPTV, OTT content delivery, and video call quality.']
  voip_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports VoIP (Voice over Internet Protocol) functionality. Critical for IMS (IP Multimedia Subsystem) service delivery and VoLTE (Voice over LTE) compatibility.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard manufacturer warranty period in months. Used for service provisioning, insurance offerings, and customer support planning.']
  water_resistance_rating varchar [note: 'IP (Ingress Protection) rating for water and dust resistance (e.g., IP67, IP68). Indicates device durability and suitability for outdoor or harsh environments.']
  weight_grams varchar [note: 'Physical weight of the device in grams. Relevant for logistics, shipping cost calculation, and customer information.']
  wholesale_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Wholesale acquisition cost from manufacturer or distributor. Used for margin analysis, CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) planning, and profitability calculations (EBITDA - Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization).']
  wifi_standard varchar [note: 'Supported Wi-Fi standard (e.g., Wi-Fi 5 (802.11ac), Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax), Wi-Fi 6E). Critical for CPE devices and home broadband service quality (QoS - Quality of Service).']

  Note: 'Master record for handsets, CPE (Customer Premises Equipment), ONT (Optical Network Terminal), routers, set-top boxes, and other devices offered through the product catalog. Captures device model name, manufacturer, device category (smartphone, tablet, CPE, ONT, STB, router), technology support (4G/LTE, 5G NR, Wi-Fi 6, GPON), eSIM capability, operating system, storage/RAM specs, color variants, IMEI range, and device status (available, end-of-life). SSOT for device catalog definitions.'
}

Table product.addon {
  addon_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the add-on product. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: Add-ons distributed via partner channels (MVNO bolt-ons, reseller add-ons) are governed by partner agreements defining distribution rights and pricing. This link is required for settlement line attrib']
  base_offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: An addon attaches to a base offering (e.g., a data top-up addon is available on the Unlimited Mobile offering). The prefix \'base_\' distinguishes this FK from any future offering references and clarifi']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Roaming day-pass and international calling add-ons are directly enabled by carrier agreements. Operations and product teams must know which carrier agreement governs each roaming addon for settlement,']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: An addon is itself a product catalog entry — data top-ups, international calling packs, and device insurance are all catalog items. Linking addon to catalog_item establishes the addon\'s identity in th']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.iot_tariff. Business justification: International roaming addons (travel passes, data boosters) priced using IOT tariff rates for specific destination zones. Essential for margin analysis, ensuring addon pricing covers wholesale settlem']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: An addon\'s pricing should be governed by a price plan record rather than denormalized price_amount and pricing_model columns on the addon table. price_plan has base_price_amount, pricing_model_type, b']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Add-ons (roaming packs, premium SMS, international calling) are governed by specific regulatory obligations (EU roaming regulations, premium rate service rules, opt-in mandates). Compliance officers m']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary base product offering that this add-on is compatible with. Null if the add-on is compatible with multiple base products (see compatibility rules).']
  activation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the add-on is activated on the customer subscription. Instant: immediate activation; Scheduled: activation at specified date/time; Manual approval: requires CSR or system approval; Provisioning required: requires network provisioning workflow.']
  addon_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the add-on product type. Determines the functional category and business rules applicable to the add-on. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_topup|voice_pack|sms_pack|international_calling|roaming|premium_content|cloud_storage|security|static_ip|device_insurance — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  addon_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the add-on product used in product catalog and billing systems. Examples: DATA_5GB, INTL_CALL_100, PREMIUM_SPORTS.']
  addon_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the add-on product, including features, benefits, and usage terms. Used for customer-facing documentation and sales materials.']
  addon_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the add-on product displayed to customers and in product catalogs.']
  addon_type varchar [note: 'Billing model type for the add-on. One-time: single charge; Recurring: periodic subscription; Usage-based: pay-per-use; Hybrid: combination of recurring and usage.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the add-on automatically renews at the end of its validity period. True: auto-renews; False: requires manual renewal or expires.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the add-on charge is billed to the customer. Applicable for recurring and usage-based add-ons. One-time add-ons are billed once at activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: one_time|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annually|usage_triggered — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sales and service channels through which the add-on can be purchased or activated. Examples: web, mobile_app, retail_store, call_center, partner_portal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|retail_store|call_center|partner_portal|self_service|agent_assisted|third_party — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the add-on product record was first created in the product catalog system. Immutable audit field.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price amount. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer segment for the add-on product. Defines eligibility based on customer classification. Examples: consumer, small_business, enterprise, government, wholesale. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: consumer|small_business|mid_market|enterprise|government|wholesale|MVNO|partner — promote to reference product]']
  deactivation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the add-on is deactivated or removed from the customer subscription. Instant: immediate removal; End of period: deactivation at billing cycle end; Manual: requires CSR intervention; Grace period: deactivation after notice period.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the add-on is no longer available for new sales. Existing subscriptions may continue beyond this date based on contract terms. Null indicates no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the add-on becomes available for sale and activation in the product catalog. Add-ons cannot be sold before this date.']
  external_product_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the add-on product in external systems such as partner catalogs, wholesale platforms, or third-party marketplaces. Used for system integration and cross-reference.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or countries where the add-on is available for sale and use. Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region identifiers. Examples: USA, CAN, MEX for North America; GBR, FRA, DEU for Europe.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for revenue posting in the financial system. Used for financial reporting and reconciliation of add-on revenue.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last updated the add-on product record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the add-on product record was last updated in the product catalog system. Updated with each modification.']
  max_quantity_per_subscription varchar [note: 'Maximum number of instances of this add-on that can be attached to a single customer subscription. Null indicates no limit. Example: customer can purchase up to 5 data top-up add-ons per month.']
  min_quantity_per_subscription varchar [note: 'Minimum number of instances of this add-on required when attached to a subscription. Typically 1 or null. Used for bundled add-ons with minimum purchase requirements.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High-level product family or portfolio to which the add-on belongs. Examples: Mobile Services, Broadband Services, Enterprise Solutions, Digital Content, IoT Services. Used for product hierarchy and reporting.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the promotional campaign associated with this add-on. Null if not promotional. Used for tracking campaign performance and eligibility validation.']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the add-on is part of a promotional campaign with special pricing or terms. True: promotional offer; False: standard catalog offering.']
  proration_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the add-on charge is prorated when activated or deactivated mid-billing cycle. True: charge is prorated; False: full charge applies regardless of activation date.']
  quota_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quota value. Defines what the quota_value represents. Examples: GB for data, minutes for voice, SMS for messaging, channels for IPTV. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GB|MB|minutes|SMS|channels|users|GB_storage|static_IP|incidents — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quota_value decimal(15,4) [note: 'Numeric value representing the allowance or quota provided by the add-on. Examples: 5 GB data, 100 international minutes, 500 SMS. Used in conjunction with quota_unit.']
  revenue_recognition_rule varchar [note: 'Accounting rule for revenue recognition of the add-on charges. Immediate: recognize at sale; Deferred: recognize over service period; Prorated: recognize proportionally; Usage-based: recognize as consumed.']
  rollover_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused quota from the current period can roll over to the next period. True: unused quota carries forward; False: quota expires at period end.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Quality of Service (QoS) tier or Service Level Agreement (SLA) class associated with the add-on. Determines priority, support level, and performance guarantees.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the add-on product for display in mobile apps, web portals, and point-of-sale systems. Typically 1-2 sentences.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that manages this add-on product. Examples: Netcracker Product Catalog, Amdocs CRM, Oracle Product Hub. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used by billing and revenue systems to determine applicable taxes, fees, and regulatory charges. Examples: TELECOM_SERVICE, DIGITAL_CONTENT, EQUIPMENT_SALE.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Network technology or service delivery platform required for the add-on. Defines technical compatibility constraints. Examples: 5G NR for 5G data add-ons, FTTH for fiber broadband add-ons, VoLTE for voice add-ons. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 4G_LTE|5G_NR|FTTH|GPON|DSL|cable|satellite|fixed_wireless|VoLTE|VoIP — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trial_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days for which the add-on is offered as a free trial before billing begins. Null if no trial period is available. Common values: 7, 14, 30 days.']
  validity_period_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of time for the validity period. Examples: 30 days, 1 month, 1 year. Perpetual indicates no expiration.']
  validity_period_value varchar [note: 'Numeric value representing the duration for which the add-on remains active after activation. Used in conjunction with validity_period_unit. Null for perpetual add-ons.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the add-on product specification. Incremented with each significant change to pricing, features, or terms. Used for change management and historical tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created the add-on product record in the product catalog. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Defines optional add-on products that can be attached to a base offering — including data top-ups, international calling packs, premium channel subscriptions, cloud storage upgrades, static IP add-ons, and security packages. Captures add-on code, add-on category, compatible base offerings, pricing model, maximum quantity per subscription, auto-renewal flag, and validity period. Supports both one-time and recurring add-ons across mobile, broadband, and enterprise product lines.'
}

Table product.bundle_component {
  bundle_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the bundle_component association']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parent bundle that contains this component offering.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the offering included as a component in the bundle.']
  component_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to this specific offering when included in this bundle, distinct from the bundle-level discount. Enables per-component pricing adjustments in bundle pricing models.']
  component_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of this offering included in the bundle. Typically 1 for most components but may be greater for multi-line or multi-device bundles.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this offering ceases to be a component of this bundle. Nullable for open-ended inclusions. Enables graceful removal of components from bundles without deleting historical composition records.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this offering became an active component of this bundle. Supports versioned bundle composition and time-bounded component inclusion for product lifecycle management.']
  is_mandatory_component boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offering is a required (mandatory) component of the bundle or an optional component that customers may substitute or remove. Directly governs flexible bundle composition rules.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Display and processing order of this offering component within the bundle. Used for rendering bundle composition in customer-facing channels and BSS order management.']
  substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'Component-level override indicating whether this specific offering within the bundle may be substituted by the customer with an alternative offering. Overrides or refines the bundle-level component_substitution_allowed flag.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Component role between bundle and offering in the telecommunications product catalog. It captures the explicit business act of including a specific offering as a component within a bundle, with terms governing that inclusion (mandatory vs. optional, quantity, component-level discount, display sequence, and effective dates). Each record links one bundle to one offering and carries attributes that exist only in the context of that specific bundle-offering composition. Product catalog managers create, modify, and retire these records as part of bundle lifecycle management in BSS/product catalog systems such as Netcracker.. Existence Justification: In telecommunications product catalog management, bundles are explicitly composed of multiple offerings (e.g., a Triple Play bundle contains broadband offering + IPTV offering + VoIP offering), and a single offering (e.g., \'Fiber 100Mbps\') can be included in multiple different bundles simultaneously (e.g., both a \'Double Play\' and a \'Triple Play\' bundle). This is a first-class operational business concept — product catalog managers actively create, modify, and retire bundle component records as part of product lifecycle management. The relationship carries its own attributes (mandatory/optional flag, component quantity, component-level discount, sequence, effective dates) that belong neither to the bundle nor the offering alone.'
}

Table order.fulfillment_order {
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for every end-to-end fulfillment order record in the telecommunications order management system. Primary key of this product; globally unique across all order types (new activation, upgrade, downgrade, disconnection, MNP port-in/port-out, change-of-service).']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical installation address for orders requiring field work (FTTH, GPON, CPE installation, fixed-line provisioning). Links to the address/location master in the Network or Customer domain. Null for mobile-only orders with no physical installation.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Regulatory and high-value orders (is_regulatory_order=true) are subject to compliance audits. Linking fulfillment_order to the audit record enables auditors to directly reference the order under revie']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle order fulfillment decomposition: when a Triple Play or converged bundle is ordered, the fulfillment order references the bundle to drive component decomposition into child order lines. Telecom ']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: B2B order processing requires corporate account linkage for contract validation, billing consolidation, account-level SLA tracking, and enterprise customer lifecycle management. Core business relation']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.customer_account.customer_account_id — Every order must join to the customer who placed it. This is the most critical cross-domain join for order management, billing reconciliation, and customer service queries.']
  dealer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.dealer. Business justification: Dealer-originated orders require direct dealer attribution for commission calculation, quota tracking, and dealer performance measurement - standard telecommunications indirect channel sales process.']
  device_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_model. Business justification: Device order fulfillment: when a handset or CPE device is ordered, the fulfillment order must reference the specific device model for logistics, inventory allocation, and device provisioning workflows']
  discount_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.discount_scheme. Business justification: The enterprise discount scheme applied at order creation governs pricing across all order lines and charges. Order-level discount scheme reference enables revenue assurance, discount scheme utilizatio']
  donor_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier. Business justification: MNP donor carrier identification: fulfillment_order for port-in must reference the donor carrier for inter-operator coordination, settlement, and regulatory reporting. Role prefix \'donor_\' distinguish']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Orders execute against master service agreements. Required for contract compliance validation, pricing verification, commitment tracking, penalty calculation, and regulatory reporting for government/e']
  iot_deployment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.iot_deployment. Business justification: Fulfillment orders for IoT deployment activation, expansion, or decommissioning must reference the IoT deployment at order header level for deployment-level order tracking and project management repor']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Fulfillment orders are raised to provision, modify, or decommission managed services. Direct link enables managed service lifecycle order history, change management tracking, and managed service deliv']
  mnp_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.mnp_compliance.   network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Orders for broadband/fiber services require assignment to specific network elements (DSLAM, OLT, BRAS) during fulfillment. Critical for network resource allocation, capacity management, and provisioni']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Orders must reference the marketed offering (not just technical product_spec) for campaign attribution, sales compensation, channel performance tracking, and marketing ROI analysis. Business-facing pr']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Orders placed through partner channels (MVNOs, dealers, retail partners) require partner attribution for commission calculation, revenue sharing settlement, and partner performance tracking - core tel']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Order-to-cash traceability: a fulfillment order is generated from an accepted quote. Telecom revenue assurance and billing teams require the fulfillment order to reference its originating quote to val']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Telco orders often fulfill regulatory obligations (lawful intercept provisioning, emergency service activation, accessibility mandates). Operations must track which regulation governs each order for c']
  revenue_share_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.revenue_share_plan. Business justification: Partner and dealer-originated fulfillment orders are governed by a specific revenue share plan determining commission rates and settlement terms. Telecom operators link orders directly to the applicab']
  sdwan_topology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.sdwan_topology. Business justification: Fulfillment orders for SD-WAN topology provisioning, site additions, or topology changes must reference the SD-WAN topology. Enables topology-level order history and multi-site rollout project trackin']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.segment. Business justification: Enterprise segment drives order processing rules, credit check requirements, SLA tier assignment, and discount eligibility at order creation. Direct link enables segment-level order volume and revenue']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.site. Business justification: Enterprise orders target specific customer sites for service installation and provisioning. Essential for field dispatch coordination, site-level SLA management, and multi-location enterprise service ']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.sla_contract. Business justification: Every fulfillment order is governed by an assurance SLA contract defining measurement rules, penalty clauses, and breach thresholds. Telecom SLA breach reporting and regulatory compliance require trac']
  slice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: 5G network slice orders (enterprise private slices, IoT dedicated slices) are tied to a specific network slice being provisioned or modified. Order management must reference the slice to orchestrate i']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or service catalog item being ordered, upgraded, downgraded, or disconnected. Links to the product catalog master in the Product domain. Sourced from Netcracker OSS/BSS Product Catalog.']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Enterprise leased-line and fixed broadband fulfillment orders are directly associated with the transmission link being activated or upgraded. Order management and provisioning orchestration require th']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fulfillment order was actually completed and all downstream provisioning tasks confirmed. Used to calculate actual fulfillment duration, SLA adherence, and MTTR reporting. Null until order reaches COMPLETED status. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fulfillment order was cancelled. Null for non-cancelled orders. Used for cancellation rate analytics, churn analysis, and order fallout reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fulfillment order record was first created in the order management system. Serves as the audit trail anchor for record lineage. Distinct from submitted_timestamp which captures the business event time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credit_check_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of the credit assessment performed during order processing. APPROVED: customer passed credit check. DECLINED: order blocked due to credit failure. WAIVED: credit check bypassed (e.g., existing premium customer). Drives order workflow progression in Oracle Communications OSM.']
  decomposition_strategy varchar [note: 'The strategy used by Oracle Communications OSM to decompose the fulfillment order into sub-orders and tasks for downstream OSS/BSS systems. Drives orchestration efficiency and parallel provisioning capabilities.']
  external_order_reference varchar [note: 'The order reference number from an external or upstream system (e.g., Salesforce CRM opportunity ID, dealer portal order number, B2B customer\'s purchase order number). Enables cross-system order tracing and reconciliation.']
  fallout_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the order fallout reason, enabling structured reporting and trend analysis on order failure types. Complements fallout_reason with a standardized taxonomy for operational dashboards.']
  fallout_reason varchar [note: 'Descriptive reason code or message explaining why the fulfillment order failed or was placed on hold during orchestration. Populated by Oracle Communications OSM when order_status transitions to FAILED or ON_HOLD. Critical for fallout management, root cause analysis, and process improvement.']
  field_work_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order requires a field technician dispatch for physical installation or maintenance (e.g., FTTH CPE installation, ONT provisioning). Drives integration with Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management for scheduling and dispatch.']
  is_regulatory_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fulfillment order is subject to specific regulatory mandates (e.g., FCC MNP porting deadlines, emergency service provisioning, Ofcom consumer protection rules). Regulatory orders receive elevated priority and mandatory compliance reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fulfillment order record was last updated in the order management system. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mnp_recipient_operator_code varchar [note: 'For MNP_PORT_OUT orders: the network operator code of the recipient (gaining) operator to whom the mobile number is being ported. Used for inter-operator coordination and MNP regulatory reporting. Null for non-MNP order types.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions associated with the fulfillment order, entered by agents, customers, or automated systems. May include customer preferences, access instructions for field technicians, or escalation context. Not used for structured data.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'The sales or service channel through which the fulfillment order was submitted. Used for channel performance analytics, cost-to-serve analysis, and omnichannel reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ONLINE|RETAIL_STORE|CALL_CENTER|DEALER|SELF_SERVICE|B2B_PORTAL|FIELD_SALES — promote to reference product]']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the fulfillment order. Communicated to customers, field technicians, and partner systems. Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) as the canonical order reference number.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the fulfillment order, influencing queue position in orchestration workflows and resource allocation. CRITICAL is reserved for regulatory-mandated orders (e.g., emergency services, MNP regulatory deadlines).']
  order_source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system or application from which the fulfillment order was submitted. Used for channel attribution, integration troubleshooting, and data lineage tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OSM|SALESFORCE_CRM|SELF_SERVICE_PORTAL|B2B_PORTAL|DEALER_PORTAL|IVR|RETAIL_POS — promote to reference product]']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fulfillment order within the OSS/BSS orchestration workflow. Drives jeopardy management, SLA monitoring, and customer communication triggers. Sourced from Oracle Communications OSM order state machine.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment order by the nature of the service change being requested. Drives orchestration workflow selection, SLA type assignment, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NEW_ACTIVATION|UPGRADE|DOWNGRADE|DISCONNECTION|MNP_PORT_IN|MNP_PORT_OUT|CHANGE_OF_SERVICE — promote to reference product]']
  porting_authorization_code varchar [note: 'The Porting Authorization Code provided by the donor operator to authorize the number port. Required for MNP_PORT_IN orders to validate the customer\'s right to port. Null for non-MNP order types.']
  porting_number varchar [note: 'The MSISDN (mobile phone number) being ported in MNP_PORT_IN or MNP_PORT_OUT orders. Classified as confidential PII as it directly identifies a subscriber. Null for non-MNP order types. Required for NRTRDE and TAP regulatory reporting.']
  promised_completion_date date [note: 'The date committed to the customer for order fulfillment completion. Used for SLA breach detection, customer communication, and regulatory compliance reporting. Distinct from SLA target hours — this is the customer-facing commitment date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  regulatory_reference_code varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory rule, mandate, or case reference code applicable to this fulfillment order when is_regulatory_order is True. Examples: FCC MNP case number, Ofcom compliance reference. Used for regulatory audit trails and compliance reporting.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The type of telecommunications service being provisioned or modified by this fulfillment order. Drives workflow template selection, resource inventory allocation, and regulatory reporting categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER|IPTV|VOIP|FIXED_LINE|IOT|ENTERPRISE_WAN — promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of hours elapsed from SLA measurement start at which the SLA is formally classified as BREACHED. Typically equals sla_target_hours but may differ for grace-period configurations. Triggers regulatory reporting obligations and penalty calculations.']
  sla_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fulfillment order SLA was formally breached. Null for orders that have not breached. Used for regulatory reporting, penalty calculation, and SLA performance analytics. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  sla_measurement_end_event varchar [note: 'The business event that stops the SLA measurement clock for this fulfillment order. Defines what constitutes successful completion for SLA purposes — e.g., SERVICE_ACTIVATED for mobile activations, CPE_INSTALLED for FTTH orders.']
  sla_measurement_start_event varchar [note: 'The business event that triggers the start of SLA measurement clock for this fulfillment order. Varies by order type and SLA type — regulatory MNP orders typically start at ORDER_ACCEPTED, while commercial orders may start at PAYMENT_CONFIRMED.']
  sla_measurement_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the SLA measurement clock started for this fulfillment order, corresponding to the sla_measurement_start_event. Used as the reference point for all SLA elapsed-time calculations and regulatory compliance measurement. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current SLA compliance status of the fulfillment order based on elapsed time versus SLA thresholds. ON_TRACK: within warning threshold. AT_RISK: between warning and breach thresholds. BREACHED: exceeded breach threshold. Primary field for jeopardy detection and regulatory compliance dashboards.']
  sla_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum number of hours within which the fulfillment order must be completed to meet the SLA commitment. Measured from the SLA measurement start event. Used for jeopardy detection and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA commitment governing this fulfillment order. REGULATORY: mandated by FCC/Ofcom/BEREC regulations (e.g., MNP porting windows). COMMERCIAL: standard commercial SLA per product tier. ENTERPRISE: premium SLA for enterprise/B2B customers.']
  sla_warning_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of hours elapsed from SLA measurement start at which an AT_RISK warning is triggered for proactive jeopardy management. Must be less than sla_target_hours. Drives automated alerting in NOC and order management dashboards.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fulfillment order was submitted by the customer or agent into the order management system. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor point for SLA measurement start calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  workflow_template_name varchar [note: 'The name of the OSM workflow template (order specification) used to orchestrate this fulfillment order. Determines the decomposition strategy, task sequence, and system interactions. Examples: \'MOBILE_NEW_ACTIVATION_V3\', \'FTTH_PROVISIONING_V2\'.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every end-to-end order in the telecommunications lifecycle — new activations, upgrades, downgrades, disconnections, MNP port-in/port-out, and change-of-service requests. Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM). Captures order type, order channel, priority, customer reference, account reference, submitted timestamp, promised completion date, current order state, fallout reason, orchestration workflow instance ID, workflow template, decomposition strategy, SLA type (regulatory, commercial, enterprise), SLA target completion hours, warning threshold hours, breach threshold hours, SLA measurement start/end events, current SLA status (ON_TRACK, AT_RISK, BREACHED), and breach timestamp. This is the SSOT for order identity, lifecycle state, orchestration context, and SLA commitment within the order domain. All SLA monitoring, jeopardy detection, and regulatory compliance measurement derives from this master record\'s SLA attributes.'
}

Table order.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order line item within the fulfillment order management system. Primary key for the order_line entity. Role: TRANSACTION_LINE.']
  addon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.addon. Business justification: Addon products ordered as separate lines need direct catalog linkage for provisioning orchestration, billing setup, and quota management. Critical for value-added services fulfillment.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical service delivery address for fixed-line, broadband, or FTTH order lines. Used for field dispatch scheduling in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management and network infrastructure provisioning.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Split billing: in telecom, individual lines (e.g., corporate-liable vs. individual-liable) can be billed to different billing accounts than the parent order. Line-level billing account assignment is r']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle orders require direct catalog reference for component validation, bundle pricing enforcement, discount calculation, and eligibility verification. Critical for multi-product offer fulfillment.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Line-level corporate account tracking enables product-level revenue attribution per enterprise customer, supports multi-site service deployments, and facilitates usage analysis for account management ']
  device_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_model. Business justification: Device line item fulfillment: order lines for handset upgrades or CPE installations must reference the specific device model for IMEI assignment, device inventory allocation, and device-specific provi']
  donor_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier. Business justification: MNP line-level donor carrier tracking: each MNP order line references the donor carrier for per-number porting coordination, settlement reconciliation, and regulatory per-MSISDN audit. Role prefix \'do']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Line-level contract linkage enables contract utilization tracking, commitment burn-down analysis, volume discount tier validation, and supports complex enterprise pricing schedules with product-specif']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent fulfillment order header. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE HEADER_REFERENCE category. Links this line item to its containing order transaction in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  inventory_cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Order lines provision CPE devices (modems, routers, ONTs) to customers. Real business need: installation tracking, warranty claims, device lifecycle management, asset return processing. Telecommunicat']
  iot_deployment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.iot_deployment. Business justification: Order lines activating IoT SIMs must reference the IoT deployment they belong to for deployment-level activation tracking, SIM inventory management, and IoT deployment progress reporting. Telecom IoT ']
  mnp_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.mnp_compliance. Business justification: MNP order lines (is_mnp_line=true, mnp_reference_number, msisdn) must be traceable to their mnp_compliance record for per-number porting SLA compliance verification and regulatory audit — each ported ']
  msisdn_range_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: Order lines for mobile services assign a specific MSISDN drawn from a number range. Direct reference enables number inventory management, range utilization tracking, and regulatory number allocation r']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Order line to product offering traceability: each order line represents a specific product offering being activated. Direct FK enables offering-level order volume reporting, product performance analyt']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Fiber service order lines reference the ONT asset being installed or activated. Direct link enables fiber asset-to-order traceability, ONT assignment tracking, and asset lifecycle management. Parallel']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: Order lines must reference the specific price plan being ordered for accurate rating, billing, and revenue recognition. Critical for order-to-cash process and finance reconciliation in telecom operati']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.promo_offer. Business justification: Promotional offer application per order line: each line can have a specific promo applied (e.g., 50% off first 3 months). Direct FK enables promo redemption tracking, budget consumption monitoring, an']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.promotion. Business justification: Order lines carry applied promotions for discount validation and promotion redemption tracking. The plain promotion_code attribute on order.line denormalizes sales.promotion data. Replacing with a pro']
  qos_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_qos_profile. Business justification: Order lines for QoS-differentiated services (enterprise VPN, 5G slice, priority broadband) reference the specific QoS profile to be provisioned. Line-level QoS profile assignment drives provisioning p']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Quote-to-order line traceability: each order line is created from a specific quote line. Telecom commission calculation, revenue assurance, and price variance reporting require matching the delivered ']
  rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing rate plan in Amdocs Revenue Management that governs the recurring and usage-based charges for the product on this order line. Established at order capture and used to configure the billing account upon fulfillment.']
  sdwan_topology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.sdwan_topology. Business justification: Order lines provisioning SD-WAN CPE devices or circuits must reference the SD-WAN topology they are part of. Enables topology-level provisioning progress tracking and site activation sequencing — expe']
  sim_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sim_stock. Business justification: Order lines activate specific SIM cards for mobile service. Real business need: SIM allocation tracking, activation audit trail, MNP porting coordination, inventory consumption. Existing iccid/imsi ar']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.site. Business justification: Line items provision services at specific enterprise sites (circuits, managed services, UC seats). Required for site-level service inventory management, capacity planning, and multi-site enterprise de']
  slice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: Order lines for 5G slice services reference the specific network slice being ordered. Enterprise customers ordering dedicated slices require line-level slice association for provisioning decomposition']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commercial product or service offering in the product catalog that this order line is acting upon (e.g., a specific mobile plan, broadband tier, or add-on). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE RESOURCE_REFERENCE category. Sourced from Netcracker OSS/BSS Product Catalog.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Each order line activates, modifies, or terminates a specific subscriber\'s service. Telecom order management requires subscriber-level linkage to drive SIM activation, MSISDN assignment, and provision']
  svc_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.svc_configuration. Business justification: Each order line specifies the service configuration to be applied (bandwidth profile, APN, VoLTE settings). Linking line to svc_configuration enables product configuration traceability per order line,']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the provisioned service instance created or modified as a result of fulfilling this order line. Populated post-fulfillment by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management upon successful service activation. Links the commercial order line to the operational service layer.']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Individual order lines for fixed broadband or leased-line services reference the specific transmission link being activated. Line-level transmission link association is needed for provisioning decompo']
  uc_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.uc_subscription. Business justification: Order lines provisioning UC seats (DID numbers, SIP trunks, feature packs) must reference the UC subscription being fulfilled. Enables UC subscription provisioning status tracking and seat count recon']
  action_code varchar [note: 'The fulfillment action to be performed for this line item. Drives order decomposition and workflow routing in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. ADD = new activation; REMOVE = disconnection; MODIFY = change of service; SUSPEND = temporary suspension; RESUME = reactivation; PORT_IN = Mobile Number Portability (MNP) inbound; PORT_OUT = MNP outbound. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ADD|REMOVE|MODIFY|SUSPEND|RESUME|PORT_IN|PORT_OUT — promote to reference product]']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date on which fulfillment of this order line was completed and the service was activated, modified, or disconnected. Compared against committed_delivery_date for SLA performance measurement and MTTR (Mean Time to Repair) analytics.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'The sales or service channel through which this order line was initiated (e.g., ONLINE web portal, RETAIL_STORE, CALL_CENTER, DEALER partner, SELF_SERVICE app, B2B_PORTAL). Used for channel performance analytics, commission calculation, and order routing.']
  committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The date committed to the customer for delivery or activation of the service on this line, as agreed at order capture. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance monitoring and breach detection in service fulfillment reporting.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'The minimum contract commitment period in months associated with the product on this order line (e.g., 0 for month-to-month, 12, 24, 36). Drives early termination fee (ETF) calculation and churn risk assessment in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  cpe_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the CPE (Customer Premises Equipment) device — such as a router, ONT (Optical Network Terminal), or set-top box — assigned or dispatched as part of this order line. Used for inventory tracking in Netcracker OSS/BSS and field installation management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this order line record was first created in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Used for order aging analysis, SLA measurement baseline, and audit trail compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the monetary values on this order line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency enterprise and wholesale order scenarios.']
  decomposition_state varchar [note: 'Indicates the order decomposition processing state for this line within Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Order decomposition translates a commercial product order line into technical service and resource fulfillment tasks across OSS systems. Critical for orchestration monitoring and fault management.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this order line at the time of order capture, reflecting promotional offers, loyalty discounts, or negotiated enterprise pricing. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for billing and revenue assurance reconciliation.']
  early_termination_fee decimal(18,4) [note: 'The early termination fee amount applicable if the customer cancels this order line before the end of the contract_term_months. Calculated at order capture based on the rate plan and contract term. Used by Amdocs Revenue Management for billing upon disconnection.']
  fallout_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the order line fallout or failure, supplementing the fallout_reason_code. Captured by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management workflow engine for manual intervention and customer communication.']
  fallout_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for order line fallout or failure during fulfillment orchestration (e.g., \'CREDIT_FAIL\', \'INVENTORY_UNAVAILABLE\', \'ADDRESS_NOT_SERVICEABLE\', \'PORTING_REJECTED\'). Used for fallout management, root cause analysis, and process improvement in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  iccid varchar [note: 'The unique SIM card identifier (ICCID) assigned to this order line for mobile service activations. Used for SIM provisioning in HLR/HSS/UDM systems and inventory management in Netcracker OSS/BSS. Classified as confidential as it identifies a specific subscriber SIM.']
  imsi varchar [note: 'The International Mobile Subscriber Identity assigned to the SIM for this order line. Used for subscriber provisioning in HSS (Home Subscriber Server) and UDM (Unified Data Management) systems. Restricted PII as it uniquely identifies a mobile subscriber globally.']
  is_esim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order line involves an eSIM (Embedded SIM) provisioning rather than a physical SIM card. When TRUE, triggers eSIM profile download orchestration via GSMA SGP.22 RSP (Remote SIM Provisioning) instead of physical SIM dispatch.']
  is_mnp_line boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order line involves a Mobile Number Portability (MNP) port-in or port-out transaction. When TRUE, triggers MNP-specific workflow orchestration including donor operator notification and number portability clearinghouse coordination.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this order line record was last updated in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trail, and concurrency control.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this line item within the parent order. Used for ordering, display, and referencing individual lines in multi-line orders (e.g., line 1 = mobile plan, line 2 = broadband, line 3 = MNP port-in). Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment lifecycle state of this individual order line. Tracks granular progress independently from the parent order header status, enabling partial fulfillment scenarios where some lines complete while others are held. Sourced from Oracle Communications Order and Service Management workflow engine.']
  mnp_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the number portability clearinghouse or regulatory body for a PORT_IN or PORT_OUT order line. Used for inter-operator tracking, regulatory compliance reporting to FCC/Ofcom/BEREC, and dispute resolution.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net commercial price for this order line after applying discounts (unit_price × quantity − discount_amount). Represents the agreed charge basis passed to Amdocs Revenue Management for billing. Distinct from tax-inclusive total.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Processing priority assigned to this order line for fulfillment queue management. HIGH and CRITICAL priorities are used for enterprise SLA orders, regulatory-mandated activations, and emergency service restorations. Drives scheduling in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the product or service being ordered on this line (e.g., \'5G Unlimited Plan\', \'FTTH 1Gbps Broadband\', \'VoLTE Add-On\'). Denormalized from the product catalog for operational reporting and customer-facing order confirmations.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the product or service being ordered on this line. Drives workflow routing, provisioning system selection, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIXED_LINE|IPTV|VOIP|IOT|ENTERPRISE|ADD_ON — promote to reference product]']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product or service being ordered on this line. Typically 1 for most telecom service lines but may be greater for bulk SIM orders, enterprise seat licenses, or CPE equipment orders. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_QUANTITY category.']
  requested_start_date date [note: 'The customer-requested date on which the product or service on this line should become active. Drives service provisioning scheduling in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management and SLA commitment tracking.']
  requires_field_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fulfillment of this order line requires a field technician visit (e.g., FTTH installation, CPE installation, ONT splicing). When TRUE, triggers work order creation and field dispatch scheduling in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Billing and operational service type for this order line, distinguishing postpaid subscription, prepaid (Comverse ONE), hybrid, MVNO (Mobile Virtual Network Operator) resale, or wholesale service arrangements. Determines downstream billing system routing.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this order line record in the originating operational system (Oracle Communications Order and Service Management). Enables lineage tracing, reconciliation, and idempotent upsert processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Network access technology associated with the product on this order line (e.g., LTE, 5G NR, FTTH/GPON, DSL). Used for network provisioning routing to Ericsson Network Manager or Nokia NetAct, capacity planning, and technology migration analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 2G|3G|4G_LTE|5G_NR|FTTH_GPON|FTTB|DSL|CABLE|FIXED_WIRELESS — promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Commercial price per unit for the product or service on this line at the time of order placement, before discounts and taxes. Represents the catalog or negotiated price used for billing rate plan association in Amdocs Revenue Management. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a fulfillment order representing a single product or service action (add, remove, modify, suspend, resume). Each line carries the commercial product reference, requested action code, quantity, requested service start date, line-level status, and decomposition state. Supports multi-line orders where a customer simultaneously activates a mobile plan, adds a broadband service, and ports a number in a single order transaction.'
}

Table order.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order status history record. Primary key of the order_status_history data product. Immutable once assigned.']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.amendment. Business justification: The status_history table explicitly tracks post-submission amendments — it has amendment_type, amendment_status, amended_field_name, amendment_request_timestamp, cost_delta, original_value, and new_va']
  appointment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field service appointment associated with this order history record, particularly relevant for APPOINTMENT_CHANGE amendments or transitions triggered by appointment outcomes. Links to Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management scheduling records.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with the fulfillment order. Enables customer-level SLA tracking, dispute resolution, and churn analysis across the order lifecycle.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order for which this status history record was created. Links every history entry back to its originating order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. Business justification: Order status transitions caused by NOC incidents (e.g., order suspended due to network outage) must reference the incident for SLA suspension justification and regulatory audit trails. status_history ']
  provisioning_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the decomposed service order generated from the parent fulfillment order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management or Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite. Supports traceability between commercial order history and service fulfillment execution.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: status_history.is_regulatory_reportable flags transitions that must be reported under a specific regulatory obligation (e.g., MNP porting status changes within mandated windows). Linking to the govern']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a ServiceNow ITSM trouble ticket or incident raised in association with this state transition or amendment (e.g., a FALLOUT event that triggered an incident). Null when no ticket is associated. Supports cross-system traceability for fault management.']
  workflow_task_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific workflow task or orchestration step within Oracle Communications Order and Service Management that generated this history record. Enables traceability from the history record back to the originating workflow execution for technical root-cause analysis.']
  actor_system varchar [note: 'Name of the OSS/BSS system that originated the transition or amendment event (e.g., Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite, Salesforce CRM, ServiceNow ITSM). Enables source-system traceability for fallout root-cause analysis.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the type of actor that triggered the state transition or amendment. SYSTEM indicates an automated OSS/BSS process; AGENT indicates a human CSR or field technician; CUSTOMER indicates a self-service action; SUPERVISOR indicates a management override; AUTOMATED_RULE indicates a policy engine or workflow rule.']
  amended_field_name varchar [note: 'For amendment records, the logical name of the order attribute that was changed (e.g., installation_address, appointment_date, selected_product_id, service_configuration_value). Provides context for interpreting original_value and new_value. Null for state transition records.']
  amendment_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the amendment was formally requested by the customer or agent. Distinct from transition_timestamp (which records when the event was processed). Used to measure amendment processing lead time and SLA compliance. Null for state transition records. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the amendment request. PENDING: awaiting review; APPROVED: authorized but not yet applied to the order; REJECTED: denied by agent or system rule; APPLIED: successfully incorporated into the active order. Null for state transition records.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'For amendment records, classifies the type of post-submission change requested. Null for state transition records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: APPOINTMENT_CHANGE|PRODUCT_SELECTION_CHANGE|ADDRESS_CHANGE|SERVICE_CONFIGURATION_CHANGE|CONTACT_CHANGE|PAYMENT_METHOD_CHANGE — promote to reference product]']
  cost_delta decimal(18,4) [note: 'For amendment records, the incremental cost impact (positive or negative, in the account currency) resulting from the amendment — e.g., a product selection change that increases or decreases the order value. Null for state transition records and zero-cost amendments. Used for revenue assurance and billing reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this history record was first written to the Silver Layer lakehouse. Distinct from transition_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data pipeline monitoring, late-arrival detection, and audit trail completeness verification. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the cost_delta amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null when cost_delta is null. Supports multi-currency operations across international markets.']
  duration_in_state_seconds varchar [note: 'Number of seconds the order spent in the previous_state before transitioning to new_state. Calculated at ingestion time as the difference between this record\'s transition_timestamp and the prior record\'s transition_timestamp for the same order. Used for SLA bottleneck analysis and process mining.']
  fallout_category varchar [note: 'When new_state is FALLOUT, classifies the root-cause category of the fallout event (e.g., PROVISIONING_FAILURE, CREDIT_REJECTION, ADDRESS_VALIDATION_FAILURE, MNP_REJECTION, NETWORK_CAPACITY, SYSTEM_ERROR). Null for non-FALLOUT transitions. Essential for fallout root-cause analysis and NOC/OSS remediation workflows.']
  fallout_resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating how a FALLOUT condition was resolved (e.g., AUTO_RETRY_SUCCESS, MANUAL_INTERVENTION, ORDER_CANCELLED, REROUTED_TO_ALTERNATE_RESOURCE). Populated when transitioning out of FALLOUT state. Null for non-FALLOUT records.']
  is_regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this history record is subject to regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., FCC order completion reporting, BEREC quality-of-service metrics, OFCOM service provisioning KPIs). True flags records that must be included in regulatory submissions.']
  mnp_reference_number varchar [note: 'For MNP port-in or port-out order types, the inter-operator MNP reference number assigned by the number portability clearinghouse. Null for non-MNP orders. Required for GSMA MNP compliance reporting and inter-carrier dispute resolution.']
  new_state varchar [note: 'The order lifecycle state entered as a result of this transition. For amendment records, reflects the order state at the time the amendment was recorded. Valid states include SUBMITTED, VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, FALLOUT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUBMITTED|VALIDATED|IN_PROGRESS|PENDING_PROVISIONING|COMPLETED|CANCELLED|FALLOUT — promote to reference product]']
  new_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For amendment records, the proposed or applied new value for the amended field (serialized as string for flexibility across amendment types). Null for state transition records. Paired with original_value to provide a complete before/after audit record.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'For amendment records, the value of the amended field before the change was applied (serialized as string for flexibility across amendment types). Null for state transition records. Supports dispute resolution and regulatory audit trails.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a standard business rule, credit policy, or SLA policy was overridden to process this transition or amendment. When True, override_justification must be populated. Supports compliance monitoring and exception reporting.']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by a supervisor or authorized agent when a standard business rule or SLA policy is overridden to approve an amendment or force a state transition. Required for compliance audit trails when override_flag is True.']
  previous_state varchar [note: 'The order lifecycle state immediately before this transition occurred. Null for the initial SUBMITTED state entry. Valid states include SUBMITTED, VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, FALLOUT. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUBMITTED|VALIDATED|IN_PROGRESS|PENDING_PROVISIONING|COMPLETED|CANCELLED|FALLOUT — promote to reference product]']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the state transition or amendment (e.g., CREDIT_CHECK_FAILED, PROVISIONING_TIMEOUT, CUSTOMER_REQUESTED_CANCEL, MNP_PORT_DELAY, NETWORK_CAPACITY_UNAVAILABLE). Used for fallout root-cause analysis and SLA reporting.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context for the state transition or amendment reason, supplementing the structured reason_code. May include agent notes, system error messages, or customer-provided explanations captured at the time of the event.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this history record captures a state transition (order lifecycle state change) or a post-submission amendment (customer or agent-initiated change to order attributes). Drives downstream processing logic and reporting segmentation.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing integer representing the chronological order of history records for a given order_id. Sequence 1 is the initial SUBMITTED state entry. Enables ordered reconstruction of the complete order lifecycle and detection of out-of-sequence processing anomalies.']
  sla_reset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this state transition or amendment triggered a reset of the order fulfillment SLA clock. True when the SLA measurement window is restarted (e.g., due to a customer-requested amendment or a FALLOUT recovery). Critical for accurate SLA compliance reporting to FCC and BEREC.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The committed SLA completion deadline applicable at the time of this history record. If sla_reset_flag is True, this reflects the recalculated SLA target post-reset. Enables point-in-time SLA compliance measurement and regulatory reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or event identifier of this record in the originating source system (e.g., the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management order history event ID). Enables reverse traceability from the Silver Layer back to the operational system for reconciliation and reprocessing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this history record was sourced (e.g., Oracle Communications Order and Service Management, Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite, Salesforce CRM). Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the state transition or amendment event occurred. This is the principal business event timestamp used for SLA measurement, fallout root-cause analysis, and regulatory audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this history record was last modified in the Silver Layer (e.g., due to a late-arriving amendment status update or correction). For a fully immutable audit trail, updates should be rare and logged. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail of every state transition and post-submission amendment for a fulfillment order throughout its lifecycle — from SUBMITTED through VALIDATED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_PROVISIONING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, or FALLOUT. For state transitions: records previous state, new state, transition timestamp, triggering actor (system or user), and reason code. For amendments: records amendment type (appointment change, product selection change, address change, service configuration change), original value, new value, amendment request timestamp, amendment status (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, APPLIED), approving agent reference, impact assessment (SLA reset flag, cost delta), and override justification. This is the SSOT for all order change history — both system-driven state transitions and customer/agent-initiated amendments. Essential for SLA measurement, fallout root-cause analysis, dispute resolution, and regulatory audit trails required by FCC and BEREC.'
}

Table order.task {
  task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each decomposed order task record generated by the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) orchestration engine. Primary key for the order_task data product.']
  cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Field dispatch tasks for CPE installation, swap, or fault resolution directly reference the physical CPE asset being worked on. Technicians need direct asset lookup for field operations without traver']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: Field-work tasks are dispatched from specific depots managing territory coverage, parts inventory, and technician availability. Critical for logistics planning, resource allocation, and technician dis']
  fiber_cable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fiber_cable. Business justification: Fiber splicing, repair, and new-connection tasks reference the specific fiber cable segment being worked on. Field operations for fiber infrastructure require direct cable asset reference for dispatch']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial order header that contains the order line from which this task was decomposed. Enables direct navigation from task to top-level order for end-to-end fulfillment traceability.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial order line from which this task was decomposed. Establishes the traceability chain from commercial order line to atomic fulfillment task within the OSM orchestration engine.']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Provisioning tasks target specific managed service instances (SD-WAN, MPLS, managed security) for configuration changes, capacity modifications, or service updates. Required for service orchestration ']
  mnp_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.mnp_transaction. Business justification: MNP task execution tracking: tasks for HLR/HSS updates, routing number changes, and donor network notifications are created per mnp_transaction. Linking enables operational tracking of which tasks exe']
  network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Provisioning tasks execute configuration commands against specific network elements (configure subscriber profile on BRAS, provision VLAN on switch). Core to automated service fulfillment workflows an']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: Field tasks for network equipment maintenance, swap, or configuration (DSLAM, switch, router) reference the specific inventory asset. Equipment maintenance scheduling and asset lifecycle tracking requ']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ServiceNow ITSM incident or change request raised in response to a task fallout or execution failure. Populated when fallout_indicator is True and an ITSM ticket has been created for manual resolution. Enables cross-system traceability between order fulfillment and incident management.']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Decomposed order tasks configure ONT devices in child systems (OSS/BSS/EMS). Real business need: task-level device tracking, fallout resolution, technician dispatch coordination, configuration rollbac']
  ran_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Field and provisioning tasks for RAN cell activation, configuration, or maintenance reference the specific cell. Workforce management and OSS task orchestration for mobile network operations require c']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_site. Business justification: Field tasks (installation, maintenance, fault resolution) are performed at enterprise sites. Direct link enables site-level task scheduling, workforce dispatch optimization, and enterprise site servic']
  slice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: Orchestration tasks for 5G slice instantiation, modification, or decommissioning reference the specific slice. Order fulfillment decomposition for slice services generates tasks that must be tied to t']
  svc_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.svc_configuration. Business justification: Provisioning tasks apply specific service configurations (QoS profiles, APN settings, eSIM profiles). Linking task to svc_configuration enables operational traceability of which configuration was appl']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service instance in the Netcracker service inventory that this task provisions, modifies, or deactivates. Links the fulfillment task to the service layer for end-to-end service lifecycle traceability.']
  task_assigned_technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field technician assigned to execute this task in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management. Populated for CPE dispatch and GPON ONT activation tasks requiring physical field intervention. Drives field dispatch scheduling and route optimisation.']
  task_technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network or physical resource (e.g., GPON ONT, CPE device, IP address, MSISDN, SIM) in the Netcracker resource inventory that this task acts upon. Enables resource-level fulfillment traceability and inventory reconciliation.']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Provisioning and field tasks targeting transmission links (fiber activation, leased-line commissioning, wavelength provisioning) must reference the specific link. Task management and workforce dispatc']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.trouble_ticket. Business justification: Order tasks are created to resolve trouble tickets — field technician dispatch, configuration changes, and network fixes are modeled as tasks linked to the originating trouble ticket. Telecom workforc']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field work order in Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management associated with this task. Populated for tasks requiring physical field dispatch (CPE installation, GPON ONT activation). Links fulfillment task to field operations scheduling.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time at which execution of this task commenced in the downstream target system. Compared against scheduled_start_time to measure scheduling adherence and identify orchestration delays.']
  automation_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this task was executed automatically by the OSM orchestration engine (True) or required manual intervention by an operations team member (False). Key metric for measuring fulfillment automation rates and identifying candidates for further automation.']
  child_order_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of lower-level order generated by the decomposition of the parent commercial order line. Distinguishes between service-layer orders (service_order), resource-layer orders (resource_order), and network-layer orders (network_order) per TM Forum SID decomposition hierarchy.']
