  child_system_target varchar [note: 'Identifies the downstream OSS/BSS system to which this task is dispatched for execution (e.g., HLR, HSS, GPON-OLT, Amdocs, Netcracker, Ericsson Network Manager). Critical for cross-system orchestration traceability and fallout diagnosis.']
  child_system_task_ref varchar [note: 'The task or work order identifier assigned by the downstream target system (e.g., Ericsson Network Manager work order ID, Netcracker service order number, ServiceNow change request number). Enables cross-system correlation and reconciliation between OSM and downstream fulfillment systems.']
  completion_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this task reached a terminal state (completed, failed, or cancelled) in the OSM orchestration engine. Used to calculate task duration, SLA compliance, and MTTR for fulfillment operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this order task record was first created in the OSM orchestration engine following order decomposition. Serves as the audit creation timestamp and the baseline for SLA elapsed-time calculations.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data governance classification level for this order task record per the enterprise data classification policy. Determines access controls, retention policies, and handling requirements in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  decomposition_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version of the decomposition rule applied at the time this task was generated. Supports impact analysis when orchestration rules are updated and enables reproducibility of order decomposition logic for audit purposes.']
  dependency_task_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of order_task_id values that must reach \'completed\' status before this task can begin execution. Captures the dependency chain within the OSM orchestration workflow to enforce correct fulfillment sequencing (e.g., HLR provisioning must complete before VoLTE IMS registration).']
  due_time timestamp [note: 'The SLA-committed deadline by which this task must reach \'completed\' status. Derived from the parent order\'s committed delivery date and the task\'s position in the fulfillment sequence. Breach of this timestamp triggers escalation workflows.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Machine-readable error code returned by the downstream target system upon task execution failure (e.g., HLR error codes, GPON provisioning error codes, HTTP status codes from REST APIs). Distinct from fallout_reason_code which is an OSM-normalised classification.']
  execution_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The elapsed time in seconds between actual_start_time and completion_time for this task. Populated upon task completion. Used for provisioning performance benchmarking, SLA analysis, and identification of slow downstream systems contributing to order fulfillment delays.']
  execution_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering position of this task within the overall order fulfillment workflow. Determines the sequence in which tasks are executed relative to sibling tasks on the same order line. Lower values execute first.']
  fallout_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the error or exception that caused this task to fall out, as returned by the downstream target system or the OSM orchestration engine. Provides diagnostic context for manual resolution by NOC or BSS operations teams.']
  fallout_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this task has entered fallout status requiring manual intervention or escalation. True when the task has exceeded retry limits or encountered an unrecoverable error. Drives fallout management queues in OSM and ServiceNow ITSM.']
  fallout_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardised code identifying the root cause of task fallout (e.g., DOWNSTREAM_TIMEOUT, INVALID_MSISDN, RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, GPON_PORT_EXHAUSTED, HLR_REJECT). Populated when fallout_indicator is True. Used for fallout trend analysis and systemic issue identification.']
  imsi varchar [note: 'The International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) of the SIM card being provisioned by this task. Populated for HLR/HSS provisioning and SIM activation tasks. A 15-digit globally unique subscriber identifier per 3GPP standards. Classified as confidential as it uniquely identifies a subscriber on the network.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this order task record was most recently modified in the OSM orchestration engine. Tracks state transitions, retry attempts, and synchronisation updates for audit and change-management purposes.']
  max_retry_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of retry attempts permitted for this task before it is automatically escalated to fallout management. Configured per task type and downstream system target in the OSM orchestration engine.']
  msisdn varchar [note: 'The Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN) — the subscriber\'s mobile phone number — associated with the provisioning action performed by this task. Populated for HLR/HSS provisioning, SIM activation, and MNP tasks. Classified as confidential as it is a customer-linked network identifier.']
  network_technology varchar [note: 'The network technology domain relevant to this provisioning task. Determines which network management system (Ericsson Network Manager for RAN, Nokia NetAct for core, OLT for GPON) handles execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 4G_LTE|5G_NR|FTTH_GPON|VoLTE|IMS|MPLS|SD_WAN|DSLAM|fixed_wireless|eSIM|NFV|SDN — promote to reference product]']
  ont_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the Optical Network Terminal (ONT) / Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) being activated or configured by this task. Populated for GPON ONT activation and CPE dispatch tasks. Used for FTTH inventory reconciliation and field technician dispatch coordination.']
  order_action_type varchar [note: 'The commercial order action that triggered the creation of this task. Inherited from the parent order line and used to determine the appropriate provisioning logic in the downstream target system. MNP (Mobile Number Portability) port-in/port-out tasks require coordination with GSMA NRTRDE and TAP processes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_activation|upgrade|downgrade|disconnection|mnp_port_in|mnp_port_out|change_of_service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this task, inherited from the parent order or overridden by the OSM orchestration engine based on customer segment, SLA tier, or order type. Drives execution queue ordering and resource allocation in fulfillment operations.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times execution of this task has been retried following a failure or timeout. Used to enforce maximum retry thresholds, trigger fallout management escalation, and analyse systemic provisioning failure patterns across downstream systems.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which this task is scheduled to begin execution within the OSM orchestration engine. Used for SLA target calculation, capacity planning, and workforce scheduling in field dispatch scenarios.']
  sla_breach_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this task has breached its SLA due_time commitment. Set to True when completion_time exceeds due_time, or when the task remains in a non-terminal state past due_time. Drives SLA reporting, customer compensation workflows, and regulatory compliance reporting to FCC/Ofcom.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this order task record originates (e.g., OSM, Netcracker, Amdocs). Used for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and cross-system reconciliation in the Revenue Assurance domain.']
  sync_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the synchronisation state of this task with the downstream target system. Tracks whether the task execution result has been confirmed and reconciled back to the OSM orchestration engine from the child system (e.g., HLR, GPON-OLT, Netcracker).']
  task_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this order task as assigned by the OSM orchestration engine (e.g., TSK-2024-00123456). Used in NOC dashboards, trouble tickets, and cross-system correlation.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order task within the OSM orchestration engine. Drives SLA monitoring, fallout management, and retry logic. Valid states: pending (awaiting execution), in_progress (actively executing), completed (successfully fulfilled), failed (execution error), cancelled (task voided), suspended (paused pending dependency resolution).']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the atomic fulfillment action this task represents within the OSM orchestration workflow. Drives routing to the appropriate downstream system and execution handler. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hlr_hss_provisioning|ip_address_assignment|cpe_dispatch|gpon_ont_activation|number_portability_submission|sim_activation|service_configuration|esim_provisioning|voip_registration|ims_registration|mpls_circuit_setup|ran_configuration — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Granular decomposed work task generated by OSM order orchestration engine from a specific order line during order decomposition. Represents both the decomposition mapping (tracing commercial order line to lower-level service/resource/network orders) and the atomic fulfillment execution step (HLR/HSS provisioning, IP address assignment, CPE dispatch, GPON ONT activation, number portability submission). Captures parent order line reference, decomposition rule applied, child system target, child order type (service_order, resource_order, network_order), task type, execution sequence, dependency chain, start time, completion time, retry count, task-level status, synchronisation status with downstream systems, creation timestamp, and fallout detail. Provides full decomposition traceability from commercial order line through orchestration logic to network provisioning action — serving as the SSOT for both order decomposition mapping and task execution within this domain.'
}

Table order.fallout {
  fallout_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each order fallout record in the Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM) system. Primary key for the order_fallout data product.']
  assigned_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: Fallout resolution for field-related failures (site access, equipment issues, address problems) requires direct technician assignment for expedited recovery. Critical for operational escalation tracki']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise fallout tracking enables account-level quality metrics, TAM escalation workflows, customer experience dashboards for B2B customers, and proactive account management for at-risk enterprise r']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer whose order has fallen out. Enables customer-level fallout analysis and SLA breach tracking in Salesforce CRM and BSS operations.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order that entered fallout state. Links this fallout record to the originating order in the Order Management domain.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line. Business justification: A fallout record captures an order that entered ERROR state during orchestration. Fallouts are typically triggered by a failure on a specific line item (e.g., a particular service action fails). The f']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Order fallouts affecting managed service provisioning must be tracked against the managed service for SLA breach assessment, root cause analysis, and managed service reliability reporting. Enables man']
  mnp_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.mnp_transaction. Business justification: MNP fallout analysis and regulatory reporting: failed MNP ports (donor rejection, HLR failure) generate fallout records that must be linked to the mnp_transaction for root-cause analysis and regulator']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding ServiceNow ITSM incident ticket raised for this fallout event. Enables cross-system traceability between order management and IT service management workflows.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Offering-level fallout rate reporting: product managers need to identify which offerings have high order fallout rates for quality improvement. Direct FK enables offering-level fallout dashboards and ']
  problem_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.problem_record. Business justification: Recurring order fallouts linked to known network problems for pattern analysis and permanent fix tracking. Enables proactive resolution of systemic provisioning issues.']
  provisioning_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.provisioning_order. Business justification: Order fallouts frequently occur during provisioning execution. Linking fallout to the specific provisioning_order that failed enables OSS fallout resolution workflows, retry orchestration, and SLA bre']
  ran_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Mobile order fallouts caused by RAN cell issues (cell capacity exceeded, cell unavailable, radio parameter conflict) reference the specific cell. Fallout analysis and resolution for mobile provisionin']
  resolution_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.team. Business justification: Fallout records are assigned to workforce teams for resolution. The existing assigned_team column is a plain-text denormalization of workforce.team. Telecom fallout management dashboards and team work']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product catalog entry for the product or service being ordered. Used to analyze fallout rates by product and identify catalog or provisioning configuration issues.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Order fallout records track provisioning failures for a specific subscriber (SIM activation failure, MSISDN porting failure). The denormalized affected_msisdn field should be replaced by a proper FK t']
  task_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific orchestration task within the order workflow that triggered the fallout condition. Maps to the OSM task-level decomposition unit.']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Order fallouts caused by transmission link issues (capacity unavailable, link down, provisioning failure) reference the specific link. Root cause analysis, fallout resolution workflows, and network-or']
  affected_service_type varchar [note: 'The type of telecommunications service affected by the fallout. Used to route fallout cases to the appropriate specialist team and apply service-specific resolution procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER|IPTV|VOIP|ENTERPRISE_WAN|WHOLESALE — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auto_resolution_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fallout record is eligible for automated resolution without manual agent intervention, based on fallout category, retry history, and configured automation rules.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The sales or service channel through which the original order was submitted. Used to identify channel-specific fallout patterns and drive channel-level quality improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ONLINE|RETAIL_STORE|CALL_CENTER|DEALER|SELF_SERVICE|B2B_PORTAL|API — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fallout record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment of the account associated with the fallen-out order. Denormalized for workqueue prioritization and segment-level SLA application without requiring a join to the customer domain.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the order orchestration engine first detected and recorded the fallout condition. This is the principal business event timestamp for SLA measurement and aging calculations.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'System-generated error code returned by the failing downstream system or orchestration engine (e.g., OSM, HLR/HSS, provisioning platform). Used for automated pattern detection and root cause analysis.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Full descriptive error message returned by the failing system at the time of fallout detection. Provides technical context for resolution agents and is used in automated error classification models.']
  escalated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fallout record was most recently escalated to a higher resolution tier. Null if the record has never been escalated.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation tier for this fallout record (0 = not escalated, 1 = team lead, 2 = manager, 3 = vendor/third-party). Incremented automatically when SLA thresholds are breached.']
  failing_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the downstream OSS/BSS system that returned the error or failed to respond, causing the order to fall out. Examples: HLR, HSS, PROVISIONING_ENGINE, INVENTORY_MANAGER, CREDIT_SYSTEM, MNP_CLEARINGHOUSE.']
  failing_system_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'The transaction or correlation identifier returned by the failing downstream system. Enables cross-system tracing and incident correlation in ServiceNow ITSM and NOC tooling.']
  fallout_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause that drove the order into fallout state. Drives workqueue routing and resolution team assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VALIDATION_FAILURE|PROVISIONING_TIMEOUT|INVENTORY_SHORTAGE|DOWNSTREAM_SYSTEM_ERROR|CREDIT_CHECK_FAILURE|DUPLICATE_ORDER|MNP_PORT_FAILURE|FRAUD_HOLD|CAPACITY_EXHAUSTION|CONFIGURATION_ERROR — promote to reference product]']
  fallout_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fallout record within the NOC/BSS operations workqueue. Drives SLA breach prevention workflows and management dashboards.']
  last_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent automated or manual re-submission attempt for this fallout order. Used to enforce retry interval policies and prevent retry storms.']
  max_retry_allowed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of automated re-submission attempts permitted for this order type before mandatory escalation to manual resolution. Configured per order type and fallout category.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to this fallout record, determining workqueue position and escalation urgency. Derived from order type, customer segment, and SLA exposure at time of fallout detection.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to this fallout event by the OSS/BSS operations center. Used by NOC and BSS workqueue agents to track and communicate about the fallout case.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the order was being fulfilled. Used for regional fallout rate analysis and to route cases to geographically appropriate field or provisioning teams.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the corrective action taken by the resolution agent to address the fallout root cause. Captured at resolution time and used for knowledge base and automation training.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the resolution action applied to close this fallout. Enables resolution pattern analysis and automation opportunity identification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AUTO_RETRY_SUCCESS|MANUAL_CORRECTION|ORDER_CANCELLED|ORDER_MODIFIED|INVENTORY_ALLOCATED|SYSTEM_BYPASS|ESCALATED_TO_VENDOR|CUSTOMER_CONTACTED — promote to reference product]']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fallout record was marked as resolved in the operations workqueue. Used to calculate mean time to resolve (MTTR) for fallout SLA reporting.']
  resubmission_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the order re-submission attempt after resolution action was applied. Indicates whether the order successfully completed fulfillment, failed again, is awaiting outcome, or was cancelled.']
  resubmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was resubmitted into the orchestration engine after resolution action was applied. Null if the order has not yet been resubmitted.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of automated or manual re-submission attempts made for this fallout order. Used to determine when to escalate from automated retry to manual intervention.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Structured root cause analysis narrative captured by the resolution agent or automated diagnostic engine. Documents the underlying cause beyond the error code for knowledge management and recurrence prevention.']
  sla_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this fallout record will breach its applicable SLA if not resolved. Calculated at fallout detection time based on order type SLA thresholds. Null if no SLA applies.']
  sla_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA for this fallout record has been breached at the time of last update. True when current timestamp exceeds sla_breach_timestamp without resolution.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record that originated this fallout record. Enables data lineage tracking and source-system-specific reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the fallout category providing additional specificity for root cause analysis. Examples: ADDRESS_VALIDATION_FAILED under VALIDATION_FAILURE, HLR_TIMEOUT under PROVISIONING_TIMEOUT, SIM_STOCK_DEPLETED under INVENTORY_SHORTAGE.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fallout record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.']
  workflow_step_name varchar [note: 'The name of the specific orchestration workflow step or process node within Oracle OSM that was executing when the fallout occurred. Enables precise identification of the failure point in the order decomposition graph.']
  workflow_step_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence position of the failing workflow step within the overall order orchestration process. Used to understand how far into fulfillment the order progressed before failing.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing every order that has entered a FALLOUT or ERROR state during orchestration. Stores the fallout category (validation failure, provisioning timeout, inventory shortage, downstream system error), affected order and task references, fallout detection timestamp, assigned resolution team, resolution action taken, and re-submission outcome. Drives NOC and BSS operations center workqueues for manual intervention and SLA breach prevention.'
}

Table order.provisioning_request {
  provisioning_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the provisioning request record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  addon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.addon. Business justification: Add-on provisioning: when provisioning an add-on (e.g., international roaming, VoLTE, extra data bolt-on), the provisioning request must reference the specific addon for correct network configuration ']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Provisioning requests for enterprise services need corporate account context for proper system routing, account-specific configuration templates, and enterprise provisioning workflow orchestration.']
  cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Broadband provisioning requests configure specific CPE assets (APN, QoS, bandwidth profile). The provisioning system must directly reference the physical CPE being configured without joining through o']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer on whose behalf this provisioning request is being executed. Enables customer-centric analytics and SLA tracking across the provisioning lifecycle.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order in the order management domain that triggered this provisioning request. Links the provisioning instruction back to the originating customer order for end-to-end traceability. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE (via order → customer chain).']
  ip_address_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ip_address_pool. Business justification: Broadband provisioning requests allocate IP addresses from specific pools. The provisioning system references the pool for allocation and tracks utilization. ip_address_assigned on provisioning_reques']
  lawful_intercept_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.lawful_intercept_order. Business justification: Lawful intercept activation requires specific provisioning actions on network elements. Linking provisioning_request to lawful_intercept_order enables chain-of-custody verification that intercept prov']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.line. Business justification: A provisioning_request is a downstream provisioning instruction generated by OSM from a specific order line. One line can generate one or more provisioning requests (e.g., SIM provisioning, HLR activa']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Provisioning requests target specific managed service instances for configuration updates, capacity changes, service modifications, or feature activations. Essential for managed service lifecycle mana']
  mnp_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.mnp_transaction. Business justification: MNP provisioning execution: provisioning_requests for HLR/HSS updates and routing number assignments are generated as part of MNP transaction execution. Linking enables end-to-end MNP provisioning aud']
  msisdn_range_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: Mobile provisioning requests allocate MSISDNs from specific number ranges. The provisioning system references the range for number assignment during new activations and MNP. msisdn on provisioning_req']
  mvno_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: Provisioning requests for MVNO subscribers must reference the MVNO profile to obtain IMSI range, APN configuration, HLR/HSS provisioning flags, and QoS parameters. Telecom provisioning systems read MV']
  network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Provisioning requests target specific network elements for service activation (HLR/HSS for mobile, BRAS for broadband). Essential for OSS/BSS integration, tracking provisioning status per network elem']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Provisioning parameter lookup by offering: provisioning systems require direct offering reference to retrieve service parameters, QoS profiles, and APN configurations without traversing fulfillment_or']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Fiber provisioning requests activate specific ONT devices on GPON network. Real business need: OLT port binding, bandwidth profile assignment, optical power monitoring, device firmware management. Exi']
  problem_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.problem_record. Business justification: Systemic provisioning failures are escalated to problem records for root cause investigation in ITIL-based telecom operations. Linking provisioning_request to problem_record enables identification of ']
  qos_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_qos_profile. Business justification: Provisioning requests carry a qos_profile_code (denormalized plain attribute) identifying the QoS profile to apply. Replacing with a proper FK to network_qos_profile enforces referential integrity and']
  ran_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Mobile provisioning requests (IMSI/MSISDN activation, SIM provisioning) target a specific RAN cell. Mobile OSS provisioning workflows require cell-level targeting for capacity validation and radio par']
  sim_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sim_stock. Business justification: Provisioning requests configure specific SIM cards in network elements (HLR/HSS/UDM). Real business need: network activation audit, provisioning failure resolution, SIM lifecycle state synchronization']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_site. Business justification: Provisioning requests for enterprise services are executed at specific physical sites. Direct link enables site-level provisioning status dashboards, installation scheduling, and infrastructure readin']
  slice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: 5G slice provisioning requests target a specific network slice for instantiation, modification, or termination. The provisioning system must reference the slice to send correct parameters to the NSSF ']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.subscriber. Business justification: Provisioning requests execute subscriber-level service changes (SIM activation, MSISDN porting, VoLTE enablement). Telecom provisioning systems are subscriber-centric; linking provisioning_request to ']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service instance being provisioned, modified, or deactivated. Links to the service domain to identify the specific service (e.g., mobile line, broadband subscription, IPTV package) subject to this provisioning action.']
  task_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.task. Business justification: A provisioning_request is dispatched by OSM as a result of a granular work task. The task orchestrates the provisioning action and the provisioning_request is the downstream instruction sent to OSS sy']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Fixed broadband and leased-line provisioning requests target a specific transmission link for activation or modification. Provisioning teams and OSS systems must know which transmission link is being ']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the target OSS system acknowledged receipt of the provisioning instruction. Used to calculate dispatch-to-acknowledgement latency and detect timeout conditions against SLA thresholds.']
  apn varchar [note: 'The Access Point Name (APN) configured for the mobile data service being provisioned. Determines the packet data network gateway and internet access policy for the subscriber. Applicable for mobile broadband and IoT service provisioning.']
  bandwidth_profile_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the bandwidth profile (upstream/downstream speed tier) to be applied to the service. Maps to a predefined bandwidth template in the OLT, DSLAM, or BNG configuration. Examples: 100M_FTTH, 1G_FTTH, 50M_ADSL.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The originating channel through which the customer order that triggered this provisioning request was placed. ONLINE: self-service web/app; STORE: retail point of sale; CALL_CENTER: agent-assisted; PARTNER: MVNO or wholesale partner; FIELD_TECH: field technician dispatch; AUTOMATED: system-triggered (e.g., auto-renewal, policy enforcement).']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the target OSS system reported final completion (success or failure) of the provisioning action. Used to measure end-to-end provisioning cycle time and SLA compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this provisioning request record was first created in the OSM system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data governance classification level for this provisioning request record, indicating sensitivity and access control requirements. Aligned with the enterprise data classification policy: restricted (highest sensitivity), confidential, internal, public.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the provisioning instruction was dispatched from Oracle Communications OSM to the target OSS system. Represents the principal real-world business event time. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error or failure reason code returned by the target OSS system when provisioning_outcome is FAILURE or TIMEOUT. Used for fault management, root cause analysis, and automated retry logic. Examples: RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, INVALID_PARAMETER, SYSTEM_UNREACHABLE, DUPLICATE_REQUEST.']
  failure_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the failure reason as returned by the target OSS system or populated by the OSM orchestration layer. Provides operational context for NOC engineers and support teams investigating provisioning failures.']
  iccid varchar [note: 'The Integrated Circuit Card Identifier (ICCID) — the unique serial number of the SIM or eSIM card — associated with this provisioning request. Used to link the provisioning action to the physical or embedded SIM being activated.']
  imsi varchar [note: 'The International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) — a unique 15-digit identifier stored on the SIM/eSIM — being provisioned in the HLR/HSS/UDM. Applicable for mobile service activations and MNP port-in operations. Classified as restricted PII as it uniquely identifies a subscriber.']
  is_rollback_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a rollback of previously applied provisioning changes is required due to a downstream failure in a multi-step orchestration workflow. When TRUE, the OSM compensation workflow is triggered to reverse partial provisioning actions.']
  last_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent retry attempt for this provisioning request. Used in conjunction with retry_count to calculate retry intervals and enforce exponential backoff policies.']
  max_retry_limit varchar [note: 'The maximum number of retry attempts configured for this provisioning request before it is escalated or marked as permanently failed. Derived from the service type SLA policy at the time of request creation.']
  mnp_donor_operator_code varchar [note: 'For Mobile Number Portability (MNP) PORT_IN provisioning actions, the network operator code of the donor (losing) operator from whom the number is being ported. Populated per GSMA PRD IR.26 and national MNP regulatory requirements.']
  mnp_port_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual date on which the Mobile Number Portability (MNP) port-in or port-out is to be executed. Applicable only when provisioning_action is PORT_IN or PORT_OUT. Governed by national MNP regulatory timelines.']
  network_region_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic network region or Point of Presence (POP) within which the provisioning action is being executed. Used for regional capacity planning, NOC routing, and geographic SLA analysis.']
  ont_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the Optical Network Terminal (ONT) / Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) device being provisioned on the GPON OLT for FTTH fiber services. Used to bind the physical device to the subscriber\'s service profile in the OLT configuration.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this provisioning request, determining queue position and resource allocation in the OSM orchestration engine. CRITICAL: emergency/regulatory-mandated; HIGH: premium customer or SLA-sensitive; MEDIUM: standard commercial; LOW: batch/bulk provisioning.']
  provisioning_action varchar [note: 'The type of provisioning operation to be performed on the target OSS system. ACTIVATE: new service activation; MODIFY: change of service parameters; DEACTIVATE: service disconnection; SUSPEND: temporary service suspension; RESUME: reinstatement after suspension; PORT_IN: Mobile Number Portability (MNP) inbound port; PORT_OUT: MNP outbound port. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACTIVATE|MODIFY|DEACTIVATE|SUSPEND|RESUME|PORT_IN|PORT_OUT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  provisioning_outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the provisioning execution as reported by the target OSS system. SUCCESS: service fully activated/modified/deactivated as requested; FAILURE: provisioning could not be completed; TIMEOUT: target system did not respond within the configured timeout window; PARTIAL: some but not all service components were provisioned (e.g., multi-component service). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category for non-monetary transactions.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the provisioning request as generated by Oracle Communications OSM (e.g., PROV-2024-0001234). Used for cross-system traceability across OSS/BSS platforms. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the provisioning request. PENDING: awaiting dispatch; DISPATCHED: sent to target OSS system; IN_PROGRESS: being processed by target system; SUCCESS: provisioning completed successfully; FAILURE: provisioning failed; TIMEOUT: no acknowledgement received within SLA window; CANCELLED: request withdrawn before execution. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING|DISPATCHED|IN_PROGRESS|SUCCESS|FAILURE|TIMEOUT|CANCELLED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this provisioning request has been retried after an initial failure or timeout. Used to enforce maximum retry policies, trigger escalation workflows, and analyze systemic provisioning issues.']
  rollback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the rollback compensation action was executed for this provisioning request. Populated only when is_rollback_required is TRUE and the rollback has been completed.']
  scheduled_activation_date date [note: 'The date on which the service activation or modification is scheduled to take effect, as agreed with the customer or mandated by the order. May differ from dispatch_timestamp for future-dated provisioning requests.']
  service_parameters_payload varchar [note: 'Serialized (JSON or XML) payload containing the service-specific configuration parameters dispatched to the target OSS system. For mobile services: MSISDN, IMSI, APN, QoS profile; for FTTH/GPON: ONT serial number, VLAN ID, bandwidth profile; for IMS: SIP URI, codec list. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous service types.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the telecommunications service being provisioned. Determines the provisioning workflow, target OSS system, and service parameters payload structure. Examples: MOBILE (LTE/5G NR), BROADBAND (DSL/cable), FIBER_FTTH (Gigabit Passive Optical Network), VoIP, IPTV, FIXED_LINE, SD-WAN, MPLS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MOBILE|BROADBAND|FIBER_FTTH|VOIP|IPTV|FIXED_LINE|SD_WAN|MPLS — promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this provisioning request breached its SLA target duration. Set to TRUE when the actual provisioning duration exceeded sla_target_duration_seconds. Used for SLA reporting, penalty calculations, and operational performance management.']
  sla_target_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The contracted Service Level Agreement (SLA) target duration in seconds within which this provisioning request must be completed end-to-end. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and breach detection in NOC dashboards.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the upstream BSS/OSS system that originated this provisioning request. OSM: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management; NETCRACKER: Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite; AMDOCS: Amdocs Revenue Management; COMVERSE: Comverse ONE prepaid charging; MANUAL: manually created by NOC operator.']
  target_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the downstream OSS system to which this provisioning instruction is dispatched. Valid values include: HLR (Home Location Register), HSS (Home Subscriber Server), UDM (Unified Data Management), IMS (IP Multimedia Subsystem), GPON_OLT (Gigabit Passive Optical Network Optical Line Terminal), IPAM (IP Address Management), DSLAM (Digital Subscriber Line Access Multiplexer), RAN (Radio Access Network), PCRF (Policy and Charging Rules Function), PCF (Policy Control Function). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HLR|HSS|UDM|IMS|GPON_OLT|IPAM|DSLAM|RAN|PCRF|PCF — promote to reference product]']
  target_system_instance_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific system instance or node within the target OSS system (e.g., HSS cluster node ID, OLT shelf/port identifier) to which the provisioning command is routed. Supports multi-node environments and geo-redundant deployments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this provisioning request record was last modified. Tracks state transitions, retry events, and status updates for audit trail and data lineage. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']
  vlan_number varchar [note: 'The Virtual LAN (VLAN) ID assigned to the subscriber\'s service on the GPON OLT or DSLAM for traffic segregation and QoS enforcement. Applicable for FTTH and broadband service provisioning.']

  Note: 'Downstream provisioning instruction record generated by OSM and dispatched to OSS systems (HLR/HSS, UDM, IMS, GPON OLT, IP address management, DSLAM) to activate, modify, or deactivate a service on the network. Captures target system identifier, provisioning action, service parameters payload, dispatch timestamp, acknowledgement timestamp, provisioning outcome (SUCCESS, FAILURE, TIMEOUT), and retry history. Bridges the order domain to the service and network domains.'
}

Table order.service_qualification {
  service_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the service qualification record. Primary key for the service_qualification data product in the order domain Silver layer.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer premises or installation address record against which the network eligibility check is performed. Resolves to the address master in Netcracker Resource Inventory or the geographic address repository.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise service qualification requests require corporate account linkage for credit validation, contract eligibility checks, account-level service portfolio management, and pre-sales feasibility an']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (prospect or existing subscriber) on whose behalf the service qualification is being performed. Links to the customer master in Salesforce CRM / Netcracker BSS. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction.']
  fiber_cable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.fiber_cable. Business justification: Fiber service qualification checks whether fiber cable infrastructure reaches the customer address. The qualification process references the specific cable segment for availability and capacity assess']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order or pre-order request that triggered this service qualification check in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management. Null if the qualification was initiated as a standalone eligibility check outside of a formal order.']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Re-qualification or upgrade of an existing managed service requires linking the qualification record to the managed service being assessed. Enables managed service upgrade eligibility reporting and te']
  mvno_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: MVNO service qualifications must check MVNO-specific constraints: subscriber capacity limits, IMSI/MSISDN range availability, spectrum access rights, and technical capabilities - required for MVNO sub']
  olt_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.olt_asset. Business justification: Fiber service qualification checks OLT port capacity and operational status before committing to a fiber service offer. Direct reference to the serving OLT asset is required for capacity management, q']
  product_offering_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product offering in the Netcracker Product Catalog for which eligibility is being assessed. Drives which product offers are surfaced to the sales agent or digital channel upon a positive qualification outcome.']
  ran_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Mobile service qualification identifies serving cell, measures signal strength, and validates capacity availability. Critical pre-sale process for determining mobile service eligibility and setting cu']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: service_qualification.broadband_subsidy_eligible and regulatory_zone_code indicate qualifications governed by regulatory obligations (universal service, broadband subsidy programs). Linking to the gov']
  sdwan_topology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.sdwan_topology. Business justification: Network infrastructure readiness qualification for SD-WAN topology deployment (bandwidth availability, latency assessment, underlay transport options) must reference the SD-WAN topology. Enables topol']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_site. Business justification: Service qualification for enterprise customers is performed against a specific physical site. Site-level qualification drives field survey scheduling, infrastructure readiness assessment, and installa']
  slice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.slice. Business justification: Service qualification for 5G slice services checks whether a specific slice type and configuration is available in the coverage area. Pre-sales qualification for enterprise slice offerings must refere']
  survey_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: When field_survey_required=true on a service qualification, a technician is dispatched for a pre-sales site survey. Telecom pre-sales operations track which technician conducted the survey to link sur']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Fixed broadband and leased-line service qualification checks capacity and availability on a specific transmission link. Pre-sales and order entry teams query transmission link status and utilization t']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the customer premises being qualified for service. Captured at qualification time as a point-in-time snapshot to avoid dependency on address master changes post-qualification.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (apartment, suite, floor, unit number) of the customer premises being qualified. Nullable for single-dwelling units.']
  available_technology_options varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of all access technologies technically available at the qualified address (e.g., \'FTTH|LTE|5G_NR\'). Enables the sales system to present all eligible product offers, not just the primary requested technology. Sourced from Netcracker Resource Inventory network coverage data.']
  broadband_subsidy_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service address qualifies for government broadband subsidy programs (e.g., FCC Affordable Connectivity Program (ACP), NTIA BEAD, UK Gigabit Voucher Scheme). Determined based on regulatory_zone_code and address-level coverage data. Relevant for compliance reporting to FCC, NTIA, and Ofcom.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the service installation address. Used for geographic coverage reporting and network rollout planning analytics.']
  constraint_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any technical or operational constraints identified during the qualification check when the outcome is ELIGIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS. Examples: \'Maximum speed limited to 100 Mbps due to copper loop length\', \'CPE upgrade required\', \'Shared GPON port — peak-hour congestion possible\'. Null when outcome is ELIGIBLE or NOT_ELIGIBLE.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the service installation address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for multi-country operators and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  cpe_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) installation or upgrade is required to deliver the requested service at the qualified address. True: CPE installation/upgrade is mandatory; False: existing CPE is compatible or no CPE is needed.']
  cpe_type varchar [note: 'Type of CPE required for service delivery at the qualified address. ONT (Optical Network Terminal) for FTTH/GPON; ROUTER for broadband; MODEM for DSL; GATEWAY for combined modem/router; OUTDOOR_UNIT/INDOOR_UNIT for fixed wireless. Null when cpe_required is False.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this service qualification record was first persisted in the Silver layer data product. Populated by the ingestion pipeline from Oracle OSM source system.']
  estimated_installation_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days from order confirmation to service activation at the qualified address. Derived from field workforce availability (Granite WFM), infrastructure readiness, and network node capacity. Displayed to customers during the ordering flow to set delivery expectations.']
  estimated_latency_ms decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated round-trip network latency in milliseconds at the qualified address for the requested service type. Relevant for QoS-sensitive product offers (VoLTE, gaming, enterprise SD-WAN) and SLA eligibility determination.']
  field_survey_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical field survey by a Telecommunication technician is required before service can be provisioned at this address. True when infrastructure readiness is PARTIAL or NOT_READY, or when the qualification engine cannot determine feasibility remotely. Triggers a field dispatch work order in Granite WFM.']
  ineligibility_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the address is NOT_ELIGIBLE for the requested service. NO_COVERAGE: outside network footprint; CAPACITY_EXHAUSTED: node at full capacity; INFRASTRUCTURE_NOT_BUILT: planned but not yet deployed; ADDRESS_NOT_FOUND: address could not be geocoded or matched; TECHNICAL_BARRIER: physical impediment; REGULATORY_RESTRICTION: regulatory constraint. Null when outcome is ELIGIBLE or ELIGIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS.']
  infrastructure_readiness varchar [note: 'Indicates the physical network infrastructure readiness at or near the service address. READY: full infrastructure in place; PARTIAL: partial build-out, service possible with constraints; NOT_READY: no infrastructure present; UNDER_CONSTRUCTION: active network build in progress with a planned completion date.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording the most recent update to this service qualification record in the Silver layer. Updated on any status change, outcome determination, or data correction.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the service installation point. Used for precise network coverage mapping, field dispatch routing via Granite WFM, and FCC broadband location fabric matching.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the service installation point. Used in conjunction with latitude for geospatial coverage analysis and network node proximity calculations.']
  max_download_speed_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum technically achievable downstream bandwidth in Megabits per second (Mbps) at the qualified address, as determined by the network qualification engine based on distance to node, line quality, and technology capability. Drives product tier eligibility in the sales flow.']
  max_upload_speed_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum technically achievable upstream bandwidth in Megabits per second (Mbps) at the qualified address. Critical for symmetric service offerings (FTTH/GPON) and business-grade SLA commitments.']
  mnp_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Mobile Number Portability (MNP) is applicable to this service qualification, relevant for mobile service types (LTE, 5G NR) where the customer intends to port an existing number. True triggers MNP eligibility checks in the downstream order flow.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or remarks added by the sales agent, network engineer, or automated qualification engine to provide additional context about the qualification result. May include special instructions for the field team, customer-specific considerations, or escalation notes.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the service installation address. Key dimension for network coverage gap analysis, FCC broadband availability reporting, and NTIA compliance.']
  qualification_channel varchar [note: 'The sales or service channel through which the qualification request was initiated. ONLINE: digital self-service portal; RETAIL_STORE: in-store agent; CALL_CENTER: inbound/outbound agent via Salesforce CRM; PARTNER_API: wholesale or MVNO partner API; FIELD_AGENT: mobile field sales; SELF_SERVICE: automated IVR or chatbot.']
  qualification_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the qualification check was fully resolved and an outcome was determined. Null if the qualification is still IN_PROGRESS or was CANCELLED before completion.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the qualification result is considered stale and must be re-evaluated before an order can be placed. Network conditions, capacity, and infrastructure status can change; this date enforces re-qualification for orders placed after a defined validity window (typically 30–90 days).']
  qualification_outcome varchar [note: 'Business result of the pre-order service qualification check. ELIGIBLE: the address/location can receive the requested service without restriction; NOT_ELIGIBLE: service cannot be delivered to this location; ELIGIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS: service is technically feasible but subject to limitations (e.g., reduced bandwidth, extended lead time, CPE upgrade required).']
  qualification_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the qualification request by Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM). Used by sales agents and customers to track the qualification check.']
  qualification_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the qualification request was submitted by the sales or ordering system. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  qualification_source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system that submitted the qualification request. OSM: Oracle Communications Order and Service Management; SALESFORCE: Salesforce CRM; NETCRACKER: Netcracker OSS/BSS Suite; PARTNER_PORTAL: wholesale/MVNO partner portal; SELF_SERVICE_PORTAL: customer-facing digital portal. Used for data lineage and system-of-record traceability.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the qualification request workflow within Oracle OSM. IN_PROGRESS: check is being executed; COMPLETED: outcome determined; FAILED: system error prevented completion; CANCELLED: request withdrawn before completion.']
  regulatory_zone_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory or geographic zone code assigned to the service address, used to determine applicable regulatory obligations (e.g., FCC Universal Service Fund (USF) eligible area, NTIA BEAD-eligible zone, Ofcom Universal Service Obligation (USO) area, BEREC regulated zone). Drives compliance reporting and subsidy eligibility.']
  requalification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this qualification result has been flagged as requiring re-execution before an order can proceed. Set to True when the qualification_expiry_date has passed, when network topology changes have occurred in the serving area, or when the customer requests a different service type.']
  requested_service_type varchar [note: 'The access technology or service type for which eligibility is being assessed. Values align with the network technologies offered by Telecommunication: FTTH (Fiber to the Home), GPON (Gigabit Passive Optical Network), LTE (Long-Term Evolution), 5G NR (5G New Radio), ADSL, VDSL, FTTB (Fiber to the Building), FTTC (Fiber to the Cabinet), FIXED_WIRELESS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTTH|GPON|LTE|5G_NR|ADSL|VDSL|FTTB|FTTC|FIXED_WIRELESS — promote to reference product]']
  signal_strength_dbm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Measured or predicted radio signal strength in dBm at the qualified address for wireless service types (LTE, 5G NR, Fixed Wireless). Null for wireline technologies. Sourced from Ericsson Network Manager RAN coverage data or Nokia NetAct RF planning tools.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the service installation address. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping (FCC, NTIA) and regional network coverage analytics.']
  survey_scheduled_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the field survey is scheduled to be conducted at the customer premises, when field_survey_required is True. Populated by Granite WFM upon technician dispatch scheduling. Null when no survey is required.']

  Note: 'Pre-order service qualification check record determining whether a customer address or network location is eligible for a requested service (FTTH, GPON, LTE, 5G NR, ADSL, VDSL). Captures qualification request timestamp, customer address reference, requested service type, qualification outcome (ELIGIBLE, NOT_ELIGIBLE, ELIGIBLE_WITH_CONSTRAINTS), available technology options, maximum achievable bandwidth, estimated installation lead time, and network node reference. Drives product offer eligibility in the sales and ordering flow.'
}

Table order.appointment {
  appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the field technician appointment record within the order fulfillment domain. Primary key for the appointment data product.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for whom the on-site appointment is being conducted. Enables customer-centric reporting and SLA tracking.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_contact. Business justification: Field appointments require a specific authorized contact at the premises for access and sign-off. The denormalized customer_contact_name and customer_contact_phone fields should be replaced by a prope']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: Appointments are scheduled from depots managing technician territories and service zones. Essential for capacity planning, technician assignment optimization, and tracking which facility supports cust']
  device_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_model. Business justification: Field technician appointment preparation: technicians need the specific device model to bring correct equipment, firmware, and installation tools. cpe_model is a denormalized device model reference; r']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order that triggered this appointment. Links the appointment to the end-to-end order lifecycle managed in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management (OSM).']
  inventory_cpe_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cpe_asset. Business justification: Field appointments install or swap specific CPE devices at customer premises. Real business need: technician dispatch with correct device, installation verification, asset tracking, warranty activatio']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Field appointments for managed service installations, maintenance windows, and fault resolution must be linked to the managed service for SLA measurement, change window compliance, and managed service']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: Appointments for network equipment installation, maintenance, or replacement at enterprise sites or exchange locations reference the specific inventory asset. Required for maintenance scheduling, asse']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a ServiceNow ITSM incident record associated with this appointment, if the appointment was triggered by or resulted in a fault management incident. Enables cross-domain traceability between order fulfillment and fault management.']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Fiber installation appointments reference the specific ONT asset to be installed at the customer premises. Technicians need direct ONT asset reference for pre-staging, configuration preparation, and p']
  ran_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: Field appointments for RAN cell installation, antenna alignment, or cell site maintenance reference the specific cell. Workforce scheduling and dispatch for cell site visits require cell-level appoint']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.site. Business justification: Field appointments for enterprise customers target specific sites. Critical for technician dispatch, site access coordination, site contact management, and tracking installation history per location.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.svc_instance. Business justification: A field appointment is scheduled to install, repair, or upgrade a specific service instance. Linking appointment to svc_instance enables field operations to track which service a technician visit addr']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.team. Business justification: Complex telecom installations (fiber, enterprise) require crew-based appointments assigned to a team rather than a single technician. Field operations scheduling and team capacity planning require lin']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assigned field technician from the workforce domain. Populated by Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management (WFM) during dispatch scheduling.']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Field appointments for transmission link installation, fiber splicing, or leased-line commissioning reference the specific link. Technician dispatch and appointment scheduling for fixed-line work requ']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.trouble_ticket. Business justification: Field appointments are frequently scheduled to resolve trouble tickets — technician dispatched to fix reported service fault. Telecom field operations require linking appointments to originating troub']
  access_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for the field technician to gain access to the customer premises (e.g., gate codes, building manager contact, parking instructions, safety requirements). Critical for enterprise and multi-dwelling unit installations.']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the field technician arrived at the customer premises. Compared against scheduled_start_time to measure on-time arrival performance and SLA adherence.']
  actual_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the field technician completed the on-site work and closed the appointment. Used to calculate job duration, MTTR (Mean Time to Repair), and workforce productivity metrics.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken to complete the on-site work, calculated from actual_arrival_time to actual_completion_time. Used for workforce productivity analysis and future duration estimation model training.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable appointment reference number communicated to the customer and field technician. Follows the format APT-NNNNNNNNNN. Used in customer notifications, WFM dispatch sheets, and trouble ticket references.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the appointment. Drives downstream workflow actions: SCHEDULED (created, not yet confirmed), CONFIRMED (customer acknowledged), COMPLETED (work done), MISSED (technician or customer no-show), RESCHEDULED (new slot assigned), CANCELLED (appointment voided).']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the on-site work to be performed. Drives technician skill matching, equipment pre-staging, and estimated duration in WFM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTTH_INSTALLATION|GPON_INSTALLATION|CPE_SWAP|ONT_REPLACEMENT|ENTERPRISE_CIRCUIT_COMMISSIONING|FAULT_REPAIR|UPGRADE|DOWNGRADE|DISCONNECTION — promote to reference product]']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the field technician upon appointment completion. Captures work performed, issues encountered, materials used, and any follow-up actions required. Populated when appointment_status is COMPLETED.']
  confirmation_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer confirmed the appointment. Supports omnichannel analytics and confirmation effectiveness reporting. Populated only when customer_confirmed is True.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer confirmed the appointment. Populated only when customer_confirmed is True. Used for lead-time analysis and confirmation workflow SLA measurement.']
  cpe_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the specific CPE or ONT unit assigned for installation or replacement. Enables asset tracking, warranty management, and post-installation inventory reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appointment record was first created in the system. Audit trail field aligned with data governance and regulatory record-keeping requirements.']
  customer_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has explicitly confirmed the scheduled appointment. True = confirmed by customer; False = not yet confirmed. Drives automated reminder workflows and no-show risk scoring.']
  dispatch_work_order_number varchar [note: 'External work order reference number generated by Granite Telecommunications WFM upon technician dispatch. Used to cross-reference appointment records with WFM dispatch sheets and field activity logs.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes required to complete the on-site work. Derived from appointment type and technology type during WFM scheduling. Used for route optimization and technician capacity planning.']
  follow_up_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why a follow-up appointment or action is required. Populated only when follow_up_required is True. Used for work order creation and technician briefing.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up appointment or action is required after this appointment. True = follow-up needed (e.g., incomplete installation, pending network provisioning). Triggers creation of a new appointment or trouble ticket.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the appointment record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  missed_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the appointment was missed. Populated only when appointment_status is MISSED. Used for SLA breach analysis, customer compensation workflows, and workforce accountability reporting.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the appointment, influencing scheduling queue position and technician dispatch urgency. CRITICAL is reserved for enterprise SLA-bound circuits and regulatory commitments.']
  reschedule_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this appointment has been rescheduled. Tracks appointment instability, customer experience impact, and operational efficiency. High reschedule counts may trigger escalation workflows.']
  reschedule_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the most recent reschedule of this appointment. Populated when appointment_status is RESCHEDULED. Used for root cause analysis and operational improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUSTOMER_REQUEST|TECHNICIAN_UNAVAILABLE|WEATHER|EQUIPMENT_NOT_READY|ACCESS_ISSUE|NETWORK_DEPENDENCY|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the field technician appointment is scheduled to occur. Used for day-level planning, route optimization, and customer communication.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the appointment window is scheduled to end. Defines the committed service window communicated to the customer and used for SLA compliance tracking.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the appointment window is scheduled to begin. Used for technician dispatch, customer notification, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) measurement.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer service segment for which the appointment is being conducted. Drives SLA tier assignment, technician skill requirements, and regulatory reporting categorization.']
  sla_commitment_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the appointment was completed within the committed SLA (Service Level Agreement) window. True = SLA met; False = SLA breached. Drives regulatory reporting, customer compensation, and performance dashboards.']
  sla_target_date date [note: 'The contractual or regulatory deadline by which the appointment must be completed to satisfy the SLA commitment. Derived from order creation date and applicable SLA tier.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this appointment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking. Primary source is Granite Telecommunications WFM; secondary sources include Oracle OSM and Salesforce CRM.']
  technology_type varchar [note: 'Network access technology associated with the appointment work. Determines required technician certifications, tools, and equipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTTH|GPON|FTTB|HFC|DSL|FIXED_WIRELESS|ENTERPRISE_FIBER|5G_FWA — promote to reference product]']
  wfm_job_reference varchar [note: 'Unique job identifier assigned by Granite Telecommunications Workforce Management system for this appointment. Enables bidirectional traceability between the order domain and the WFM dispatch system.']

  Note: 'Field technician installation or service appointment record associated with a fulfillment order requiring on-site work — FTTH/GPON installation, CPE swap, ONT replacement, or enterprise circuit commissioning. Captures appointment type, scheduled date and time window, customer address, assigned technician reference (linked to workforce domain), appointment status (SCHEDULED, CONFIRMED, COMPLETED, MISSED, RESCHEDULED), customer confirmation flag, and completion notes. Integrates with Granite Telecommunications WFM for dispatch.'
}

Table order.sla {
  sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the order SLA commitment record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_agreement. Business justification: Partner-originated orders may have SLA terms defined in partner agreements rather than standard retail SLAs - required for partner SLA compliance measurement and penalty/credit calculation in telecomm']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise SLA performance reporting is aggregated at corporate account level for account management reviews, churn risk assessment, and executive dashboards. Direct link enables account-level SLA bre']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Order SLA commitments are governed by enterprise contracts that define SLA tiers, penalty clauses, and breach thresholds. Direct link enables contract-level SLA breach penalty calculation and regulato']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order to which this SLA commitment is attached. Links the SLA record to its parent order in Oracle Communications Order and Service Management.']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. Business justification: SLA clocks are suspended during NOC-declared network incidents in telecom. order.sla has clock_suspended and suspension_reason fields indicating this scenario. Linking to the noc_incident enables accu']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Offering-level SLA compliance reporting: SLA commitments and breach rates must be tracked per product offering for regulatory reporting, product quality management, and customer segment SLA dashboards']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Many order SLAs are regulatory-mandated (FCC broadband deployment commitments, EU consumer protection timelines, MNP porting deadlines). Telcos must link SLA definitions to their regulatory source for']
  sla_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.sla_definition. Business justification: Operational SLA records must reference the contractual SLA definition from the partner agreement that governs measurement method, penalty formula, and thresholds. Telecom operators link live SLA insta']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: An order SLA commitment is governed by the service SLA profile tier (e.g., Gold, Platinum) associated with the ordered service. Linking order.sla to service.sla_profile enables SLA compliance reportin']
  transmission_link_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.transmission_link. Business justification: Order-level SLAs for leased-line and fixed broadband services are tied to the specific transmission link. SLA measurement, breach detection, and regulatory reporting for fixed-line services require kn']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.work_order. Business justification: SLA breach root-cause analysis in telecom requires direct linkage between an SLA record and the work order responsible for field execution delays. SLA compliance reporting and penalty calculation depe']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the measurement_end_event occurred, marking fulfillment of the order. Null if the order has not yet been completed. Used to compute final SLA outcome (met or breached) for regulatory reporting.']
  breach_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal breach notification has been dispatched to the customer and/or regulatory authority (True) following a BREACHED status. Required for compliance with FCC, Ofcom, and BEREC breach reporting obligations.']
  breach_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the breach notification was sent to the customer and/or regulatory authority. Null if no notification has been sent. Used to verify compliance with mandatory notification timeframes.']
  breach_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The number of elapsed hours from the measurement start event at which the SLA is formally classified as BREACHED. May equal target_completion_hours or include a grace period defined in the contract or regulatory instrument.']
  breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time at which the SLA was formally classified as BREACHED (i.e., elapsed hours exceeded breach_threshold_hours). Null if the SLA has not been breached. Critical for regulatory penalty calculations and breach notification obligations.']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Defines how elapsed time is measured for this SLA: CALENDAR_HOURS (24x7 clock), BUSINESS_HOURS (measured only during defined business operating hours), or BUSINESS_DAYS (measured in working days). Determines how target_completion_timestamp is calculated.']
  clock_suspended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA measurement clock is currently paused (True) due to a customer hold, third-party dependency, or regulatory exclusion event. When True, elapsed_hours accumulation is halted until the clock is resumed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this SLA record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED timestamp for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the penalty_credit_amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Ensures multi-currency support for international operations.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment to which the order and SLA apply: CONSUMER (residential), SMB (Small and Medium Business), ENTERPRISE (large corporate), WHOLESALE (carrier/partner), or MVNO (Mobile Virtual Network Operator). Determines SLA tier and applicable contractual terms.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this SLA definition version became effective. Used for SLA versioning and to ensure the correct SLA terms are applied to orders placed within a given period.']
