Business justification: Billing adjustments (credits, waivers) are frequently triggered by failed field visits, repeat technician dispatches, or SLA breaches on work orders. Direct work_order_id on adjustment enables SLA bre']
  adjustment_category varchar [note: 'Business category classifying the nature of the adjustment for reporting, revenue assurance, and analytics purposes. Supports MRR/ARPU impact analysis and churn management reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goodwill|sla_compensation|dispute_resolution|overbilling_correction|equipment_return|service_outage|promotional|other — promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable reference number for the adjustment. Used in customer communications, dispute correspondence, and financial reconciliation. Format: ADJ- followed by 10 digits.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the adjustment. Pending: awaiting approval. Approved: authorized but not yet applied to the account. Applied: posted to the billing account. Reversed: previously applied adjustment that has been undone. Expired: approved adjustment that was not applied within the validity window.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment increases (credit) or decreases (debit) the customer\'s account balance. A credit reduces the amount owed; a debit increases it.']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment was posted and applied to the billing account balance. Populated when adjustment_status transitions to \'applied\'.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment was formally approved by the authorizing agent. Supports approval audit trails and SLA compliance for dispute resolution timelines.']
  approval_threshold_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment amount exceeded the standard agent approval threshold, requiring escalated authorization. Supports revenue assurance controls and fraud prevention.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period to which this adjustment relates. Used alongside billing_period_start for period-accurate revenue recognition and financial reporting.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period to which this adjustment relates. Used for period-accurate revenue recognition, MRR reporting, and financial close processes.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP cost center code associated with this adjustment for internal financial allocation and OPEX reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this adjustment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  credit_note_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit note was formally issued to the customer. Populated only when document_type is \'credit_note\'. Determines the financial period for revenue recognition and regulatory reporting.']
  credit_note_number varchar [note: 'Formal reference number of the customer-facing credit note document. Populated only when document_type is \'credit_note\'. Used in customer communications, accounts receivable reconciliation, and regulatory financial reporting. Format: CN- followed by 10 digits.']
  credited_onetime_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the net adjustment amount attributable to one-time charges such as activation fees, equipment charges, or installation fees.']
  credited_recurring_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the net adjustment amount attributable to recurring subscription or plan charges (e.g., monthly plan fees, MRR components). Supports MRR impact analysis.']
  credited_usage_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the net adjustment amount attributable to usage charges (e.g., voice, data, SMS). Supports breakdown of credited amounts by charge type for revenue assurance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this adjustment record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency billing environments.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the adjustment as either an internal account balance correction (internal_adjustment) or a formal customer-facing financial document (credit_note). When document_type is credit_note, the credit_note_number and credit_note_issue_date fields are populated.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business date on which the adjustment takes effect on the billing account. Determines the billing period impacted and is used for revenue recognition period alignment.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which an approved but unapplied adjustment expires and can no longer be applied to the billing account. Supports dunning and collections workflows.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger account code to which this adjustment is posted for financial accounting purposes. Supports SAP S/4HANA integration and revenue recognition journal entries.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The pre-tax gross amount of the adjustment before tax impact is applied. Positive value for credits; negative value for debits. Used in revenue recognition and MRR reporting.']
  is_customer_visible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment is visible to the customer on their invoice or self-service portal. Internal adjustments may not be customer-facing.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net amount of the adjustment including gross amount, tax amount, and tax reversal. Represents the actual financial impact posted to the billing account. Used for revenue assurance and ARPU impact reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments entered by the agent or system regarding this adjustment. Captures supplementary context not covered by structured fields.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the business reason for the adjustment. Examples include: GOODWILL, SLA_BREACH, OVERBILLING, DISPUTE_RESOLUTION, EQUIPMENT_RETURN, SERVICE_OUTAGE, BILLING_ERROR. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GOODWILL|SLA_BREACH|OVERBILLING|DISPUTE_RESOLUTION|EQUIPMENT_RETURN|SERVICE_OUTAGE|BILLING_ERROR — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific reason for the adjustment, supplementing the reason_code with contextual details such as the nature of the SLA breach, outage duration, or billing discrepancy.']
  requires_manager_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment requires manager-level approval due to amount thresholds, category sensitivity, or policy rules. Drives approval workflow routing in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment amount was recognized in the financial statements under IFRS 15. Populated when revenue_recognition_flag is true.']
  revenue_recognition_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment has been recognized in the financial revenue recognition process under IFRS 15. Supports financial close and revenue assurance reporting.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date on which a previously applied adjustment was reversed. Populated when adjustment_status transitions to \'reversed\'. Used for revenue assurance and audit trail purposes.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason why a previously applied adjustment was reversed. Populated when adjustment_status is \'reversed\'. Supports revenue assurance and audit compliance.']
  source_reference_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this adjustment record in the originating source system (e.g., Amdocs internal adjustment ID, Comverse ONE transaction reference). Supports cross-system reconciliation and data lineage.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this adjustment. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements across BSS platforms.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The tax component of the adjustment, representing the tax impact associated with the gross adjustment amount. May be positive (credit) or negative (debit) depending on adjustment_type.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment is exempt from tax calculation. Applicable for certain goodwill credits or adjustments on tax-exempt accounts.']
  tax_reversal_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of tax reversed as part of this adjustment, applicable when the original invoice tax must be partially or fully reversed. Distinct from tax_amount which represents new tax applied to the adjustment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this adjustment record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Credit or debit adjustment applied to a billing account, serving as SSOT for all account balance corrections including internal adjustments and customer-facing credit note documents. Captures adjustment type (credit/debit), document type (internal adjustment or customer-facing credit note), reason code, amount, tax impact, tax reversal amount, credit note number and issue date (when document type is credit note), approving agent, approval date, reference dispute or ticket ID, reference invoice, credited amount breakdown, and adjustment status (pending, approved, applied, reversed, expired). Covers goodwill credits, SLA breach compensation, dispute resolutions, overbilling corrections, returned equipment credits, and service outage compensation. When document type is credit note, serves as the formal financial document issued to the customer. Sourced from Amdocs Revenue Management adjustments module.'
}

  billing_charge_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific charge or rated event being disputed. Nullable when the dispute covers an entire invoice rather than a single line item.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.channel. Business justification: Disputes are raised through specific channels (retail store, online, call center). Channel-level dispute reporting is required for regulatory compliance and channel performance management. billing_dis']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Enterprise disputes require corporate account context for executive escalation workflows, relationship health scoring, churn risk assessment, and corporate-level dispute trend analysis. B2B dispute ma']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Telecom dispute management systems track disputes at customer_account level for SLA compliance, regulatory complaint reporting, and account-level dispute rate KPIs. Direct FK enables account dispute h']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Disputes are raised by specific authorized contacts (account holders, authorized users, corporate account managers). Tracking who raised dispute is critical for authorization validation, communication']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Enterprise billing disputes are frequently about contract terms (discount application, minimum commitment charges, penalty clauses). Dispute resolution and contract compliance reporting require linkin']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: A billing dispute is typically raised against a specific invoice. Linking billing_dispute to the disputed invoice enables invoice-level dispute tracking, disputed amount reconciliation, and invoice st']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Billing disputes involving partners (roaming charge disputes, wholesale billing disputes). Required for partner dispute management, settlement dispute resolution, and inter-operator dispute tracking i']
  previous_dispute_billing_dispute_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the earlier dispute record if this is a repeat or re-opened dispute. Enables dispute lineage tracking and repeat-dispute root-cause analysis. Nullable for first-time disputes.']
  rated_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rated_event. Business justification: A billing dispute can be raised against a specific rated_event (e.g., disputed call duration, data usage charge). billing_dispute has a cdr_reference string field that partially captures this, but a p']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: When a billing dispute escalates to a regulatory complaint, the telco must submit a regulatory filing to the authority. billing_dispute.regulatory_complaint_flag and regulatory_reference_number (denor']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Dispute resolution SLAs (sla_due_date, sla_breached on billing_dispute) are mandated by specific regulatory obligations (consumer protection regulations). Compliance teams must report dispute resoluti']

Business justification: Customer billing disputes for roaming charges often trigger or relate to wholesale settlement disputes with roaming partners—essential for coordinated dispute resolution and determining liability betw']
