  wifi_password varchar [note: 'Default or configured Wi-Fi password for CPE devices with Wi-Fi capability. Encrypted at rest.']
  wifi_ssid varchar [note: 'Default or configured SSID for CPE devices with Wi-Fi capability.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this CPE asset record.']

  Note: 'Master record for Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) assets including routers, modems, set-top boxes, ONTs, and Wi-Fi gateways. Tracks device serial number, MAC address, model, firmware version, ownership type (owned/leased/BYOD), procurement date, warranty expiry, current assignment status, and physical condition. SSOT for all CPE hardware managed via Oracle OSM resource inventory.'
}

Table inventory.sim_stock {
  sim_stock_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SIM stock record. Primary key for physical SIM cards and eSIM profiles managed in the operator\'s inventory system.']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: SIM stock is physically stored at and distributed from depots to technicians and retail channels. Depot-level SIM inventory tracking is a core telecom logistics process for stock replenishment, alloca']
  lawful_intercept_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.lawful_intercept_order. Business justification: Lawful intercept orders target specific IMSI/ICCID identifiers for surveillance. LEA compliance requires linking intercept orders to physical SIM inventory for provisioning intercept capabilities, mai']
  msisdn_range_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: SIM cards are assigned MSISDN numbers from managed number ranges. The msisdn_range tracks blocks of numbers allocated from regulatory authorities. Linking sim_stock to msisdn_range enables tracking of']
  mvno_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: MVNO SIM provisioning requires knowing which MVNO profile each SIM card belongs to. Telecom operators allocate SIM batches to specific MVNO profiles for IMSI/MSISDN range assignment and profile-based ']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: SIM stock is provisioned and activated against a specific product offering (prepaid, postpaid, IoT plan). Telecom operators must track which offering a SIM batch is allocated to for provisioning autom']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: SIM stock is procured from and allocated to MVNO/supplier partners. Partner SIM allocation tracking and supplier reconciliation reports require this FK. supplier_name is a denormalized text representa']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the subscriber to whom this SIM stock unit is assigned. Null if the SIM is unallocated or reserved.']
  target_device_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_model. Business justification: eSIM profiles and physical SIMs are pre-configured for specific device models (iPhone 14, Samsung Galaxy S23). Critical for activation workflows, compatibility validation, and inventory allocation in ']
  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SIM stock unit was activated and provisioned for service. Marks the transition to in-service status.']
  activation_code varchar [note: 'Unique activation code or QR code string used by the end user to download and install the eSIM profile. Only applicable for eSIM profiles.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SIM stock unit: unallocated (in warehouse), reserved (allocated to order), activated (in service), suspended (temporarily disabled), deactivated (service terminated), or retired (end of life).']
  allocated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SIM stock unit was allocated or reserved for a specific order or subscriber. Marks the transition from unallocated to reserved status.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing or provisioning batch identifier for grouping SIM cards or eSIM profiles. Used for quality control, recall management, and inventory tracking.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, or rack location within the warehouse for physical SIM card storage. Enables precise inventory picking and cycle counting. Null for eSIM profiles.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SIM stock record was first created in the inventory system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP.']
  deactivated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SIM stock unit was deactivated and removed from service. Marks the end of the active service lifecycle.']
  download_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the eSIM profile was downloaded from the SM-DP+ platform to the target device. Only applicable for eSIM profiles.']
  eid varchar [note: '32-digit unique identifier for the embedded Universal Integrated Circuit Card (eUICC). Only applicable for eSIM profiles. Null for physical SIM cards.']
  esim_profile_state varchar [note: 'State of the eSIM profile on the SM-DP+ platform: available (ready for download), downloaded (transferred to device), installed (on eUICC), enabled (active), disabled (inactive), or deleted (removed). Only applicable for eSIM profiles.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the SIM card or eSIM profile expires and should no longer be issued. Used for inventory rotation and obsolescence management.']
  form_factor varchar [note: 'Physical form factor of the SIM card: 2FF (Mini-SIM), 3FF (Micro-SIM), 4FF (Nano-SIM), MFF2 (M2M form factor), or embedded (eSIM). Null for eSIM profiles.']
  iccid varchar [note: 'Unique 19-20 digit identifier for the SIM card or eSIM profile. Globally unique identifier assigned by the card manufacturer or SM-DP+ platform.']
  imsi_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending IMSI number in the range allocated to this SIM stock batch. Defines the upper boundary of the IMSI allocation for inventory management.']
  imsi_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting IMSI number in the range allocated to this SIM stock batch. 15-digit identifier used for subscriber authentication in mobile networks.']
  is_test_sim boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this SIM stock unit is designated for testing, lab use, or quality assurance purposes. True for test SIMs, false for production SIMs.']
  ki_value decimal(18,2) [note: '128-bit secret authentication key stored on the SIM card for network authentication. Highly sensitive cryptographic material. Encrypted at rest.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the SIM card manufacturer or eSIM profile provider. Examples: Gemalto, Giesecke+Devrient, IDEMIA, Oberthur Technologies.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date when the physical SIM card was manufactured or the eSIM profile was generated on the SM-DP+ platform.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Mobile network generation supported by the SIM card or eSIM profile: 2G (GSM), 3G (UMTS), 4G LTE, 5G NR, or multi (supports multiple generations).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special handling instructions related to this SIM stock unit. Used for operational annotations.']
  opc_value decimal(18,2) [note: '128-bit operator-specific variant of the authentication algorithm configuration. Used in conjunction with Ki for LTE/5G authentication. Encrypted at rest.']
  pin1 varchar [note: '4-8 digit PIN code for SIM card access control. Required for device unlock and SIM operations. Encrypted at rest.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the SIM profile: consumer (handset), M2M (machine-to-machine), IoT (Internet of Things), enterprise (corporate), test (lab/QA), or bootstrap (initial provisioning).']
  puk1 varchar [note: '8-digit PIN Unblocking Key used to reset PIN1 after multiple failed attempts. Critical for customer self-service. Encrypted at rest.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the procurement of this SIM stock batch. Links inventory to procurement and accounts payable.']
  quality_check_date date [note: 'Date when the quality assurance check was completed. Used for compliance tracking and inventory release management.']
  quality_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the quality assurance check performed on the SIM stock: pending (awaiting inspection), passed (approved for use), failed (rejected), or not_required (no QA needed).']
  received_date date [note: 'Date when the SIM stock was received into the warehouse or provisioned on the SM-DP+ platform. Marks the start of inventory availability.']
  roaming_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether international roaming is enabled for this SIM stock unit. True if roaming is allowed, false if restricted to home network.']
  sim_type varchar [note: 'Type of SIM card: physical SIM card or eSIM profile. Determines the inventory management and provisioning workflow.']
  smdp_address varchar [note: 'Fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the Subscription Manager Data Preparation Plus (SM-DP+) server hosting the eSIM profile. Only applicable for eSIM profiles.']
  unit_cost decimal(10,4) [note: 'Per-unit cost of the SIM card or eSIM profile in the operator\'s base currency. Used for inventory valuation and CAPEX tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SIM stock record was last modified in the inventory system. Audit trail for record changes.']

  Note: 'Master inventory record for all SIM stock units including physical SIM cards (2FF/3FF/4FF) and eSIM profiles managed on the operator\'s SM-DP+ platform. Physical SIM attributes: ICCID, IMSI range, batch number, manufacturer, form factor, warehouse location, and expiry date. eSIM profile attributes: EID (eUICC Identifier), profile ICCID, SM-DP+ profile state (downloaded/enabled/disabled/deleted), activation code, download timestamp, target device model, and profile type (operational/test/bootstrap). Common attributes: profile type (consumer/M2M/IoT), activation status (unallocated/reserved/activated/deactivated), subscriber assignment, and lifecycle timestamps. SSOT for all SIM and eSIM inventory managed per GSMA SGP.22 RSP specification via Netcracker resource inventory and Comverse ONE.'
}

  ont_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ONT hardware asset record. Primary key for the ONT asset inventory.']
  device_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.device_model. Business justification: ONTs (Huawei MA5671, Nokia G-010S-A) are specific device models.   installation_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: ONT installation at customer premises is performed by field technicians. Tracking installer supports quality control, first-time-fix metrics, warranty claims, and technician performance evaluation—sta']
  ip_address_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ip_address_pool. Business justification: ONT assets require management IP addresses for remote configuration, monitoring, and firmware updates. These management IPs are assigned from specific IP address pools (typically a dedicated managemen']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Internal equipment identifier used in OSS/BSS systems for asset tracking and service provisioning workflows.']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: ONT assets are provisioned with bandwidth profiles and QoS parameters driven by the specific fiber broadband offering subscribed. Telecom provisioning systems require this link to apply correct downst']
  olt_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent OLT device to which this ONT is connected in the GPON/FTTH network topology.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: ONT devices are supplied by vendor partners. Business processes: warranty claim processing, RMA management, supplier performance tracking, spare parts procurement. Vendor_name denormalized; proper FK ']
  staging_depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: ONT assets are physically staged at depots before field deployment and returned post-decommission. Telecom fiber operations require depot-level ONT stock visibility for dispatch planning and inventory']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber account currently using services through this ONT device.']
  supply_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: ONT devices are procured from partners under supply agreements. Fiber broadband operations require linking ONT assets to their supply/maintenance agreements for warranty management, partner performanc']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the ONT was activated and provisioned in the network management system for service delivery.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tracking tag or barcode identifier used for inventory management and physical asset audits.']
  configuration_profile varchar [note: 'Name of the configuration profile or template applied to this ONT for service provisioning and feature enablement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ONT asset record was first created in the inventory management system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the ONT was decommissioned and removed from active service. Null if still in service.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of ONT device type: SFU (Single Family Unit), HGU (Home Gateway Unit), MDU (Multi-Dwelling Unit), MTU (Multi-Tenant Unit).']
  distance_from_olt_meters varchar [note: 'Physical fiber distance in meters between the OLT and this ONT, calculated from optical signal propagation delay.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the ONT device. Critical for feature support and security patching.']
  gpon_port_reference varchar [note: 'Unique GPON identifier assigned to the ONT for authentication and provisioning in the PON network.']
  hardware_version varchar [note: 'Hardware revision version of the ONT device indicating manufacturing batch and component specifications.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the ONT device was physically installed at the customer premises or network location.']
  last_firmware_update_date date [note: 'Date when the ONT firmware was last updated or patched.']
  last_online_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last successful connection and communication between the ONT and OLT.']

  max_upstream_bandwidth_mbps varchar [note: 'Maximum upstream bandwidth capacity in megabits per second that this ONT device supports.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this ONT asset.']
  olt_port_number varchar [note: 'Physical port identifier on the OLT device where this ONT is connected, typically in shelf/slot/port format.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the ONT asset in the network lifecycle.']
  optical_power_rx_dbm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Received optical power level at the ONT in decibels relative to one milliwatt (dBm). Critical for signal quality monitoring.']
  optical_power_tx_dbm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Transmitted optical power level from the ONT in decibels relative to one milliwatt (dBm). Used for upstream signal quality assessment.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the ONT is owned by the service provider, leased from a vendor, or owned by the customer.']
  port_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Ethernet ports available on the ONT device for customer equipment connectivity.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'Date when the ONT asset was purchased from the vendor.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number under which this ONT asset was procured from the vendor.']
  reboot_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the ONT device has been rebooted since installation. Used for stability analysis.']
  remote_management_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether remote management and configuration capabilities are enabled on this ONT device.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the ONT device. Used for device identification and warranty tracking.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current operating temperature of the ONT device in degrees Celsius, monitored for thermal management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ONT asset record was last modified in the inventory management system.']
  uptime_hours varchar [note: 'Total number of hours the ONT has been continuously operational since last reboot or power cycle.']
  voip_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ONT device supports VoIP telephony services with integrated analog telephone adapters.']
  warehouse_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage facility location code where the ONT is stored when not deployed. Null if currently deployed.']
  wifi_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ONT device has integrated WiFi capability for wireless connectivity.']

  Note: 'Master record for Optical Network Terminal (ONT) hardware assets deployed at customer premises in FTTH/GPON networks. Captures ONT serial number, model, vendor, GPON port assignment, optical power levels, firmware version, installation date, service address, and operational state. Linked to OLT infrastructure and fiber plant records. Managed via Oracle OSM and Netcracker.'
}

}

Table inventory.fiber_cable {

  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.depot. Business justification: Fiber cable reels and segments are stored at depots before deployment. Telecom field operations require depot-level fiber cable inventory visibility for project planning, dispatch logistics, and mater']
  installation_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: Fiber cable deployment is performed by specialized outside plant technicians. Tracking installer supports quality control, maintenance history, and accountability for cable performance issues—essentia']
  olt_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.olt_asset. Business justification: Fiber cable segments in GPON/FTTH networks feed into OLT assets at the central office or headend. Tracking which OLT a fiber cable segment terminates at is essential for physical plant management, cap']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Fiber infrastructure involves construction partners, joint build agreements, IRU arrangements. Business processes: shared infrastructure billing, right-of-way partner coordination, joint ownership tra']
  termination_network_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.element. Business justification: Fiber cables terminate at network elements (OLT, ODF, DWDM terminal). Logical-to-physical mapping for circuit provisioning, port assignment, and fault isolation requires linking physical fiber to term']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Physical asset tag or barcode identifier affixed to splice enclosures or cable markers for field identification. Used for asset tracking and inventory audits.']
  assigned_fiber_count varchar [note: 'Number of fibers currently allocated to active services or reserved for planned deployments. Used for utilization tracking and capacity management.']
  attenuation_db_per_km decimal(6,3) [note: 'Signal loss measurement in decibels per kilometer. Critical QoS metric for network planning and service quality assurance.']
  available_fiber_count varchar [note: 'Number of unused (dark) fibers available for new service provisioning. Calculated as total fiber count minus assigned fibers. Critical for capacity planning.']
  cable_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the fiber cable segment used in field operations, engineering drawings, and OSP documentation.']
  cable_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fiber optic cable based on transmission mode and construction. Single-mode for long-haul, multi-mode for short-distance, ribbon for high-density, hybrid for mixed applications.']
  conduit_fill_ratio_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of conduit cross-sectional area occupied by cables. Industry best practice limits fill to 60-70% to allow for future expansion and cable movement. Null for non-conduit deployments.']
  conduit_inner_diameter_mm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Internal diameter of the conduit in millimeters. Used for calculating fill ratio and determining additional cable placement capacity. Null for non-conduit deployments.']
  conduit_material_type varchar [note: 'Material composition of the conduit housing the fiber cable. HDPE (High-Density Polyethylene) is standard for modern installations. Null for non-conduit deployments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fiber cable inventory record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost. Supports multi-currency asset tracking for international operations.']
  deployment_method varchar [note: 'Installation technique used for the fiber cable segment. Determines maintenance procedures, access requirements, and environmental exposure.']
  end_location_gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the cable segment ending point in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and field dispatch.']
  end_location_gps_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the cable segment ending point in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and field dispatch.']
  environmental_zone varchar [note: 'Classification of the deployment environment affecting cable specifications and maintenance requirements. Coastal zones require corrosion-resistant materials, mountainous terrain affects accessibility.']
  fiber_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual optical fibers contained within the cable. Critical for capacity planning and strand-level inventory management.']
  fire_rating varchar [note: 'Fire safety classification of the cable sheath. OFNR (Optical Fiber Non-conductive Riser), OFNP (Plenum), OFCP (Conductive Plenum), LSZH (Low Smoke Zero Halogen). Required for building code compliance.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the fiber cable segment was physically installed and made operational. Used for warranty tracking, depreciation calculations, and lifecycle management.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical or optical inspection of the cable segment. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fiber cable inventory record. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  maintenance_responsibility varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for ongoing maintenance and repair of the fiber cable segment. Determines escalation procedures and cost allocation for fault resolution.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s catalog or part number for the specific cable model. Used for procurement, spare parts ordering, and technical specification lookup.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection based on maintenance policies and regulatory requirements. Used for work order planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, historical incidents, or unique characteristics of the cable segment not captured in structured fields.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fiber cable segment. In-service indicates active use, available for new provisioning, reserved for planned deployment, under construction during installation, maintenance during repair, damaged requiring attention, retired for decommissioned assets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_service|available|reserved|under_construction|maintenance|damaged|retired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal ownership classification of the fiber asset. IRU (Indefeasible Right of Use) represents long-term lease rights. Critical for asset management and financial reporting.']
  right_of_way_permit_number varchar [note: 'Government or municipal permit number authorizing the cable installation in public or private right-of-way. Required for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  route_length_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length of the fiber cable segment measured in meters along the deployment route. Used for attenuation calculations and material inventory.']
  sheath_type varchar [note: 'Outer protective jacket material of the fiber cable. Determines environmental resistance, fire safety compliance, and installation method suitability.']
  splice_point_count varchar [note: 'Number of splice enclosures or splice points along this cable segment. Each splice introduces potential signal loss and maintenance access points.']
  start_location_gps_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the cable segment starting point in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and field dispatch.']
  start_location_gps_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the cable segment starting point in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping and field dispatch.']
  temperature_rating_max_celsius varchar [note: 'Maximum operating temperature in Celsius for the fiber cable. Critical for deployments in extreme climates to ensure signal integrity.']
  temperature_rating_min_celsius varchar [note: 'Minimum operating temperature in Celsius for the fiber cable. Critical for deployments in extreme climates to ensure signal integrity.']

  Note: 'Master record for fiber optic cable segments and conduit/duct infrastructure in the outside plant (OSP). Cable attributes: cable type (single-mode/multi-mode/ribbon), fiber count, sheath type, route length, attenuation measurements, splice point references, and strand-level capacity summary. Conduit/duct attributes: duct ID, material type, inner diameter, route segment, total length, fill ratio, associated cable IDs, installation date, and ownership (owned/leased/municipal). Common attributes: route geometry (GPS coordinates), installation date, deployment method (burial/aerial/conduit), ownership type (owned/leased/IRU/municipal), and route segment identification. SSOT for all physical fiber plant and duct infrastructure inventory supporting OSP capacity planning and fiber rollout project management.'
}

Table inventory.ip_address_pool {
  ip_address_pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IP address pool resource. Primary key for the pool entity.']
  network_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network device managing this pool (BNG, BRAS, leaf switch, DHCP server). Links pool to physical infrastructure.']
  parent_pool_ip_address_pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to parent pool if this is a sub-pool carved from a larger allocation. Enables hierarchical pool management and delegation.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: IP address pools are allocated to MVNO and wholesale partners for their subscriber base. Telecom IP address management and MVNO wholesale operations require tracking which partner owns or is allocated']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: LI-enabled IP pools (is_lawful_intercept_enabled=true) and CGNAT pools are governed by specific regulatory obligations (CALEA, data retention mandates). Compliance teams must track which regulatory ob']
  allocation_policy varchar [note: 'Strategy for assigning resources from this pool. Sequential for ordered assignment, random for load distribution, least-used for utilization balancing.']
  assigned_count varchar [note: 'Current number of resources actively assigned from this pool. Updated in near-real-time as assignments and releases occur.']
  assignment_scope varchar [note: 'Type of entity that resources from this pool are assigned to. Subscriber for end-user accounts, CPE for customer equipment, network node for infrastructure.']
  available_count varchar [note: 'Current number of unassigned resources available for allocation. Calculated as total_capacity minus assigned_count minus reserved_count.']
  cgnat_ratio varchar [note: 'Number of subscribers sharing each public IP address in CGNAT pools. Typical ratios range from 16:1 to 256:1 depending on service type and usage patterns.']
  cidr_block varchar [note: 'CIDR notation for IP address pools (e.g., 10.0.0.0/16 for IPv4, 2001:db8::/32 for IPv6). Null for non-IP resource types.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code for charging pool-related expenses (IP address leasing fees, equipment costs). Used for chargeback and financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pool record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail for resource lifecycle tracking.']
  default_lease_duration_hours varchar [note: 'Default lease duration in hours for dynamic assignments from this pool. Applies to DHCP leases and temporary allocations. Null for static pools.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this pool is scheduled to be retired or decommissioned. Null for pools with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this pool becomes active and available for resource assignment. Used for planned pool activations and migrations.']
  encapsulation_type varchar [note: 'Network encapsulation protocol used for this resource pool. 802.1Q for single VLAN tagging, QinQ for double tagging, VXLAN for overlay networks, EVPN for BGP-based VPN.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where this pool is deployed (e.g., Northeast, California, EMEA). Used for regional resource planning and compliance.']
  high_watermark_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Utilization percentage threshold that triggers capacity alerts. Typically 80-90% to allow time for pool expansion before depletion.']
  ip_version varchar [note: 'IP protocol version for address pools. IPv4 for legacy networks, IPv6 for modern deployments, dual-stack for transition scenarios.']
  is_cgnat_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pool is used for CGNAT (Carrier-Grade NAT) to share public IPs across multiple subscribers. Common for IPv4 conservation.']
  is_lawful_intercept_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assignments from this pool are subject to lawful intercept logging and monitoring requirements per regulatory mandates.']
  is_public_routable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IP addresses in this pool are publicly routable on the internet (true) or private/RFC1918 addresses (false).']
  last_assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent resource assignment from this pool. Used for activity tracking and idle pool identification.']
  last_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent resource release back to this pool. Used for churn analysis and capacity reclamation tracking.']
  low_watermark_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Utilization percentage threshold below which the pool is considered underutilized. Used for resource optimization and consolidation decisions.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this pool record. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pool record was last modified. Updated whenever pool attributes, capacity, or configuration changes.']
  network_zone varchar [note: 'Network architecture layer where this pool is deployed. Core for backbone, edge for distribution, access for last-mile, data center for cloud services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: core|edge|access|aggregation|metro|data_center|customer_premises — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form operational notes and comments about this pool. Used for documenting special configurations, migration plans, or operational considerations.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the pool, used in operational systems and external references.']
  pool_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the IP address pool, used for identification and operational reference.']
  pool_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit, department, or team responsible for managing this pool. Used for accountability and operational ownership.']
  pool_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pool. Active pools are available for assignment, depleted pools have no available resources, quarantined pools are temporarily blocked.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory designation for this pool. Lawful intercept pools require logging for law enforcement, emergency services pools have priority routing requirements.']
  reserved_count varchar [note: 'Number of resources reserved for future use or specific purposes (e.g., network infrastructure, management, emergency services).']
  resource_type varchar [note: 'Type of logical network resource managed by this pool. Distinguishes between IP address pools, VLAN segment pools, VXLAN pools, and CGNAT ranges.']
  routing_domain varchar [note: 'Routing domain or VRF (Virtual Routing and Forwarding) instance this pool belongs to. Enables network segmentation and multi-tenancy.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Business service category this pool is dedicated to. Enables service-based segmentation of network resources for QoS and billing purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|enterprise|wholesale|management|voice|iot|mobile_backhaul — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of assignable resources in this pool. For IP pools, count of usable addresses; for VLAN pools, count of VLAN IDs.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization rate of the pool, calculated as (assigned_count / total_capacity) * 100. Used for capacity planning and threshold alerting.']
  vlan_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending VLAN ID for VLAN pool resources. Must be greater than or equal to vlan_range_start. Null for non-VLAN resource types.']
  vlan_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting VLAN ID for VLAN pool resources. Valid range 1-4094 per IEEE 802.1Q. Null for non-VLAN resource types.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this pool record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master record for logical network resource pools and their individual assignments. Covers IP address pools (IPv4/IPv6 subnets, CGNAT ranges), VLAN/VXLAN segment pools, and individual resource assignment transactions. Pool-level attributes: CIDR block, VLAN range, encapsulation type (802.1Q/QinQ/VXLAN/EVPN), allocation policy, associated BNG/BRAS or leaf switch, service type segmentation (residential/enterprise/wholesale/management/voice), utilization tracking, and maximum capacity. Assignment-level attributes: assigned IP or VLAN, assignment type (static/dynamic/DHCP), assignment start/end timestamps, lease duration, assigned-to entity (subscriber/CPE/network node), and release reason. SSOT for all logical network resource inventory and assignment lifecycle supporting broadband provisioning, enterprise VPN, IMS voice, and wholesale services. Managed via Netcracker.'
}

Table inventory.msisdn_range {
  msisdn_range_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MSISDN or DID number range record. Primary key for the number range inventory.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interconnect.carrier. Business justification: MSISDN ranges are allocated to specific carriers/operators for regulatory and routing purposes. MNP operations, roaming routing, and number range audits require knowing which interconnect.carrier owns']
  hlr_network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: msisdn_range.hlr_reference: STRING is a denormalized string reference to the HLR (Home Location Register) or HSS (Home Subscriber Server) network equipment node that routes calls/data for numbers in t']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: MSISDN ranges allocated to MVNO partners for their subscriber base. Critical business processes: wholesale number allocation, MVNO billing, number portability coordination, regulatory compliance repor']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: MSISDN number ranges are allocated by national regulatory authorities via formal filings. The existing regulatory_allocation_reference plain-text field is a denormalization of the regulatory filing re']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this number range was activated and made available for assignment to subscribers or services.']
  allocation_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or numbering authority that allocated this number range to the operator.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date when this number range was officially allocated to the telecommunications operator by the regulatory authority.']
  assigned_count varchar [note: 'Current count of numbers from this range that have been assigned to subscribers or services.']
  available_count varchar [note: 'Current count of numbers from this range that are available for new assignment. Excludes quarantined and reserved numbers.']
  block_size varchar [note: 'Standard block size or increment in which numbers from this range are typically allocated or reserved for operational efficiency.']
  cost_per_number decimal(15,4) [note: 'Regulatory fee or cost per individual number in this range charged by the numbering authority for allocation and maintenance.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ITU-T E.164 country calling code for the number range. Identifies the country or geographic region of the number allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this number range record was first created in the inventory management system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost per number and related financial attributes.']
  end_number varchar [note: 'The last MSISDN or DID number in the allocated range. Must comply with ITU-T E.164 international numbering format.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory allocation of this number range expires and must be renewed or returned to the numbering authority.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or service area associated with this number range for routing and billing purposes.']
  last_assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent number assignment transaction from this range to a subscriber or service.']
  last_deallocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent number deallocation or return transaction from a subscriber back to available inventory.']
  ndc varchar [note: 'National destination code or area code portion of the number range. Used for geographic or network routing within the country.']
  network_operator_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the network operator or Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) to which this range is assigned.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, comments, or administrative information about this number range for internal reference.']
  number_format_mask varchar [note: 'Display format mask or template for presenting numbers from this range to end users in customer-facing systems and billing documents.']
  porting_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether numbers in this range are eligible for mobile number portability per national MNP regulations.']
  quarantine_period_days varchar [note: 'Mandatory cooling-off period in days that numbers from this range must remain quarantined after deallocation before becoming available for reassignment per regulatory requirements.']
  quarantined_count varchar [note: 'Count of numbers in quarantine status awaiting release per regulatory cooling-off period after deallocation.']
  range_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the number range allocation. Used for external reference and operational tracking.']
  range_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the number range indicating its availability and operational state.']
  range_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the number range indicating its purpose and usage category within the telecommunications network.']
  reserved_count varchar [note: 'Count of numbers reserved for specific purposes such as VIP customers, corporate accounts, or regulatory requirements.']
  routing_prefix varchar [note: 'Network routing prefix used for call routing and signaling for numbers in this range within the telecommunications infrastructure.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Primary service type or technology platform that numbers in this range are designated to support.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that is the authoritative source for this number range data. Typically Oracle OSM Resource Inventory or Netcracker.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any special regulatory conditions, restrictions, or usage requirements imposed on this number range by the numbering authority.']
  start_number varchar [note: 'The first MSISDN or DID number in the allocated range. Must comply with ITU-T E.164 international numbering format.']
  total_capacity varchar [note: 'Total count of individual numbers available within this range from start to end number inclusive.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this number range record was last modified in the inventory management system.']
  usage_category varchar [note: 'Business classification indicating the intended customer segment or usage purpose for numbers in this range.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage of numbers in this range currently assigned to subscribers or services relative to total capacity. Key performance indicator for number resource management.']

  Note: 'Master record for MSISDN (Mobile Subscriber ISDN Number) and DID (Direct Inward Dialing) number ranges and their individual number assignment transactions. Range-level attributes: start/end number, country code, NDC, range type, regulatory allocation reference, porting eligibility, and available count. Assignment-level attributes: specific number assigned, subscriber reference, assignment date, service type, porting status (MNP port-in/port-out), quarantine release date, deallocation reason, and assignment lifecycle timestamps. SSOT for all number resource inventory, assignment lifecycle, and MNP compliance per ITU-T E.164 and national MNP regulations.'
}

  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (decommissioning, write-offs, major state changes) are frequently triggered by compliance audit findings. The existing audit_date and auditor_name plain attributes are denormali']

Fiber cable lifecycle events (installation, inspection, splice repair, conduit damage, decommission) must link directly to the fiber cable record. P']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order or internal order that triggered this lifecycle event (e.g., service activation order, equipment procurement order). Nullable for non-order-driven events.']
  ip_address_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ip_address_pool. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to ip_address_pool. IP address pools are logical inventory assets that undergo lifecycle transitions (creation, exhaustion alerts, CGNAT enablement, decommission, p']
  msisdn_range_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.msisdn_range. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to msisdn_range. MSISDN/DID number ranges are logical inventory assets that undergo lifecycle transitions (allocation from regulator, activation, quarantine, expira']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to network_equipment. Network equipment lifecycle events (installation, hardware swap, end-of-life, audit) must link directly to the network equipment record. This ']
  noc_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.noc_incident. OLT lifecycle events (commissioning, maintenance, firmware upgrade, decommission) must link directly to the OLT asset record. Part of the normalizatio']

ONT lifecycle events (installation, firmware update, decommission, optical power audit) must link directly to the ONT asset record. Part of the normal']
  outage_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.outage_record. Business justification: Asset replacements performed during outage restoration (e.g., emergency OLT swap, fiber splice) must link to the outage record for restoration cost tracking, post-outage review, and regulatory reporti']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (installation, maintenance, decommission) are frequently executed by or attributed to specific partners. Partner SLA performance reporting and partner-specific asset lifecycle a']
  problem_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.problem_record. Business justification: ITIL problem management permanent fixes often involve asset changes (firmware upgrades, hardware replacement). Linking asset_lifecycle_event to problem_record supports tracking which asset changes wer']
  ran_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.ran_cell. Business justification: RAN cell commissioning, decommissioning, hardware swap, and software upgrade events are tracked as asset lifecycle events. Telecom field operations teams record RAN cell state transitions (installed, ']
  sim_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sim_stock. Business justification: Typed FK from asset_lifecycle_event to sim_stock, replacing the polymorphic asset_reference pattern. SIM stock lifecycle events (activation, deactivation, profile download, quarantine) must be traceab']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field technician or workforce member who performed the lifecycle event (deployment, swap, repair). Nullable for automated or non-field events.']
  triggering_alarm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.alarm_event. Business justification: Asset state changes (decommission, replacement, emergency swap) are often triggered by specific alarm events. Linking asset_lifecycle_event to alarm_event supports tracing asset decisions back to the ']
  trouble_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.trouble_ticket. Business justification: When event_type=\'FAILURE\' or \'RETURN\', links asset failure events to originating trouble tickets for root cause analysis, warranty claim substantiation, and vendor quality scorecards.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field service work order that triggered this lifecycle event (e.g., installation, repair, swap work order). Nullable for non-field-service events.']
  actual_count varchar [note: 'Actual quantity of assets found during the physical audit or cycle count. Applicable only for audit and reconciliation events.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle event required managerial or system approval before execution (e.g., write-off events typically require approval). True if approval was required, False otherwise.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status for this lifecycle event. Applicable only if approval_required is True.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lifecycle event was approved. Nullable if approval was not required or is still pending. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Category or class of the asset involved in this event (e.g., CPE, ONT, OLT, fiber-cable, spare-part). Used for audit and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPE|ONT|OLT|fiber-cable|network-equipment|spare-part|SIM|eSIM|test-equipment|tools — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lifecycle event complies with regulatory or internal policy requirements (e.g., proper audit trail, authorized personnel, documented approval). True if compliant, False if non-compliant or flagged for review.']
  cost_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial impact of this lifecycle event in the base currency (e.g., asset acquisition cost for procurement, write-off amount for decommission, repair cost). Nullable for non-financial events.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this lifecycle event record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost_impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Nullable if cost_impact is null.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about the lifecycle event (e.g., reason for swap, repair details, audit findings).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lifecycle event occurred in the real world (e.g., when the asset was deployed, swapped, or decommissioned). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of lifecycle event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: procurement|deployment|swap|repair|decommission|write-off|physical-audit|cycle-count|reconciliation|relocation|upgrade|downgrade — promote to reference product]']
  expected_count varchar [note: 'Expected quantity of assets at the audited location or category based on system records. Applicable only for audit and reconciliation events.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this lifecycle event record was last updated in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  new_state varchar [note: 'The asset state immediately after this lifecycle event occurred. Captures the to state in the state transition.']
  previous_state varchar [note: 'The asset state immediately before this lifecycle event occurred. Captures the from state in the state transition.']
  reconciliation_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the inventory variance (e.g., write-off for lost assets, adjustment to correct system records, investigation for unresolved discrepancies). Applicable only for reconciliation events.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the asset at the time of the event. Captured for traceability and audit purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this lifecycle event record (e.g., Oracle OSM, Netcracker, SAP S/4HANA, ServiceNow). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction or event identifier from the source system. Used for traceability and idempotency in data integration.']
  variance_quantity varchar [note: 'Difference between expected and actual count (actual_count minus expected_count). Positive indicates surplus, negative indicates shortage. Applicable only for audit and reconciliation events.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause or reason for the inventory variance identified during audit or reconciliation. Applicable only for audit and reconciliation events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theft|damage|data-entry-error|unreported-deployment|unreported-return|obsolescence|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all lifecycle state transitions, audit events, and inventory reconciliation activities for physical inventory assets (CPE, ONT, OLT, network equipment, fiber plant, spare parts). Event types: procurement, deployment, swap, repair, decommission, write-off, physical-audit, cycle-count, and reconciliation. Core attributes: event timestamp, previous/new asset state, triggering work order or order reference, technician, location change. For audit/reconciliation events: audit date, stocking location or asset category audited, auditor name, expected vs actual count, variance quantity, variance reason, and reconciliation action taken (write-off/adjustment/investigation). SSOT for full asset lifecycle audit trail and inventory accuracy reporting per TM Forum eTOM asset management process, SAP S/4HANA asset management, and regulatory compliance requirements.'
}

  installation_technician_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.technician. Business justification: Network equipment installation requires certified technicians. Tracking installer enables asset lifecycle accountability, warranty validation, and technician performance tracking—standard practice in ']
  maintenance_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: Network equipment is covered by specific partner maintenance agreements (hardware support contracts). Telecom operations teams track which agreement governs each equipment\'s maintenance SLA, warranty ']
  maintenance_sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.sla_contract. Business justification: Network equipment is covered by vendor maintenance SLA contracts (MTTR guarantees, spare parts availability). Linking network_equipment to sla_contract supports vendor SLA compliance monitoring, maint']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Network equipment has vendor relationships for support contracts, firmware updates, technical support. Business processes: maintenance contract management via maintenance_contract_id, vendor SLA track']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Network equipment deployment (base stations, towers, data center gear) requires regulatory filings for site permits, environmental compliance, and zoning approvals. Telecom compliance teams must link ']
  spectrum_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.spectrum_license. Business justification: Radio network equipment (base stations, RAN nodes) must operate under a valid spectrum license. Regulators require operators to demonstrate each transmitting network element is covered by an active sp']
  administrative_status varchar [note: 'Administrative control state indicating whether the equipment is permitted to provide service. UNLOCKED (allowed to operate), LOCKED (administratively disabled), SHUTTING_DOWN (graceful shutdown in progress).']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tracking identifier assigned by the organization for inventory management and physical audits. Typically a barcode or RFID tag.']
  climate_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment requires temperature and humidity-controlled environment. True for most indoor equipment, false for outdoor-hardened units.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this equipment record was first created in the inventory system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  end_of_life_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment is planned for decommissioning and removal from the network. Drives CAPEX planning for replacements.']
  end_of_sale_date date [note: 'Date announced by the vendor when the equipment model is no longer available for purchase. Triggers lifecycle planning and migration strategies.']
  end_of_support_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor discontinues technical support, software updates, and spare parts availability. Critical for technology refresh planning.']
  equipment_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the physical equipment unit. Used for warranty tracking, maintenance history, and asset identification.']
  equipment_subtype varchar [note: 'Detailed equipment classification within the type category. Examples: eNodeB, gNodeB, BNG, BRAS, MPLS_ROUTER, SD_WAN_EDGE, IMS_CSCF, HSS, UDM, PCF, DSLAM, OLT_GPON, OLT_XGS_PON, RECTIFIER, BATTERY_BANK.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of network equipment by functional layer: RAN (Radio Access Network - eNodeB/gNodeB), CORE (IMS/HSS/UDM/PCF), TRANSPORT (MPLS routers/SD-WAN), ACCESS (BNG/BRAS/DSLAM/OLT), POWER (rectifiers/batteries), CPE (Customer Premises Equipment).']
  frequency_bands varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of radio frequency bands supported by RAN equipment. Examples: 700MHz, 1800MHz, 2100MHz, 2600MHz, 3500MHz, 28GHz. Null for non-radio equipment.']
  hardware_revision varchar [note: 'Hardware revision or version code assigned by the manufacturer. Used to track component changes and compatibility.']
  hostname varchar [note: 'Fully qualified domain name (FQDN) or hostname assigned to the equipment for network identification and DNS resolution.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment was physically installed and commissioned at the site. Used for age tracking and lifecycle management.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Primary management IP address (IPv4 or IPv6) assigned to the equipment for network management, monitoring, and configuration access.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance activity performed on the equipment. Used for maintenance scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this equipment record. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  max_power_draw_watts decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum power consumption in watts under peak load conditions. Critical for power supply sizing and cooling requirements.']
  mtbf_hours varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-rated Mean Time Between Failures in hours. Reliability metric used for spare parts planning and network resilience design.']
  mttr_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average time in hours required to repair or replace the equipment when a failure occurs. Used for SLA planning and workforce scheduling.']
  network_function varchar [note: 'Primary network function or service role performed by the equipment. Examples: PACKET_CORE, VOICE_SWITCHING, BROADBAND_AGGREGATION, RADIO_ACCESS, TRANSPORT_ROUTING, POLICY_CONTROL.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance activity based on vendor recommendations or organizational policy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special configurations, known issues, or operational notes about the equipment.']
  operating_temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum ambient temperature in Celsius at which the equipment can operate within specifications. Used for cooling system design.']
  operating_temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum ambient temperature in Celsius at which the equipment can operate within specifications. Critical for outdoor and remote site deployments.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the equipment: IN_SERVICE (active and carrying traffic), STANDBY (ready but not active), MAINTENANCE (undergoing planned maintenance), FAULTY (failed or degraded), DECOMMISSIONED (retired from service), RESERVED (allocated but not yet deployed).']
  port_capacity_gbps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate throughput capacity across all ports in gigabits per second (Gbps). Used for capacity planning and traffic engineering.']
  port_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical or logical ports available on the equipment for connectivity (e.g., Ethernet ports, fiber ports, radio carriers).']
  power_draw_watts decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical power consumption of the equipment in watts under normal operating conditions. Used for power capacity planning and OPEX calculation.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number under which the equipment was procured. Links to procurement and financial systems for cost tracking.']
  rack_location varchar [note: 'Physical rack identifier within the site where the equipment is mounted. Format varies by site convention (e.g., R01, RACK-A-12).']
  rack_unit_height varchar [note: 'Number of rack units (RU) occupied by the equipment. Standard values: 1U, 2U, 4U, etc.']
  rack_unit_position varchar [note: 'Starting rack unit position (1U = 1.75 inches) from the bottom of the rack where the equipment is mounted. Used for physical space planning and cable management.']
  redundancy_role varchar [note: 'Role of the equipment in redundancy configuration. PRIMARY (active), SECONDARY (standby backup), STANDALONE (no redundancy), N_PLUS_1 (shared backup for multiple primaries).']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware or software version running on the equipment. Format typically follows semantic versioning (e.g., 21.4.2, 5.1.0-R2A).']
  supported_standards varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of technology standards and protocols supported by the equipment. Examples: LTE, 5G_NR, GPON, XGS_PON, DOCSIS_3.1, MPLS, SD_WAN, VoLTE, IMS.']

  Note: 'Master record for core, transport, and access network equipment assets and their hardware model reference catalog. Equipment instance attributes: equipment type, vendor, model, serial number, rack/slot location (FK to rack_slot_port), POP/site (FK to pop_facility), power draw, software version, operational status, and maintenance contract reference. Covers RAN base stations (eNodeB/gNodeB), BNG/BRAS, MPLS routers, SD-WAN edge nodes, IMS/HSS/UDM/PCF nodes, and DSLAM units. Equipment model catalog attributes: model name, manufacturer, equipment category (CPE/ONT/OLT/RAN/transport/core/power), technical specifications (ports, throughput, capacity), supported standards (LTE/5G NR/GPON/XGS-PON/DOCSIS 3.1), physical dimensions, power requirements, MTBF rating, end-of-sale/support/life dates, and approved vendor status. SSOT for non-OLT/ONT network equipment inventory and hardware model catalog. Managed via Ericsson Network Manager and Nokia NetAct.'
}

Table interconnect.carrier_agreement {
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for carrier_agreement']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Wholesale and MVNO agreements are commercial contracts with enterprise customers. Business need: tracking which enterprise account holds the roaming/interconnect agreement for billing, SLA management,']
  counterparty_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the domestic or international operator that is the counterparty to this agreement. Links to the carrier/partner master record in the interconnect domain.']
  iot_tariff_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Inter-Operator Tariff (IOT) rate table applicable to this agreement. The IOT table defines per-unit wholesale rates for voice (per minute), SMS (per message), and data (per MB/GB) exchanged between operators.']
  mvno_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.mvno_profile. Business justification: MVNO wholesale access is governed by a carrier agreement between the host MNO and the MVNO. Linking carrier_agreement to mvno_profile enables MVNO wholesale agreement tracking, capacity management, an']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Roaming agreements with MVNO/reseller partners (not traditional carriers) require partner_id link. Business process: MVNO roaming agreement lifecycle management, partner contract tracking, consolidate']
  qos_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.qos_profile. Business justification: Roaming and interconnect agreements specify QoS requirements (latency, jitter, packet loss) that must be enforced by network QoS profiles. Required for SLA compliance verification and service assuranc']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: International carrier interconnect and roaming agreements must be notified to or filed with national regulators in many jurisdictions. Compliance officers track the specific regulatory filing record a']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Roaming/interconnect agreements must comply with specific regulatory obligations (FCC Part 64, international settlement policies, GDPR). Tracks which obligation each agreement satisfies for audit trai']
  roaming_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.roaming_agreement. Business justification: A carrier_agreement of type roaming references a specific partner roaming_agreement for operational parameters (TAP exchange, NRTRDE, steering policy). Linking enables cross-domain agreement lifecycle']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.sla_contract. Business justification: Carrier agreements have formal SLA performance obligations (TAP transmission windows, settlement cycles). Linking to assurance.sla_contract enables SLA breach monitoring and penalty enforcement agains']
  sla_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Service Level Agreement (SLA) record governing quality of service commitments under this carrier agreement, including network availability, latency, and fault resolution targets.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive commercial name of the carrier agreement (e.g., \'Roaming IOT Agreement – Carrier X 2024\'). Used for display in BSS portals and settlement reports.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the bilateral or multilateral carrier agreement. Used in correspondence, TAP file headers, and inter-operator settlement documents. Maps to the contract reference in Amdocs Revenue Management and Netcracker OSS/BSS.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the carrier agreement. DRAFT = under negotiation; ACTIVE = commercially live and binding; SUSPENDED = temporarily halted by mutual or unilateral action; TERMINATED = permanently ended; EXPIRED = reached end date without renewal; PENDING_APPROVAL = awaiting regulatory or internal sign-off.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier agreement by commercial relationship type. IOT = Inter-Operator Tariff roaming; CAMEL = Customised Applications for Mobile networks Enhanced Logic; MVNO_HOSTING = Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) hosting arrangement; NATIONAL_ROAMING = domestic network sharing; WHOLESALE_BROADBAND = wholesale broadband resale; CAPACITY_RESALE = bandwidth/capacity resale; TRANSIT = transit traffic routing; PEERING = direct traffic exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IOT|CAMEL|MVNO_HOSTING|NATIONAL_ROAMING|WHOLESALE_BROADBAND|CAPACITY_RESALE|TRANSIT|PEERING — promote to reference product]']
  agreement_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the agreement document (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\'). Incremented when amendments are executed. Enables tracking of agreement evolution and ensures the correct rate tables are applied for each version period.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments executed against this agreement since its original signing. Provides a quick indicator of agreement complexity and negotiation history.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews upon reaching its effective_until_date without requiring a new signed instrument. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action.']
  covered_services varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of telecommunication services covered under this agreement (e.g., VOICE, SMS, DATA, VOLTE, VONR, MMS, CAMEL_GPRS). Governs which service types are eligible for IOT settlement and TAP file exchange. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VOICE|SMS|DATA|VOLTE|VONR|MMS|CAMEL_GPRS|USSD — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier agreement record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  data_protection_addendum_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a data protection addendum (DPA) is attached to this agreement, governing the processing of subscriber personal data exchanged between operators in compliance with GDPR or equivalent regulations.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to standard IOT rates when discount_type is PERCENTAGE or VOLUME_TIERED. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Null when discount_type is FLAT_RATE or NONE.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Type of discount structure applied to IOT rates under this agreement. FLAT_RATE = fixed per-unit reduction; VOLUME_TIERED = discount scales with traffic volume; PERCENTAGE = percentage reduction off standard IOT; NEGOTIATED_RATE = bespoke bilaterally agreed rate; NONE = standard IOT applies.']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving financial or operational disputes arising under this agreement. GSMA_MEDIATION refers to the GSMA Billing and Accounting Dispute Resolution process.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the carrier agreement becomes commercially binding and operationally active. Governs the start of IOT tariff applicability, TAP file exchange obligations, and NRTRDE reporting requirements.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the carrier agreement ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended agreements. When populated, triggers renewal workflow and settlement true-up processing in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  fraud_prevention_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement includes specific fraud prevention obligations such as NRTRDE, SIM box detection cooperation, or GSMA Fraud Information Gathering System (FIGS) participation.']
  governing_law_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern this agreement (e.g., GBR for English law, USA for US law). Relevant for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.']
  interconnect_agreement_ref varchar [note: 'External reference number or document identifier for the physical signed agreement document, as stored in the document management system or SAP S/4HANA contract repository. Enables traceability to the legal instrument.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was last formally reviewed by the commercial team for rate competitiveness, volume performance, and compliance. Supports the agreement lifecycle management process.']
  min_volume_commitment_units decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum traffic volume committed by one or both parties under this agreement, expressed in the unit defined by min_volume_unit (e.g., minutes, MB, messages). Triggers true-up billing if actual volume falls below this threshold.']
  min_volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum volume commitment (min_volume_commitment_units). MINUTES = voice traffic; MEGABYTES/GIGABYTES = data traffic; MESSAGES = SMS/MMS; ERLANGS = traffic intensity for capacity agreements.']
  network_generation varchar [note: 'Radio access network generation(s) covered by this agreement. 2G = GSM/GPRS/EDGE; 3G = UMTS/HSPA; 4G_LTE = Long-Term Evolution (LTE); 5G_NR = 5G New Radio (NR); ALL = all generations. Determines applicable IOT tariff tables and NRTRDE reporting scope.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of this agreement. Triggers workflow notifications to the commercial owner and finance team in advance of the review.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days of advance written notice required by either party to terminate the agreement. Governs the termination workflow in contract lifecycle management.']
  nrtrde_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement requires Near Real-Time Roaming Data Exchange (NRTRDE) reporting per GSMA PRD FS.11. NRTRDE enables fraud detection by providing near-real-time CDR visibility to the home operator.']
  nrtrde_transmission_window_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours within which NRTRDE files must be transmitted to the home operator after the roaming CDR is generated. Standard GSMA requirement is 4 hours; some bilateral agreements specify tighter windows.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each automatic renewal cycle when auto_renewal_flag is True. Null when auto-renewal is not applicable.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross revenue shared with the counterparty operator under MVNO hosting, content, or wholesale broadband agreements. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 20.00 = 20%). Null for pure IOT/TAP agreements.']
  roaming_direction varchar [note: 'Direction of roaming traffic covered by this agreement. INBOUND = foreign subscribers roaming on Telecommunication\'s network; OUTBOUND = Telecommunication\'s subscribers roaming on the counterparty\'s network; BILATERAL = both directions.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial settlement under this agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Governs currency conversion in Amdocs Revenue Management inter-operator billing.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which financial settlement is performed between the parties under this agreement. Governs the billing cycle in Amdocs Revenue Management inter-operator invoicing.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which all parties executed the agreement. May precede the effective_from_date during a transition or notice period. Used for legal and audit trail purposes.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this agreement record was sourced. AMDOCS = Amdocs Revenue Management; NETCRACKER = Netcracker OSS/BSS; SALESFORCE = Salesforce CRM; SAP = SAP S/4HANA; MANUAL = manually entered.']
  tap_file_exchange_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement mandates Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) file exchange for roaming CDR settlement. True = TAP files must be exchanged per GSMA PRD TD.57 specifications.']
  tap_version varchar [note: 'Version of the GSMA Transferred Account Procedure (TAP) standard used for CDR file exchange under this agreement. Governs file format and field mapping in the TAP processing pipeline.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which the agreement was terminated, if applicable. Distinct from effective_until_date which is the planned end; this captures early or unilateral termination events.']
  true_up_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which minimum volume commitment true-up calculations are performed. If actual traffic falls below min_volume_commitment_units within the period, a shortfall charge is raised in Amdocs Revenue Management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier agreement record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for change tracking and incremental ETL processing.']

  Note: 'Master record of bilateral or multilateral commercial agreements between Telecommunication and domestic/international operators covering roaming, wholesale, and MVNO relationships. Captures agreement type (IOT, CAMEL, LTE, 5G NR, MVNO hosting, national roaming, wholesale broadband, capacity resale), counterparty carrier reference, effective dates, covered services (voice, SMS, data, VoLTE, VoNR), discount structures, TAP file exchange obligations, NRTRDE requirements, minimum volume commitments and true-up terms, revenue share terms, SLA references, and agreement status. SSOT for all inter-operator commercial relationship governance.'
}

Table interconnect.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each carrier record in the interconnect carrier master registry. Primary key for all interconnect domain relationships.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner. Business justification: Many interconnect carriers are also business partners (MVNOs, wholesale customers, resellers). Business process: Unified partner relationship management, consolidated billing across interconnect and p']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or trading name of the carrier, transit operator, MVNO, or wholesale partner as registered with the relevant regulatory authority. Used as the primary human-readable identifier across all interconnect domain products.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier relationship. Active indicates live traffic exchange; suspended indicates temporary halt pending dispute or non-payment; terminated indicates the relationship has ended; pending indicates onboarding in progress; inactive indicates dormant with no current traffic.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier by network ownership and service role: MNO (Mobile Network Operator), MVNO (Mobile Virtual Network Operator), transit (transit/wholesale carrier), IXC (Interexchange Carrier), LEC (Local Exchange Carrier), ISP (Internet Service Provider). Drives applicable tariff tables, settlement rules, and regulatory reporting.']
  clearinghouse_carrier_code varchar [note: 'Carrier identifier assigned by the clearinghouse (e.g., Syniverse or BICS carrier code) used for TAP file routing, NRTRDE submission, and financial settlement matching within the clearinghouse platform.']
  clearinghouse_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial and data clearinghouse through which TAP file exchange and inter-operator financial settlement is processed for this carrier (e.g., HSCSD, Syniverse, BICS). Required for TAP file routing and settlement reconciliation.']
  commercial_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the carrier\'s commercial or wholesale account management team. Used for agreement negotiations, invoice disputes, and settlement correspondence.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the carrier\'s primary country of registration and network operation. Used for geographic segmentation, IOT tariff lookup, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier record was first created in the interconnect carrier master registry. Audit trail field conforming to ISO 8601 extended format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding settlement balance (in settlement currency) permitted for this carrier before traffic suspension or escalation is triggered. Used for wholesale credit risk management and revenue assurance controls.']
