  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the privacy request was received by the organization. Marks the start of the regulatory SLA clock.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of data subject rights requests received from customers under GDPR (right to access, erasure, portability, rectification, restriction) and CCPA (right to know, delete, opt-out). Captures request type, submission channel, submission timestamp, identity verification status, assigned handler, processing deadline (regulatory SLA), fulfillment status, and closure timestamp. Tracks end-to-end DSR (Data Subject Request) lifecycle.'
}

Table compliance.spectrum_license {
  spectrum_license_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the spectrum license record. Primary key for the spectrum license entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Spectrum licenses require periodic compliance reviews and utilization audits mandated by the granting authority. These compliance review engagements are tracked as audit records. Linking spectrum_lice']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Spectrum licenses are held by legal entities. Enterprise/wholesale operators need to track which corporate entity holds each license for regulatory compliance, asset management, and transfer-of-contro']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: A spectrum license is obtained, renewed, or modified through a regulatory filing submitted to the granting authority (FCC, Ofcom, etc.). The spectrum license is the outcome/grant resulting from the fi']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Spectrum licenses are granted under regulatory obligations that define usage conditions, utilization requirements, and interference protection rules. This FK links licenses to the regulatory obligatio']
  acquisition_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost paid by the operator to acquire this spectrum license, including auction bids, transfer fees, and transaction costs. Expressed in the currency specified by the acquisition cost currency code.']
  acquisition_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'The date on which the operator acquired ownership or rights to this spectrum license. May differ from issue date if acquired through secondary market.']
  acquisition_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the operator acquired this spectrum license. Indicates whether it was obtained through auction, direct assignment, purchase, or other means.']
  annual_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The annual regulatory fee charged by the granting authority for holding this spectrum license. Expressed in the currency specified by the fee currency code.']
  bandwidth_mhz decimal(10,3) [note: 'The total bandwidth allocated to this license in megahertz. Calculated as the difference between upper and lower frequency bounds.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the license against regulatory conditions and utilization requirements. Indicates whether the licensee is meeting all obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this spectrum license record was first created in the system. Represents the initial data capture event.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the license becomes operationally valid and the licensee may begin using the spectrum. May differ from issue date due to administrative or technical conditions.']
  enforcement_action_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of any enforcement actions taken by the regulatory authority, including the nature of the violation and remediation requirements. Null if no enforcement action exists.']
  enforcement_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any regulatory enforcement action (warning, fine, suspension) has been taken against this license. True if enforcement action exists, False otherwise.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the spectrum license expires and spectrum rights revert to the regulatory authority unless renewed.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual license fee (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  frequency_band varchar [note: 'The radio frequency band allocated to this license, expressed in standard band notation (e.g., 600 MHz, 3.5 GHz, 28 GHz). Identifies the spectrum range for transmission.']
  granting_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory body or national authority that issued this spectrum license (e.g., FCC, Ofcom, NTIA, BEREC member regulator).']
  granting_authority_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the granting authority operates.']
  interference_protection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this license includes regulatory protection from interference by other spectrum users. True if protected, False if shared or unlicensed spectrum.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the spectrum license was officially granted by the regulatory authority. Marks the beginning of the license term.']
  last_compliance_review_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent regulatory compliance review or audit for this license. Used to track periodic compliance verification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this spectrum license record was last updated in the system. Tracks the most recent change to any attribute.']
  license_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the spectrum license by service type. Determines the permitted use case and operational constraints.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'The official license number or call sign assigned by the regulatory authority. This is the externally-known unique identifier for the spectrum license.']
  license_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the spectrum license. Indicates whether the license is operational, under review, or terminated.']
  license_term_years varchar [note: 'The duration of the license grant in years. Defines the period for which the spectrum rights are valid before renewal is required.']
  lower_frequency_mhz decimal(10,3) [note: 'The lower bound of the licensed frequency range in megahertz. Defines the start of the spectrum allocation.']
  minimum_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum spectrum utilization percentage required by the regulatory authority to maintain the license in good standing. Failure to meet this threshold may result in penalties or revocation.']
  next_compliance_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next regulatory compliance review or audit. Null if no review is scheduled.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special conditions, or internal notes related to this spectrum license.']
  renewal_application_date date [note: 'The date on which the licensee submitted the renewal application to the regulatory authority. Null if no renewal has been requested.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the license renewal process. Tracks whether renewal is required, applied for, or completed.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The internal department or business unit responsible for managing this spectrum license (e.g., Network Engineering, Regulatory Affairs, Spectrum Management).']
  shared_spectrum_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this license operates in a shared spectrum environment (e.g., CBRS, LSA) where multiple operators may use the same frequencies under coordination rules. True if shared, False if exclusive.']
  technology_standard varchar [note: 'The radio access technology or standard authorized for use under this license (e.g., LTE, 5G NR, Wi-Fi 6, satellite). May be technology-neutral or technology-specific depending on regulatory framework.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this license may be transferred or sold to another operator subject to regulatory approval. True if transferable, False if non-transferable.']
  upper_frequency_mhz decimal(10,3) [note: 'The upper bound of the licensed frequency range in megahertz. Defines the end of the spectrum allocation.']
  usage_conditions varchar [note: 'Textual description of the regulatory conditions, restrictions, and obligations attached to this license. May include build-out requirements, interference limits, and service quality mandates.']

  Note: 'Master record of spectrum licenses held by the telecommunications operator including periodic utilization reporting and regulatory compliance tracking. Captures frequency band, bandwidth (MHz), geographic coverage area, license category (mobile broadband, fixed wireless, satellite, experimental, CBRS/shared), license term, annual fees, renewal status, usage conditions, and granting authority (FCC, Ofcom, NTIA, national regulators). Includes all usage reports as line items: reporting period, measured utilization percentage, interference incidents, compliance status against license conditions, submission date, and regulatory authority acknowledgment. SSOT for spectrum asset compliance, license management, utilization tracking, and regulatory efficiency reporting.'
}

Table compliance.mnp_compliance {
  mnp_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for mnp_compliance']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account.   customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: MNP compliance records include account_number_verified_flag and billing_address_verified_flag, confirming account-level verification is required during porting authorization. Linking to customer_accou']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Enterprise customers port large number blocks under governing enterprise contracts that define porting SLAs, authorization procedures, and penalty terms. Linking mnp_compliance to enterprise_contract ']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: MNP (Mobile Number Portability) compliance records may be associated with specific regulatory filings submitted to the porting authority or national regulator — for example, periodic MNP compliance re']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: MNP compliance records fulfill regulatory obligations that define porting SLAs, rejection criteria, and notification requirements. This FK links MNP records to the specific regulatory obligation that ']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the subscriber associated with the ported number. Links to the customer master record.']

  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to detailed audit trail records for this porting transaction, supporting regulatory audit requirements.']
  authorization_method varchar [note: 'Method by which customer authorization was obtained (electronic signature, verbal recording, written Letter of Authorization, online consent, in-store verification).']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer authorization was obtained and verified.']
  billing_address_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s billing address was verified as part of the porting authorization process.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from compliance officers regarding special circumstances, exceptions, or regulatory considerations for this porting transaction.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal compliance review for this porting transaction, particularly for SLA breaches or rejections.']
  compliance_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the compliance review was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this compliance record was first created in the system.']
  customer_authorization_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer authorization for the port was verified according to regulatory requirements (to prevent unauthorized porting/slamming).']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when this compliance record is eligible for deletion according to regulatory data retention policies (typically 2-7 years depending on jurisdiction).']
  donor_operator_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the telecommunications operator from which the number is being ported (the losing carrier).']
  donor_operator_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the donor telecommunications operator.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of fraud detection checks performed on this porting request to prevent unauthorized number hijacking.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical fraud risk score assigned to this porting transaction by fraud detection systems. Higher scores indicate higher risk.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this compliance record was last updated.']
  msisdn varchar [note: 'The mobile phone number being ported. This is the subscriber\'s telephone number in international format.']
  number_count varchar [note: 'Total count of numbers included in this porting transaction. Typically 1 for simple ports, multiple for complex or corporate ports.']
  number_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending number in a range port (for complex or corporate porting involving multiple consecutive numbers). Null for simple single-number ports.']
  number_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting number in a range port (for complex or corporate porting involving multiple consecutive numbers). Null for simple single-number ports.']
  porting_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the number porting was successfully completed and the number became active on the recipient operator\'s network.']
  porting_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is a port-in (number coming to this operator) or port-out (number leaving this operator) transaction.']
  porting_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique regulatory reference number assigned to the porting transaction by the central porting authority or clearinghouse. Used for inter-operator coordination and regulatory tracking.']
  porting_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the porting request was officially submitted to the central porting authority or clearinghouse.']
  porting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the porting transaction in the regulatory compliance lifecycle.']
  porting_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the porting transaction complexity. Simple (single number), complex (multiple numbers), or corporate (enterprise account porting).']
  recipient_operator_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the telecommunications operator to which the number is being ported (the gaining carrier).']
  recipient_operator_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the recipient telecommunications operator.']
  regulatory_authority_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of notification to the regulatory authority regarding this porting transaction. Some jurisdictions require operators to report porting metrics to regulators.']
  regulatory_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the regulatory authority was notified of this porting transaction or compliance event.']
  regulatory_sla_deadline_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which the porting must be completed to comply with FCC, BEREC, or local regulatory authority SLA requirements (typically 1 business day in the US, varies by jurisdiction).']
  rejection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the porting request was rejected by the donor operator or central porting authority.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for porting rejection (e.g., account mismatch, outstanding balance, invalid authorization).']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of why the porting request was rejected, providing context beyond the standardized code.']
  sla_breach_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of hours by which the porting transaction exceeded the regulatory SLA deadline. Null if no breach occurred.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the porting transaction breached the regulatory SLA deadline. True if the completion timestamp exceeded the regulatory deadline.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking Mobile Number Portability (MNP) regulatory compliance for each port-in and port-out transaction. Captures porting request reference, donor/recipient operator, MSISDN, porting completion timestamp, regulatory SLA deadline, SLA breach indicator, rejection reason (if applicable), and regulatory authority notification status. Supports FCC MNP compliance reporting and BEREC number portability obligations.'
}

  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Enterprise contracts include audit_rights_clause provisions. Compliance teams run contract-scoped audits (government framework, wholesale agreement, ISO certification audits). Linking audit to enterpr']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the regulatory obligation that mandated or triggered this audit engagement.']

  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the audit fieldwork or on-site activities commenced.']

  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit engagement. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SCHEDULED|IN_PROGRESS|FIELDWORK_COMPLETE|UNDER_REVIEW|COMPLETED|CANCELLED|DEFERRED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit engagement type. Indicates the regulatory framework, standard, or internal policy being audited. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO_27001_SURVEILLANCE|ISO_27001_RECERTIFICATION|FCC_INSPECTION|GDPR_SUPERVISORY_AUTHORITY_AUDIT|SOC_2_EXAMINATION|PCI_DSS_ASSESSMENT|INTERNAL_REVIEW|3GPP_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT|GSMA_SAS_AUDIT|ETSI_STANDARDS_AUDIT|CALEA_LAWFUL_INTERCEPT_AUDIT|CPNI_PROTECTION_AUDIT|UNIVERSAL_SERVICE_FUND_AUDIT|SPECTRUM_LICENSE_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT|MNP_REGULATORY_AUDIT|OFCOM_COMPLIANCE_REVIEW|BEREC_REGULATORY_AUDIT — promote to reference product]']

  compliance_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of the compliance risk level associated with the audit scope and findings. Indicates potential regulatory, financial, or reputational impact.']
  corrective_action_deadline date [note: 'Deadline date by which corrective actions for identified nonconformities must be completed and submitted for verification.']
  corrective_actions_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the auditing body verified that all corrective actions have been satisfactorily implemented.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the audit engagement, including auditor fees, travel expenses, and related costs.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the audit cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance audit record was first created in the system.']
  external_counsel_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external legal counsel firm engaged to support the audit, if applicable.']
  external_counsel_involved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external legal counsel was engaged to support the audit process. True if external counsel was involved, False otherwise.']
  internal_audit_coordinator_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the internal audit coordinator for audit logistics and communication.']
  internal_audit_coordinator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the internal employee responsible for coordinating the audit activities and serving as the primary liaison with the auditing body.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance audit record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  major_nonconformities_count varchar [note: 'Number of major nonconformities identified during the audit. Major nonconformities represent significant failures to meet compliance requirements.']
  minor_nonconformities_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor nonconformities identified during the audit. Minor nonconformities represent less critical deviations from compliance requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context related to the audit engagement and certification lifecycle.']
  observations_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations or opportunities for improvement noted during the audit that do not constitute nonconformities.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Overall result or conclusion of the audit engagement. Indicates whether the organization met the compliance requirements under review.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period under audit review. Defines the conclusion of the timeframe for which compliance is being assessed.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period under audit review. Defines the beginning of the timeframe for which compliance is being assessed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the audit engagement based on regulatory importance, risk exposure, and business impact.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the audit engagement by the auditing body or internal audit function.']
  report_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document management system reference or file path to the formal audit report.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date when the formal audit report was issued to the organization.']
  responsible_executive_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the responsible executive officer for compliance communications.']
  responsible_executive_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the executive officer responsible for compliance oversight and signatory authority for the audit and certification.']
  responsible_executive_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the responsible executive officer (e.g., Chief Compliance Officer, Chief Information Security Officer, Data Protection Officer).']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned date for the audit fieldwork or on-site activities to conclude.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the audit fieldwork or on-site activities to commence.']
  team_size varchar [note: 'Total number of auditors and technical experts assigned to the audit engagement.']

  Note: 'Master record of compliance audit engagements and resulting certifications held by the telecommunications operator. For audits: captures audit type (ISO 27001 surveillance/recertification, FCC inspection, GDPR supervisory authority audit, SOC 2 examination, PCI-DSS assessment, internal review), auditing body, scope, period, lead auditor, status, and overall outcome. For certifications: captures certification type (ISO/IEC 27001, SOC 2 Type II, PCI-DSS, FCC CPNI annual, GSMA SAS), issuing body, certification scope, issue date, expiry date, renewal status, certificate reference number, and responsible executive signatory. SSOT for the complete audit-to-certification lifecycle including certification renewal tracking.'
}

Table compliance.data_breach_incident {
  data_breach_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the data breach incident record. Primary key for the data breach incident entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Data breach incidents trigger compliance audits to investigate root cause, assess control failures, and verify remediation. This FK links breach incidents to the audits that investigated them. FK will']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.corporate_account. Business justification: Data breaches affecting enterprise customers require entity-specific notification, regulatory reporting (GDPR, state breach laws), and remediation tracking. Critical for enterprise account management ']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: Data breaches in telecom frequently originate in or affect specific managed services (managed security, managed SD-WAN, managed cloud connectivity). Linking breach incidents to managed_service enables']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Data breach incidents trigger regulatory obligations for notification (e.g., GDPR 72-hour notification, CCPA breach notification). This FK links breach incidents to the specific regulatory obligation ']
  affected_data_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of personal data categories affected by the breach (e.g., name, email, phone, address, payment card, CPNI, location data, account credentials).']
  affected_systems varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of systems, applications, or infrastructure components affected by the breach (e.g., CRM, billing system, customer portal, database server).']
  breach_severity_level varchar [note: 'Assessed severity level of the data breach incident based on data sensitivity, volume, and potential harm (critical, high, medium, low).']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the data breach incident (detected, under investigation, contained, notification in progress, closed, escalated).']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the data breach incident by attack vector or cause (unauthorized access, accidental disclosure, ransomware, insider threat, third-party breach, lost device).']
  closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data breach incident was formally closed after all remediation, notification, and regulatory obligations were completed.']
  confirmed_affected_individuals_count varchar [note: 'Confirmed number of individuals whose personal data was compromised, based on completed forensic investigation.']
  containment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data breach incident was successfully contained and the vulnerability or exposure was closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this data breach incident record was first created in the system.']
  credit_monitoring_offered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credit monitoring or identity protection services were offered to affected individuals as part of breach remediation.']
  data_exfiltration_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether forensic investigation confirmed that personal data was actually exfiltrated or accessed by unauthorized parties (true) or only exposed without confirmed access (false).']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the data breach incident was first detected by internal monitoring systems, staff, or external notification.']
  encryption_in_place boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affected personal data was encrypted at the time of the breach, which may reduce notification obligations under certain regulations.']
  estimated_affected_individuals_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of individuals whose personal data was compromised in the breach incident.']
  estimated_financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total financial impact of the breach including investigation costs, notification costs, remediation costs, regulatory fines, and potential litigation costs.']
  external_forensics_firm_engaged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an external cybersecurity or forensics firm was engaged to investigate the breach.']
  external_forensics_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external cybersecurity or forensics firm engaged to investigate the breach.']
  financial_impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated financial impact amount.']
  incident_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the data breach incident for tracking and regulatory reporting purposes.']
  individual_notification_completion_date date [note: 'Date when direct notification to all affected individuals was completed.']
  individual_notification_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to notify affected individuals (email, postal mail, SMS, phone, website notice, substitute notice).']
  individual_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach meets the threshold for mandatory direct notification to affected individuals under applicable data protection regulations.']
  individual_notification_start_date date [note: 'Date when direct notification to affected individuals began (via email, postal mail, or other channels).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this data breach incident record was last modified or updated.']
  law_enforcement_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach requires notification to law enforcement agencies based on criminal activity or regulatory requirements.']
  legal_counsel_engaged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external legal counsel was engaged to advise on breach notification obligations and regulatory compliance.']
  legal_counsel_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external legal counsel firm engaged to advise on the breach response.']
  litigation_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether civil litigation or class action lawsuits were initiated by affected individuals or consumer advocacy groups as a result of the breach.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context regarding the data breach incident, investigation, or response activities.']
  occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time when the data breach incident actually occurred or began, based on forensic investigation.']
  public_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date when the breach was publicly disclosed through media notification, press release, or regulatory public notice.']
  public_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach requires public disclosure or media notification due to the number of affected individuals or regulatory requirements.']
  regulatory_fine_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of regulatory fines or penalties imposed by supervisory authorities as a result of the breach.']
  regulatory_fine_imposed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory fine or penalty was imposed by a supervisory authority as a result of the breach.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach meets the threshold for mandatory notification to supervisory authorities under applicable data protection regulations.']
  remediation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all remediation actions were completed and verified.']
  remediation_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the remediation plan implemented to address the breach, prevent recurrence, and mitigate harm to affected individuals.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary root cause category identified through incident investigation (human error, system vulnerability, malicious attack, third-party failure, physical security breach, process failure).']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause identified through forensic investigation and incident analysis.']
  supervisory_authority_acknowledgment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or acknowledgment identifier provided by the supervisory authority upon receipt of the breach notification.']
  supervisory_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supervisory authority or regulatory body to which the breach was notified (e.g., ICO, CNIL, FCC, state attorney general).']
  supervisory_authority_notification_deadline timestamp [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the breach must be notified to the supervisory authority (e.g., GDPR 72-hour deadline from detection, FCC 30-day deadline).']
  supervisory_authority_notification_on_time boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supervisory authority notification was submitted within the regulatory deadline (true) or was late (false).']
  supervisory_authority_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the breach notification was submitted to the supervisory authority.']

  Note: 'Master record of personal data breach incidents covering the complete lifecycle from detection through regulatory notification, individual notification, and closure. Captures breach type (unauthorized access, accidental disclosure, ransomware, insider threat, third-party breach), affected data categories, estimated affected individual count, detection and containment timestamps, root cause, and remediation plan. Includes all notification records as line items: notification type (supervisory authority, affected individuals, law enforcement), recipient authority or channel, notification timestamp, content summary, regulatory deadline (GDPR 72-hour, CCPA \'without unreasonable delay\', FCC 30-day), on-time indicator, and acknowledgment reference. SSOT for complete data breach lifecycle management including mandatory notification compliance tracking.'
}

Table enterprise.corporate_account {
  corporate_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the corporate account. Primary key for the enterprise B2B customer entity.']
  enterprise_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.segment. Business justification: Corporate accounts must be classified by enterprise segment for pricing, SLA, and account management purposes. The segment table defines the segmentation taxonomy. Currently corporate_account has acco']
  parent_account_corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent corporate account if this account is part of a multi-entity corporate hierarchy or subsidiary structure.']
  account_activation_date date [note: 'Date when the corporate account was first activated and became operational for service delivery.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the corporate account used in customer communications, invoices, and contracts.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corporate account indicating whether it is actively transacting, temporarily suspended, closed, or awaiting activation.']
  annual_revenue_range varchar [note: 'Estimated annual revenue range of the corporate entity, used for account classification and opportunity sizing.']
  auto_pay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this corporate account to settle invoices on due date.']
  average_revenue_per_month decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average monthly recurring revenue generated by this corporate account, calculated as total revenue divided by number of months active.']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this corporate account is billed and invoiced (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  business_registration_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued business registration number, tax identification number, or company registration number used for legal and tax purposes.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated risk score (0-100) indicating the likelihood of this corporate account terminating services, used for proactive retention efforts.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current master service agreement or contract term expires, triggering renewal or termination decision.']
  contract_renewal_date date [note: 'Next scheduled date for contract renewal negotiation or automatic renewal, used for proactive account management and retention planning.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the master service agreement or primary contract with this corporate account became effective and services commenced.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this corporate account record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corporate account record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure or outstanding balance amount authorized for this corporate account before payment is required.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit worthiness rating assigned to the corporate entity based on financial assessment, payment history, and risk evaluation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|not_rated — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  customer_lifetime_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated total revenue value expected from this corporate account over the entire relationship lifetime, used for strategic account prioritization.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the corporate entity operates, if different from legal entity name.']
  employee_count_range varchar [note: 'Size classification of the corporate entity based on number of employees, used for segmentation and solution sizing.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the corporate headquarters street address including building number and street name.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the corporate headquarters address for suite, floor, or additional location details.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the corporate headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the corporate headquarters is located (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the corporate headquarters address.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the corporate headquarters is located.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'Primary industry sector or vertical market in which the corporate customer operates, used for segmentation and targeted offerings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial_services|healthcare|manufacturing|retail|government|education|technology|energy|transportation|hospitality|media|professional_services|construction|agriculture|other — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this corporate account record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corporate account record was most recently updated or modified.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received from this corporate account.']
  last_service_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent service order placed by this corporate account, used for activity tracking and engagement analysis.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official registered legal name of the corporate entity as it appears on government registration documents and contracts.']
  net_promoter_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (-100 to +100) measuring the corporate account\'s likelihood to recommend services to other businesses.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allowed for payment after invoice date per the negotiated payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).']
  preferred_language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the preferred language for communications, documentation, and support (e.g., en, es, fr).']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Default payment method preferred by the corporate account for invoice settlement.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact for account-related communications, notifications, and escalations.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person at the corporate entity authorized to make decisions and receive communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the designated business contact at the corporate account.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel through which this corporate account was acquired and is managed.']

  Note: 'Master record for an enterprise/B2B corporate account representing a legal business entity (corporation, government agency, SME) contracting with the telco. Captures legal entity name, registration number, industry vertical, account tier (strategic, key, standard), assigned account manager, credit rating, CLTV, ARPU, contract start/end dates, billing currency, preferred language, account status, and churn risk indicators. SSOT for enterprise customer identity within the B2B segment — all enterprise domain entities reference this as the root account.'
}

Table enterprise.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the account hierarchy relationship record.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Account hierarchies drive consolidated billing — the hierarchy node determines which billing account receives consolidated invoices. account_hierarchy has billing_consolidation_flag confirming this bu']
  child_account_corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child account in the corporate hierarchy structure. References the subordinate organizational entity in the relationship.']
  primary_corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent account in the corporate hierarchy structure. References the superior organizational entity in the relationship.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate shared costs or discounts across the parent-child relationship. Defines the business rule for distributing enterprise-level charges or credits.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state for establishing or modifying this hierarchy relationship. Ensures proper governance and authorization for corporate structure changes.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship was formally approved by authorized personnel for activation in billing and reporting systems.']
  billing_consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether charges from the child account should be rolled up and consolidated into the parent account invoice for unified enterprise billing.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the business unit or division responsible for managing this hierarchy relationship within the enterprise organizational structure.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center identifier associated with this hierarchy relationship for general ledger allocation and departmental chargeback purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship record was first created in the system for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the parent-child account relationship ceases to be active. Null value indicates an open-ended relationship with no planned termination.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the parent-child account relationship becomes active and valid for billing consolidation and organizational reporting purposes.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or reference number for this hierarchy relationship used for cross-system reconciliation and integration with upstream BSS/OSS platforms.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the primary geographic region or territory associated with this hierarchy relationship for regional sales and operational reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth level of the child account within the overall corporate hierarchy tree structure. Level 1 represents top-level holding companies, with increasing numbers indicating deeper nesting.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path representation of the complete lineage from root to current node in the account hierarchy tree. Typically formatted as slash-delimited account identifiers for efficient tree traversal queries.']
  is_primary_relationship boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary or default parent relationship for the child account in scenarios where an account may belong to multiple hierarchies.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hierarchy relationship record was most recently updated or modified for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal entity under which this hierarchy relationship operates for regulatory compliance and statutory reporting requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or business rationale related to this parent-child account relationship.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership or control that the parent account holds over the child account entity. Used for proportional billing allocation and financial reporting in complex corporate structures.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial profit center identifier associated with this hierarchy relationship for revenue attribution and profitability analysis in enterprise reporting.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the parent-child account relationship. Indicates whether the hierarchical linkage is currently operational for billing consolidation and reporting.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational relationship between parent and child accounts. Defines the nature of the corporate structure linkage for governance and billing purposes.']
  reporting_consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether usage, revenue, and performance metrics from the child account should be aggregated into parent-level enterprise reporting and analytics.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the upstream operational system that originated or maintains this hierarchy relationship record for data lineage and reconciliation purposes.']

  Note: 'Defines parent-child relationships within a corporate account structure, supporting multi-subsidiary, holding company, and government department hierarchies. Captures parent account, child account, hierarchy level, relationship type (subsidiary, division, branch, department), effective dates, and consolidation flags for billing rollup and reporting. Enables enterprise-wide spend visibility and consolidated invoicing across complex B2B organizational structures.'
}

Table enterprise.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the enterprise site. Primary key.']

  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent corporate account that owns this site.']
  ont_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ont_asset. Business justification: Fiber-to-premises enterprise service delivery: the ONT installed at an enterprise site is the terminating device for fiber connectivity. Linking enterprise_site to ont_asset enables fault isolation, S']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: Enterprise sites have SLA tiers governing service delivery commitments at that location. Site-level SLA management and field operations require knowing which SLA profile applies. The existing sla_tier']
  access_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for field technicians to access the site, including security procedures, parking, and entry points.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the site became operationally active and began receiving billable services.']
  bandwidth_requirement_mbps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Required bandwidth capacity for the site measured in megabits per second, used for circuit provisioning and SD-WAN (Software-Defined Wide Area Network) design.']
  building_type varchar [note: 'Physical building classification affecting infrastructure deployment and access procedures.']
  business_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the site, used for scheduling maintenance and support activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the site was deactivated or services were disconnected.']
  disaster_recovery_tier varchar [note: 'Disaster recovery classification indicating backup and failover requirements for business continuity planning.']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Specific floor or level within the building where equipment is located, used for field dispatch instructions.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when services were first installed and activated at this site.']
  is_24x7_operation boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the site operates continuously around the clock, affecting maintenance window planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site record was last updated, used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes capturing additional site-specific information, special requirements, or operational considerations.']
  number_of_users varchar [note: 'Total count of end users or employees at this site, used for capacity planning and service dimensioning.']
  primary_service_type varchar [note: 'Primary telecommunications service delivered to this site (SD-WAN: Software-Defined Wide Area Network, MPLS: Multiprotocol Label Switching, IoT: Internet of Things).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business criticality classification of the site determining incident response priority and resource allocation.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the site, used in service orders and field dispatch.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the site (e.g., \'Headquarters Building A\', \'Chicago Branch Office\').']
  site_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the site indicating its operational state.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the site based on its business function and infrastructure requirements.']
  sla_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic service zone classification used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) response time calculations and field dispatch routing.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the site location, used for scheduling maintenance windows and SLA (Service Level Agreement) calculations.']

  Note: 'Physical or logical site/location belonging to a corporate account where services are delivered — office buildings, data centers, branch offices, government facilities, or remote sites. Captures site name, address, site type, geographic coordinates, access instructions, site contact, CPE inventory reference, number of users, bandwidth requirements, and site status. Used for SD-WAN/MPLS topology planning, field dispatch, and SLA zone assignment.'
}
