Service fulfillment teams need SIM ICCID/IMSI for device onboarding and conn']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical site or facility where the managed service is delivered and CPE is installed.']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal SLA contract document that governs performance commitments, availability targets, and remediation terms for this managed service.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: Managed services are governed by a specific SLA profile defining performance targets. Managed service SLA monitoring, breach detection, and credit processing require this direct link. The existing sla']
  spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.spec. Business justification: Managed services are delivered against technical specifications (bandwidth, QoS, latency thresholds). Linking spec_id enables SLA validation against spec-defined parameters and service delivery verifi']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the managed service instance was successfully activated and became operational for the customer.']
  bgp_asn varchar [note: 'Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) Autonomous System Number (ASN) assigned to the customer for BGP peering on this managed service.']
  change_window varchar [note: 'Approved maintenance window schedule for planned changes, upgrades, and configuration updates to minimize business impact.']
  circuit_reference varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned circuit identifier for the underlying transport connection (MPLS VPN, DIA, or private line) supporting this managed service.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the service contract term. Null for evergreen or month-to-month agreements.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the service contract term, marking the beginning of billing and SLA obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this managed service record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts associated with this managed service instance.']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether end-to-end encryption (IPsec, TLS, or proprietary) is enabled for data in transit over the managed service connection.']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol and version used to secure data transmission over the managed service connection.']
  ipv4_subnet varchar [note: 'IPv4 subnet allocated to the customer for this managed service instance, in CIDR notation.']
  ipv6_subnet varchar [note: 'IPv6 subnet allocated to the customer for this managed service instance, in CIDR notation.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent planned maintenance activity performed on this managed service instance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this managed service record was last updated, reflecting configuration changes, status transitions, or attribute modifications.']
  monthly_recurring_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly recurring charge for the managed service subscription, excluding usage-based fees and one-time charges.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled maintenance activity for firmware updates, configuration changes, or hardware refresh.']
  noc_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this managed service instance is actively monitored by the Network Operations Center (NOC) for performance, availability, and fault detection.']
  proactive_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proactive monitoring and alerting is enabled, allowing the NOC to detect and remediate issues before customer impact.']
  redundancy_type varchar [note: 'Type of redundancy configuration deployed for high availability: None, Active-Standby, Active-Active, or Geo-Redundant across multiple sites.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Next scheduled renewal date for the managed service contract, triggering renewal workflow and pricing review.']
  routing_protocol varchar [note: 'Dynamic routing protocol used between customer and provider networks: Border Gateway Protocol (BGP), Open Shortest Path First (OSPF), Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP), or Static routing.']
  service_instance_identifier varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the managed service instance, used in customer communications and service tickets.']
  service_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, escalation procedures, or customer-specific requirements related to this managed service.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the managed service instance in the fulfillment and operations workflow.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Service level tier defining the scope of management, support responsiveness, and feature set included in the managed service offering.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of managed service delivered: Software-Defined Wide Area Network (SD-WAN), Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) Virtual Private Network (VPN), Unified Communications as a Service (UCaaS), Internet of Things (IoT) Connectivity, Managed Security, Dedicated Internet Access (DIA), or Cloud Connect. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SD-WAN|MPLS VPN|UCaaS|IoT Connectivity|Managed Security|DIA|Cloud Connect — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vlan_tag varchar [note: 'Virtual Local Area Network (VLAN) identifier used for traffic segmentation and isolation on the managed service connection.']

  Note: 'Master record for a managed service instance delivered to a corporate account — SD-WAN, MPLS VPN, UCaaS, IoT connectivity, managed security, or dedicated internet access. Captures service type, tier, contracted bandwidth, QoS class, SLA tier reference, provisioning status, activation date, managed CPE references (linking to inventory domain), NOC monitoring flag, and assigned service delivery manager. Represents the operational service instance distinct from the product catalog definition.'
}

Table enterprise.enterprise_contract {
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Enterprise contracts frequently cover bundled services. Linking bundle_id enables bundle-level discount enforcement, minimum commitment tracking, and renewal management — core processes in enterprise ']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Enterprise contracts often reference master catalog items for pricing schedules, SLA terms, and product eligibility. Required for contract compliance audits, pricing governance, and product-to-contrac']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise customer account that holds this contract. Links to the corporate hierarchy and account management structure.']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_cycle. Business justification: Enterprise contracts specify custom billing cycles (monthly, quarterly, annual) as negotiated terms. enterprise_contract.billing_cycle_code is a denormalized plain-text attribute; replacing it with a ']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Enterprise contracts are negotiated against specific product offerings. Offering-level FK enables contract management systems to enforce offering-specific terms, pricing models, and eligibility rules ']
  parent_contract_enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or master contract when this contract is a subsidiary agreement, amendment, or task order. Enables contract hierarchy modeling.']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: Enterprise contracts are negotiated against specific price plans. Linking price_plan_id enables contract management systems to enforce pricing terms, minimum commitments, and early termination fees — ']
  promo_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.promo_offer. Business justification: Enterprise contracts incorporate promotional offers (discounted first year, free trial periods). Linking promo_offer_id enables promo redemption tracking, budget consumption monitoring, and audit comp']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master SLA document or SLA contract that defines performance commitments, measurement methods, and remedies for this enterprise contract.']
  agency_billing_code varchar [note: 'Government agency-specific billing or accounting code for invoice routing and payment processing. Required for government contract billing.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of contract amendments or modifications. Zero for original contract. Increments with each formal change.']
  audit_rights_clause varchar [note: 'Full text of audit rights provisions allowing customer or regulatory inspection of service delivery, billing, and compliance records.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiry unless explicitly terminated. Drives renewal notification and approval workflows.']
  ceiling_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total contract value over the contract term. Mandatory for government contracts (GSA Schedule, G-Cloud). Nullable for commercial agreements.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level for contract terms and commercial information. Determines access controls and disclosure restrictions.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract. Controls service provisioning, billing activation, and renewal workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|terminated|expired|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the category of enterprise agreement. Determines applicable terms, compliance requirements, and billing structures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this contract record was first created in the enterprise contract management system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract. Determines billing currency and foreign exchange handling.']
  data_sovereignty_obligation varchar [note: 'Contractual requirements for data residency, storage location, and cross-border data transfer restrictions. Critical for government and regulated industry contracts.']
  discount_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing discount schedule or rate card applicable to this contract. Links to product catalog and billing rules.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Agreed mechanism for resolving contractual disputes. Determines escalation path and legal procedures.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Secure storage location or document management system URL for the executed contract PDF and related legal documents.']
  early_termination_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty for contract termination before expiry date. Nullable if no early termination fee applies.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms become legally binding and services may be provisioned. Start of the contract period.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the contract term ends. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal or termination workflows.']
  foia_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contract terms and performance data are subject to FOIA disclosure requirements. Applies to US government contracts.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law for contract interpretation and dispute resolution. Critical for international agreements.']
  government_framework_type varchar [note: 'Specific government procurement framework or vehicle under which this contract was awarded. Null for non-government contracts.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified this contract record. Audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this contract record. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  minimum_commitment_arr decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual recurring revenue commitment. Alternative or complementary to MRR for annual billing cycles.']
  minimum_commitment_mrr decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monthly recurring revenue commitment by the enterprise customer. Used for revenue assurance and shortfall billing calculations.']
  mvno_host_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract is an MVNO host agreement where the telco provides wholesale network access to a virtual operator.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal notes, special terms, or operational instructions related to contract management. Not shared with customer.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date that payment is due. Standard commercial terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Impacts cash flow and collections.']
  penalty_clause_text varchar [note: 'Full text of contractual penalty clauses for service level breaches, early termination, or non-compliance. Legal reference for dispute resolution.']
  procurement_vehicle_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the government procurement vehicle or framework agreement. Required for government contract reporting and compliance.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the contract. Used in all customer communications, invoices, and legal documentation.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiry that renewal or termination notice must be provided. Contractual obligation for both parties.']
  security_clearance_required varchar [note: 'Level of security clearance required for personnel servicing this contract. Mandatory for government and defense contracts.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized signatory who executed the contract on behalf of the enterprise customer. Legal record for contract validity.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the signatory. Validates authority to bind the organization to contractual terms.']
  signature_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was formally executed by all parties. Legal record for contract formation and statute of limitations.']
  task_order_structure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract uses a task order or call-off structure. Common in government IDIQ (Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity) contracts.']
  wholesale_agreement_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of wholesale telecommunications agreement. Null for non-wholesale contracts.']

  Note: 'Master record for all commercial contracts between the telco and a corporate account. Covers all enterprise agreement types via a type discriminator: master service agreements (MSA), government frameworks (G-Cloud, Crown Commercial Service, GSA Schedule), MVNO host agreements, and wholesale contracts. Captures contract reference, type (commercial, government, wholesale, MVNO), effective/expiry dates, auto-renewal flag, minimum commitment (MRR/ARR), discount schedule reference, penalty clauses, governing law, status, signatory details. For government-type contracts: security clearance requirements, data sovereignty obligations, audit rights, FOIA compliance flag, contract ceiling value, task order structure, procurement vehicle, and agency-specific billing codes. SSOT for all enterprise contractual obligations regardless of customer segment or procurement vehicle.'
}

Table enterprise.sla_measurement {
  sla_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA measurement record. Primary key for the enterprise SLA measurement entity.']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.managed_service. Business justification: SLA measurements are calculated per managed service instance for contract compliance monitoring, credit entitlement calculation, and service performance reporting in enterprise telecommunications oper']
  performance_measurement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assurance.performance_measurement. Business justification: Enterprise SLA measurements derive from underlying network performance measurements (latency, jitter, packet loss, throughput). Data lineage for SLA reporting, dispute resolution, and credit calculati']
  sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise SLA contract under which this measurement was taken. Links to the master SLA contract defining targets and terms.']
  svc_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific enterprise managed service being measured (SD-WAN circuit, MPLS connection, unified communications service, IoT solution, etc.).']
  breach_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The total duration in minutes that the service was in breach of the SLA target during the measurement period. Null if no breach occurred. Used to calculate service credits and assess severity.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured value breached (failed to meet) the target value for this measurement period. True indicates a breach occurred; False indicates target was met or exceeded.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the breach severity based on the magnitude of deviation from target and business impact. Severity levels typically determine credit entitlement amounts and escalation procedures.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SLA measurement record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  credit_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit entitlement amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  credit_entitlement_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary credit amount (in contract currency) that the enterprise customer is entitled to receive as compensation for this SLA breach, calculated according to the credit schedule defined in the SLA contract.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enterprise customer was notified of this SLA measurement result, particularly for breach events. True if notification was sent; False otherwise.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer was notified of this SLA measurement result. Null if no notification was sent. Used for compliance with notification SLAs.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A quality score (0-100) indicating the reliability and completeness of the measurement data. Lower scores may indicate gaps in monitoring coverage, probe failures, or data collection issues that affect measurement accuracy.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enterprise customer has disputed this SLA measurement result. True if disputed; False otherwise. Disputed measurements may require investigation and resolution before credit processing.']
  dispute_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dispute resolution process for this measurement. Null if no dispute was raised. Tracks the lifecycle of customer challenges to SLA measurement accuracy.']
  exclusion_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any contractual exclusions were applied to this measurement (e.g., planned maintenance windows, force majeure events, customer-caused outages). True if exclusions were applied; False otherwise.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why an exclusion was applied to this measurement, referencing the specific SLA contract clause. Null if no exclusion was applied.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SLA measurement record was last updated. Audit field for tracking changes and data quality.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual measured value of the SLA metric during the measurement period. Units depend on metric_type (e.g., percentage for availability, milliseconds for latency, percentage for packet loss).']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'The method used to capture the SLA metric. Options include automated network probes, synthetic transactions, passive traffic monitoring, customer-reported incidents, or manual calculation from operational data.']
  measurement_period_end timestamp [note: 'The end date and time of the measurement period for this SLA metric observation. Defines the close of the window during which performance was measured.']
  measurement_period_start timestamp [note: 'The start date and time of the measurement period for this SLA metric observation. Defines the beginning of the window during which performance was measured.']
  measurement_source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or network monitoring platform that captured this SLA measurement (e.g., Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, ServiceNow, third-party monitoring tool). Provides data lineage and audit trail.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this SLA measurement record. Draft measurements are preliminary; validated measurements have passed quality checks; approved measurements are ready for reporting; disputed measurements are under review; finalized measurements are locked for billing and reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about this measurement, such as unusual circumstances, data quality issues, or operational events that affected the measurement.']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The business reporting period to which this measurement belongs (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-03, Week-12-2024). Used for aggregating measurements into periodic SLA reports and dashboards.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The contractually agreed target value for this SLA metric as defined in the SLA contract. Performance is evaluated by comparing measured_value against this target.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for both measured_value and target_value. Common units include percent (for availability, packet loss), milliseconds/seconds (for latency, jitter, MTTR), and mbps/gbps (for throughput). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percent|milliseconds|seconds|minutes|hours|mbps|gbps|packets_per_second — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  validated_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the operations staff member who validated this SLA measurement. Null for automated validations. Provides accountability and audit trail.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SLA measurement was validated and approved for reporting. Null if not yet validated.']

  Note: 'Periodic SLA performance measurement for an enterprise managed service against contracted targets. Captures measurement period, service reference, metric type (availability, MTTR, latency, jitter, packet loss), measured value, target value, breach flag, breach duration, credit entitlement, and data source. Feeds breach detection and enterprise customer SLA reporting.'
}

Table enterprise.sla_breach {
  sla_breach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sla_breach']
  enterprise_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enterprise customer account affected by the SLA breach. Identifies the B2B customer experiencing the service degradation.']
  enterprise_sla_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA contract that was breached. Links to the enterprise SLA contract defining the service level commitments.']
  managed_service_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific managed service instance that experienced the breach. Links to the provisioned service (SD-WAN, MPLS, unified communications, IoT solution) under SLA monitoring.']
  sla_measurement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.sla_measurement. Business justification: SLA breaches are triggered by SLA measurements that fall below contracted thresholds. This FK links each breach event to the specific measurement that triggered it, enabling root cause analysis, measu']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: SLA breach records must reference the SLA profile that defines the breached thresholds for credit calculation and root cause analysis. Enterprise SLA breach management and credit issuance processes re']
  actual_metric_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual measured value of the SLA metric during the breach period. Represents the degraded performance level that triggered the breach.']
  affected_service_component varchar [note: 'Specific service component or network element that experienced the degradation leading to the SLA breach (e.g., router, circuit, application server, SD-WAN edge device).']
  breach_detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA breach was first detected by monitoring systems. Represents the real-world event time when the service metric fell below the contracted threshold.']
  breach_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the SLA breach in minutes. Calculated as the difference between breach end and breach start timestamps. Used for credit calculation and severity assessment.']
  breach_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the end of the SLA breach period when service performance returned to contracted levels. Null if breach is ongoing.']
  breach_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique reference number for the SLA breach event. Used in customer communications, credit memos, and escalation workflows. Format: SLB-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the SLA breach based on duration, impact scope, and deviation from threshold. Determines escalation path and credit calculation tier.']
  breach_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the beginning of the SLA breach period. May differ from detection time if breach is identified retroactively through performance analysis.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA breach event. Tracks progression from detection through resolution and credit issuance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: detected|confirmed|notified|acknowledged|credit_issued|resolved|disputed|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  breach_type varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the SLA breach type. Groups related metric breaches for reporting and trend analysis.']
  contract_termination_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SLA breach (or pattern of breaches) creates a risk of contract termination by the enterprise customer. Triggers executive engagement and retention actions.']
  contracted_threshold_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The contracted threshold value for the SLA metric as defined in the enterprise SLA contract. Represents the minimum acceptable performance level.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA breach event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary credit amount owed to the enterprise customer as compensation for the SLA breach. Calculated based on breach duration, severity, and contract terms.']
  credit_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the SLA credit amount as defined in the enterprise SLA contract. Determines how breach duration and severity translate to monetary compensation.']
  credit_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the SLA credit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  credit_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SLA credit has been issued to the customer account. True if credit has been applied, false if pending or waived.']
  credit_issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA credit was issued to the customer billing account. Null if credit has not yet been issued.']
  customer_acknowledgment_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the enterprise customer has acknowledged receipt of the SLA breach notification. Used for audit trail and dispute prevention.']
  customer_acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enterprise customer acknowledged the SLA breach notification. Null if not yet acknowledged.']
  customer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of customer notification regarding the SLA breach. Tracks whether the enterprise customer has been informed of the breach and credit eligibility.']
  customer_notified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enterprise customer was notified of the SLA breach. Used to track compliance with contractual notification requirements.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the enterprise customer has disputed the SLA breach determination, credit calculation, or root cause analysis. Triggers dispute resolution workflow.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Customer-provided reason for disputing the SLA breach. Captures customer perspective on measurement accuracy, threshold interpretation, or credit calculation.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation level for the SLA breach. Determines which management tier is responsible for resolution and customer communication.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SLA breach requires escalation to senior management or executive account team based on severity, customer tier, or repeat occurrence.']
  impacted_location_count varchar [note: 'Number of enterprise customer locations affected by the SLA breach. Used for impact assessment and credit calculation for multi-site services.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA breach event record was last modified. Tracks updates to status, credit issuance, customer acknowledgment, and resolution details.']
  preventive_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of preventive actions implemented to avoid recurrence of similar SLA breaches. Used for continuous service improvement and customer communication.']
  repeat_breach_count varchar [note: 'Count of previous breaches of the same SLA metric for this service instance within the current measurement period. Used for trend analysis and contract review triggers.']
  repeat_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a repeat breach of the same SLA metric for the same service instance within a defined time window. Triggers enhanced escalation and remediation.']
  resolution_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the resolution action or change request that addressed the root cause of the SLA breach. Links to change management or problem management records.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the underlying service issue was resolved and normal SLA performance was restored. Used to calculate total impact duration.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Root cause category for the SLA breach as determined by network operations and engineering analysis. Used for trend analysis and preventive action planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: network_outage|equipment_failure|capacity_constraint|configuration_error|external_provider|planned_maintenance|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause analysis findings. Provides context for the breach and remediation actions taken.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational support system that detected and recorded the SLA breach (e.g., Nokia NetAct, Ericsson Network Manager, ServiceNow, Netcracker OSS).']
  threshold_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the SLA metric threshold and actual value (e.g., percent for availability, milliseconds for latency, Mbps for throughput). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percent|milliseconds|seconds|minutes|hours|mbps|gbps|packets_per_second — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Confirmed SLA breach event triggered when a managed service metric falls below contracted threshold. Captures breach start/end timestamps, duration, affected service, breach type, root cause category, credit amount, notification status, customer acknowledgment, and resolution reference. Drives automated credit issuance and escalation workflows.'
}

  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the IoT deployment was activated and devices became operational.']
  active_device_count varchar [note: 'Number of IoT devices currently active and transmitting data within this deployment.']
  apn_configuration varchar [note: 'Network Access Point Name configuration string that defines the gateway between the mobile network and the enterprise IoT platform.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month on which billing is calculated and invoiced for this IoT deployment.']
  connectivity_technology varchar [note: 'Primary wireless connectivity technology standard used for device communication in this deployment.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this IoT deployment record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IoT deployment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary values associated with this IoT deployment.']
  deployment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier or reference code for this IoT deployment, used in customer communications and billing.']
  deployment_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name assigned to this IoT deployment for identification and reporting purposes.']
  deployment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this IoT deployment.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the IoT deployment indicating operational readiness and availability.']
  device_count varchar [note: 'Total number of IoT devices or endpoints provisioned under this deployment.']
  geographic_coverage_zones varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic regions or coverage zones where IoT devices in this deployment are authorized to operate.']
  iot_use_case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the business use case or application domain for this IoT deployment.']
  last_device_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent data transmission or activity from any device in this deployment.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this IoT deployment record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this IoT deployment record was most recently updated.']
  managed_platform_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the managed IoT platform or device management system used to monitor and control devices in this deployment.']
  monthly_data_allowance_mb decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monthly data allowance in megabytes allocated to this IoT deployment across all devices.']
  monthly_recurring_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly subscription fee charged for this IoT deployment, excluding usage-based charges.']
  planned_decommission_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for decommissioning or terminating this IoT deployment, if known.']
  platform_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party or internal platform provider delivering device management and analytics services for this deployment.']
  primary_coverage_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary geographic market for this IoT deployment.']
  roaming_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IoT devices in this deployment are permitted to roam on partner networks outside the home coverage area.']
  security_profile varchar [note: 'Security classification level applied to this deployment, defining encryption, authentication, and access control requirements.']
  sim_type varchar [note: 'Type of SIM technology used for device authentication and connectivity in this deployment.']
  technical_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the technical point of contact for operational and troubleshooting matters related to this deployment.']
  technical_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the technical contact for this IoT deployment.']
  uptime_sla_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed network uptime percentage for IoT connectivity services in this deployment.']

  Note: 'Master record for an enterprise IoT solution deployment — tracking the rollout of IoT connectivity services (SIM-based, NB-IoT, LTE-M) for a corporate account\'s use case (fleet tracking, smart metering, industrial automation, asset monitoring). Captures deployment name, IoT use case type, device count, connectivity technology (NB-IoT, LTE-M, 4G), APN configuration, data plan reference, geographic coverage zones, activation status, and managed platform reference.'
}

  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: UC subscriptions have associated price plans (per-seat pricing, feature pack tiers). Required for subscription billing, revenue recognition, price plan migration workflows, and ARPU analysis by UC pro']
  rate_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rate_plan. Business justification: UC subscriptions are billed according to a rate plan governing per-seat charges, SIP trunk rates, and DID number charges. Billing configuration and charge calculation for UC services require this link']

UC service assurance and breach credit processing require this link. The existing sla_tier plain attribute is a']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the UC subscription was activated and became operational for the customer.']

  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of billing for this subscription: monthly, quarterly, annual, or custom interval.']
  call_recording_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether call recording capability is enabled for this subscription (True) or not (False). Used for compliance, quality assurance, and training.']
  call_recording_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days call recordings are retained before automatic deletion. Null if call recording is not enabled.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was cancelled or terminated. Null for active subscriptions.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for subscription cancellation: customer request, non-payment, contract expiry, migration to another service, service quality issues, cost concerns, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|non_payment|contract_expiry|migration|service_quality|cost|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date of the UC subscription contract term. Null for month-to-month or evergreen contracts.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the UC subscription contract term.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the subscription contract in months (e.g., 12, 24, 36). Null for month-to-month agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this subscription record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this subscription (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  did_count varchar [note: 'Total number of DID numbers allocated to this subscription.']
  did_number_block_end varchar [note: 'Ending phone number of the DID block assigned to this UC subscription for inbound calling.']
  did_number_block_start varchar [note: 'Starting phone number of the DID block assigned to this UC subscription for inbound calling.']
  e911_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Enhanced 911 emergency calling service is enabled for this subscription (True) or not (False). Required for VoIP services in many jurisdictions.']
  e911_registered_address varchar [note: 'Full address registered with E911 services for emergency response dispatch. May differ from service address for mobile/remote workers.']
  feature_pack varchar [note: 'Service tier or feature bundle included in the subscription: basic (core calling), standard (calling + voicemail), premium (calling + collaboration), or enterprise (full suite with advanced features).']
  integration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the third-party integration: active (operational), inactive (disabled), pending (configuration in progress), failed (integration error), or not applicable (standalone subscription).']
  integration_type varchar [note: 'Third-party platform or CRM system integrated with the UC subscription: Microsoft Teams, Cisco Webex, Zoom, Google Workspace, Salesforce, standalone (no integration), or custom integration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: microsoft_teams|cisco_webex|zoom|google_workspace|salesforce|standalone|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this subscription record was last updated.']
  monthly_recurring_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monthly recurring revenue for this subscription, calculated as seat count multiplied by monthly seat charge plus any fixed platform fees.']
  monthly_seat_charge decimal(10,2) [note: 'Recurring monthly charge per user seat in the subscription currency. Does not include usage-based charges or one-time fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or comments about the subscription.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact for service notifications, billing, and support escalations.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact or account manager for this UC subscription at the customer organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary business contact for urgent service issues and support.']
  provisioning_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when technical provisioning was successfully completed and the service became operational.']
  provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Current status of technical provisioning and service activation: not started, in progress, completed (fully provisioned), failed (provisioning error), or rolled back (provisioning reversed).']
  qos_class varchar [note: 'Network quality of service tier assigned to this subscription for traffic prioritization and performance guarantees.']
  seat_count varchar [note: 'Number of licensed user seats or concurrent users provisioned under this UC subscription.']
  sip_trunk_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent SIP trunk channels provisioned for voice calls. Null if SIP trunking is not part of the subscription.']
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the UC subscription, used in customer communications and billing.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the UC subscription: active (in service), suspended (temporarily disabled), pending activation (ordered but not yet live), pending cancellation (termination requested), cancelled (terminated), or expired (contract ended).']
  uc_platform_type varchar [note: 'Type of UC platform provisioned: hosted PBX, SIP trunking, UCaaS (Unified Communications as a Service), CCaaS (Contact Center as a Service), video conferencing, or collaboration suite.']
  uptime_percentage_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual uptime percentage target for the UC service (e.g., 99.90 for 99.9% uptime SLA).']
  voip_codec varchar [note: 'Audio codec used for voice compression and transmission: G.711 (uncompressed), G.729 (compressed), Opus (adaptive), or G.722 (wideband).']

  Note: 'Master record for a Unified Communications (UCaaS) subscription provisioned for a corporate account — covering hosted PBX, VoLTE business lines, video conferencing, collaboration tools, and SIP trunking. Captures subscription reference, UC platform type, number of seats/users, DID number block, SIP trunk capacity, feature pack, call recording flag, integration type (Microsoft Teams, Cisco Webex), provisioning status, and monthly seat charge.'
}

Table enterprise.sdwan_topology {
  sdwan_topology_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SD-WAN topology configuration. Primary key for this entity.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: SD-WAN topology deployments generate monthly recurring charges billed to a specific billing account. Enterprise SD-WAN invoice generation and billing reconciliation require this link; sdwan_topology c']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate account that owns this SD-WAN topology. Links to the enterprise customer master.']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: SD-WAN topologies are governed by commercial contracts defining pricing, term, SLA, and service levels. Currently sdwan_topology has contract_term_months and contract_expiration_date but no contract F']
  ip_address_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.ip_address_pool. Business justification: SD-WAN overlay IP address management: SD-WAN topologies require IP pools for overlay tunnel endpoints, VPN addressing, and site-to-site routing. This link supports IPAM for SD-WAN overlays, capacity p']
  network_equipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.network_equipment. Business justification: SD-WAN topologies reference orchestrator/controller equipment for centralized management. Network operations require equipment tracking for software upgrades, configuration backups, and disaster recov']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: SD-WAN topologies are provisioned against specific product offerings defining underlay transport types, QoS classes, and SLA parameters. Offering-level FK is required for service activation, billing, ']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_profile. Business justification: SD-WAN topologies have SLA commitments (latency, packet loss, availability) governed by an SLA profile. SD-WAN operations and breach management require this link. The existing sla_tier plain attribute']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this SD-WAN topology was activated and became operational for customer traffic. Marks the start of service delivery and billing.']
  backup_underlay_transport varchar [note: 'Secondary or backup physical transport technology for failover and redundancy. Common patterns include MPLS primary with broadband or LTE backup. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mpls|broadband|dedicated_internet|lte|5g|satellite|none|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values associated with this topology (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cloud_on_ramp_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct cloud connectivity (cloud on-ramp) is enabled for optimized access to public cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) without backhauling through data center.']
  cloud_providers_integrated varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of public cloud providers integrated with this topology for direct connectivity (e.g., \'AWS,Azure,GCP\'). Enables optimized cloud application performance.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current service contract for this topology expires. Triggers renewal or termination processes.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the service contract for this topology in months. Common terms are 12, 24, 36, or 60 months. Affects pricing, early termination fees, and renewal terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this topology record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when this SD-WAN topology was or will be deactivated and removed from service. Null for active topologies.']
  encryption_key_length varchar [note: 'Length of the encryption key in bits (e.g., 128, 256). Longer keys provide stronger security but may impact performance. Common values are 128-bit (AES-128) and 256-bit (AES-256).']
  encryption_standard varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol used to secure overlay traffic between sites. IPsec is most common for site-to-site VPN; TLS for application-layer encryption; MACsec for layer-2 encryption; some vendors use proprietary encryption.']
  failover_mode varchar [note: 'Failover behavior when primary underlay path fails. Active-active uses all paths simultaneously; active-standby keeps backup idle until primary fails; load-balanced distributes traffic across paths; manual requires operator intervention.']
  failover_threshold_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum acceptable latency in milliseconds before triggering failover to backup path. Typical values range from 100ms to 500ms depending on application sensitivity.']
  failover_threshold_packet_loss_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable packet loss percentage before triggering failover to backup path. Typical values range from 1% to 5% depending on application tolerance.']
  firewall_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether stateful firewall inspection is enabled for traffic traversing this topology. Firewall rules control inter-site and internet-bound traffic based on source, destination, application, and user identity.']
  ids_ips_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether intrusion detection and prevention capabilities are enabled. IDS/IPS inspects traffic for known attack signatures and anomalous behavior, blocking or alerting on threats.']
  internet_breakout_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing where internet-bound traffic exits to the public internet. Centralized routes all traffic through hub data center; local allows direct internet access at branch; hybrid or application-based routes by application type.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this topology record was last modified. Tracks configuration changes, updates, and maintenance activities.']
  monthly_recurring_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monthly recurring charge for this SD-WAN topology service in the billing currency. Includes all recurring fees for connectivity, equipment, management, and support.']
  orchestration_platform varchar [note: 'SD-WAN orchestration and management platform used to provision, configure, and monitor this topology. Determines available features, policy capabilities, and integration points. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cisco_viptela|vmware_velocloud|fortinet_fortimanager|silver_peak|versa_networks|citrix_sdwan|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  orchestration_platform_version varchar [note: 'Software version of the orchestration platform managing this topology. Critical for feature compatibility, security patching, and support lifecycle management.']
  overlay_routing_protocol varchar [note: 'Routing protocol used within the SD-WAN overlay to exchange reachability information between sites. May be standard (BGP, OSPF) or vendor-proprietary.']
  primary_underlay_transport varchar [note: 'Primary physical transport technology for this topology. Typically the highest-quality, lowest-latency path (e.g., MPLS for mission-critical traffic). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mpls|broadband|dedicated_internet|lte|5g|satellite|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  qos_classes_configured varchar [note: 'Number of distinct QoS classes (traffic classes) configured in this topology. Typical enterprise deployments use 4-8 classes (e.g., real-time voice, interactive video, business-critical data, best-effort).']
  qos_profile_name varchar [note: 'Name of the QoS profile applied to this topology. QoS profiles define traffic classification, prioritization, queuing, and bandwidth allocation rules to ensure service level objectives for critical applications (e.g., voice, video, ERP).']
  security_policy_name varchar [note: 'Name of the security policy applied to this topology. Security policies define firewall rules, intrusion detection/prevention settings, URL filtering, malware protection, and data loss prevention controls.']
  site_count varchar [note: 'Total number of enterprise sites (locations) participating in this SD-WAN topology. Includes hub sites, spoke sites, and any branch or remote office locations.']
  sla_availability_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual availability target for this topology expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.9%, 99.99%). Defines the minimum uptime guarantee over the measurement period.']
  sla_jitter_target_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum acceptable latency variation (jitter) in milliseconds as defined in the SLA. Critical for real-time applications like voice and video conferencing.']
  sla_latency_target_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum acceptable round-trip latency in milliseconds as defined in the SLA. Typically measured between hub and spoke sites or between critical site pairs.']
  sla_packet_loss_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable packet loss percentage as defined in the SLA. Typical enterprise SLAs guarantee less than 0.1% to 1% packet loss.']
  spoke_site_count varchar [note: 'Number of spoke sites (branch offices, remote locations) in hub-and-spoke or hybrid topologies. Spoke sites typically connect to hub sites for centralized services and internet breakout.']
  topology_design_type varchar [note: 'Logical design pattern of the SD-WAN topology. Hub-and-spoke centralizes traffic through hub sites; full-mesh provides direct connectivity between all sites; partial-mesh selectively connects high-traffic site pairs; point-to-point connects two sites; hybrid combines multiple patterns.']
  topology_identifier varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code for this topology, used in customer-facing documentation, service orders, and billing. May be customer-provided or system-generated.']
  topology_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name for this SD-WAN topology configuration, typically reflecting the customer organization or deployment scope (e.g., \'Global HQ Mesh\', \'Regional Hub-Spoke\').']
  topology_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this SD-WAN topology. Active indicates fully operational; provisioning indicates deployment in progress; suspended indicates temporary service hold; decommissioning indicates teardown in progress.']
  traffic_steering_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how the SD-WAN controller steers traffic across available underlay paths. Application-aware uses deep packet inspection to route by application; latency/bandwidth/cost-based optimize for specific metrics; manual uses static rules.']
  underlay_transport_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of physical transport technologies used as underlay for this SD-WAN overlay (e.g., \'MPLS,Broadband,LTE\'). Underlay diversity enables path redundancy and failover.']
  url_filtering_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether URL filtering (web content filtering) is enabled to block access to malicious, inappropriate, or non-business websites based on category and reputation.']
  vcpe_ucpe_deployment_model varchar [note: 'Deployment model for customer premises equipment. vCPE runs network functions as virtual machines on generic hardware; uCPE is a physical appliance hosting multiple virtualized functions; hybrid combines both; none indicates traditional dedicated appliances.']

  Note: 'Logical SD-WAN network topology for a corporate account — hub-and-spoke or full-mesh design, site count, underlay transports (MPLS, broadband, LTE backup), overlay routing policy, QoS profile, security policy (firewall, IDS/IPS), vCPE/uCPE device references, and orchestration platform (Cisco Viptela, VMware VeloCloud, Fortinet). Governs traffic engineering, failover behavior, and policy-based routing across enterprise sites.'
}

Table enterprise.discount_scheme {
  discount_scheme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for discount_scheme']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Volume and loyalty discount schemes are applied to bundles. Linking bundle_id enables bundle-level discount calculation, minimum commitment threshold enforcement, and renewal eligibility checks — core']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Discount schemes apply to specific catalog products (e.g., 20% off fiber 1Gbps plans). Required for pricing engine rules, discount eligibility validation, and revenue impact analysis by product.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corporate account to which this discount scheme is assigned.']
  enterprise_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.enterprise_contract. Business justification: Discount schemes are typically negotiated as part of commercial contracts. While discount_scheme already links to corporate_account, adding the contract FK enables tracking which specific contract gov']
  enterprise_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enterprise.segment. Business justification: Discount schemes can be segment-specific (e.g., government discounts, SMB promotions, enterprise volume discounts). This FK enables segment-based discount eligibility rules and segment-specific pricin']
  offering_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.offering. Business justification: Discount schemes are applied at the offering level to capture offer-specific pricing rules. Linking offering_id enables offer-specific discount validation, stacking rules enforcement, and revenue impa']
  price_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.price_plan. Business justification: Discount schemes are applied against specific price plans to calculate net charges. Linking price_plan_id enables billing systems to apply correct discounts during invoice generation — a named process']
  applicable_product_category varchar [note: 'Product or service categories to which this discount scheme applies (e.g., \'SD-WAN Services\', \'MPLS Circuits\', \'Unified Communications\', \'IoT Solutions\', \'All Enterprise Services\'). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sd_wan|mpls|unified_communications|iot_solutions|managed_services|voice_services|data_services|cloud_services|security_services|all_services — promote to reference product]']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or committee that approved this discount scheme (e.g., \'VP Enterprise Sales\', \'Pricing Committee\', \'CFO\').']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the discount scheme was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount scheme (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract or agreement type associated with this discount scheme: government framework, commercial enterprise, strategic account, standard corporate, or custom negotiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discount scheme record was first created in the system.']
  discount_fixed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount amount applied when discount_type is fixed_amount. Currency is defined by billing_currency_code.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when discount_type is percentage-based. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 15.00 for 15%).']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount mechanism: percentage-based, fixed monetary amount, tiered based on usage or spend, volume-based, loyalty reward, or promotional campaign.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the discount scheme expires or is no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended scheme.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the discount scheme becomes active and applicable to qualifying accounts.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this discount scheme record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discount scheme record was last modified in the system.']
  maximum_discount_cap decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total discount amount that can be applied under this scheme within a billing period, regardless of calculated discount. Null indicates no cap.']
  minimum_commitment_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum spend, usage volume, or contract value required to qualify for this discount scheme. Unit of measure defined by threshold_unit_of_measure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or internal comments related to the discount scheme.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for discount application when multiple schemes are eligible. Lower numbers indicate higher priority (1 = highest priority).']
  proration_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount can be prorated for partial billing periods. True if proration is allowed, False if discount applies only to full periods.']
  renewal_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount scheme is eligible for automatic renewal upon contract renewal. True if eligible, False if requires re-approval.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the discount scheme, used for external reference and integration with billing systems.']
  scheme_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the discount scheme, including business rationale, eligibility criteria, and terms of application.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the discount scheme (e.g., \'Government Framework Pricing 2024\', \'Volume Discount Tier 3\', \'Strategic Account Loyalty Discount\').']
  scheme_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the discount scheme: draft (under review), active (in effect), suspended (temporarily inactive), expired (past end date), or cancelled (terminated before expiry).']
  stacking_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount scheme can be combined (stacked) with other discount schemes on the same account. True if stacking is permitted, False if mutually exclusive.']
  stacking_rules varchar [note: 'Detailed rules governing how this discount scheme interacts with other discounts, including precedence, maximum combined discount caps, and exclusions.']
  threshold_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum commitment threshold (e.g., currency amount, data volume in gigabytes, number of circuits, user seats, connected devices, or contract duration in months).']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Tier level for tiered discount schemes (e.g., 1 for bronze, 2 for silver, 3 for gold). Null for non-tiered schemes.']
  tier_threshold_lower decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the usage or spend range for this tier. Applicable only to tiered discount schemes.']
  tier_threshold_upper decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the usage or spend range for this tier. Null indicates no upper limit. Applicable only to tiered discount schemes.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the discount scheme definition, incremented with each modification to track scheme evolution and changes over time.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this discount scheme record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master definition of a discount scheme applicable to a corporate account or commercial contract — including volume discounts, loyalty discounts, government framework pricing, and strategic account pricing. Captures scheme name, discount type (percentage, fixed amount, tiered), applicable product categories, minimum commitment threshold, discount percentage or amount, effective period, approval authority, and stacking rules with other discounts. Governs enterprise-specific pricing distinct from standard consumer rate plans.'
}

Table enterprise.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for segment']
  account_management_model varchar [note: 'Customer relationship management approach assigned to this segment. Self-service for digital-only interaction, inside sales for phone-based support, dedicated account manager for personalized service, strategic account team for multi-stakeholder engagement, partner-managed for channel-driven accounts.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status for new or modified segment definitions. Draft segments are under development, pending approval awaits business owner sign-off, approved segments are ready for operational use, rejected segments require rework.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment definition was approved. Null if segment has not been approved. Supports compliance and governance audit trails.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the business owner or executive who approved this segment definition for operational use. Null if segment has not been approved.']
  churn_risk_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Churn risk score threshold (0-100 scale) above which accounts in this segment trigger retention intervention workflows. Lower thresholds indicate more proactive retention strategies for high-value segments.']
  contract_term_minimum_months varchar [note: 'Minimum contract commitment period in months required for accounts in this segment. Longer terms may qualify for better pricing. Null if segment has no minimum term requirement.']
  contract_term_standard_months varchar [note: 'Standard contract duration in months typically offered to accounts in this segment. Represents the most common commitment period for this customer class.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this segment definition. Supports accountability and change management processes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether credit verification is mandatory for new accounts in this segment during onboarding. True for segments requiring financial vetting, false for pre-approved segments such as government or established enterprise relationships.']
  deposit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a security deposit is required for new accounts in this segment. True for higher-risk segments or those with limited credit history, false for established enterprise segments with strong credit profiles.']
  discount_tier varchar [note: 'Standard discount tier available to accounts in this segment. Higher tiers receive greater discounts on products and services. Custom tier indicates individually negotiated discount structures for strategic accounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active segments. Used for historical reporting and migration tracking when segmentation strategies evolve.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment definition became active and available for account classification. Supports temporal segmentation strategy changes and historical analysis.']
  go_to_market_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary sales and distribution approach for acquiring and serving accounts in this segment. Direct sales uses internal sales force, channel partner leverages resellers and agents, hybrid combines both, digital-only uses web-based acquisition, strategic alliance involves co-selling with technology partners.']
  industry_vertical_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of industry vertical codes that qualify for this segment. Used for vertical-based segmentation. Examples: FINANCE, HEALTHCARE, MANUFACTURING, GOVERNMENT, EDUCATION. Null if segment is not restricted by industry vertical.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this segment definition. Tracks ownership of segmentation strategy changes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last updated. Tracks changes to segment definitions, criteria, or business rules over time.']
  maximum_annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annual revenue threshold in base currency for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Defines upper bound for revenue band. Null if segment has no upper revenue limit or does not use revenue criteria.']
  maximum_employee_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of employees for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Defines upper bound for size band. Null if segment has no upper employee limit or does not use employee count criteria.']
  minimum_annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annual revenue threshold in base currency for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Used for revenue-based segmentation. Null if segment does not use revenue criteria.']
  minimum_employee_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of employees for an enterprise account to qualify for this segment. Used for size-based segmentation. Null if segment does not use employee count criteria.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, special considerations, migration notes, or strategic rationale not captured in structured fields. Used for knowledge transfer and decision documentation.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Business unit, department, or executive role responsible for strategy and performance of this segment. Examples: Enterprise Sales VP, Government Sales Director, Mid-Market Business Unit, Wholesale Operations.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days from invoice date for accounts in this segment. Common values include 15, 30, 45, or 60 days. Larger enterprise segments typically receive extended payment terms.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing strategy applied to accounts in this segment. Standard uses list pricing, volume discount applies graduated discounts based on spend, custom negotiated allows bespoke pricing for strategic accounts, tiered uses predefined price bands, flat rate offers fixed monthly fees.']
  reporting_category varchar [note: 'Financial and operational reporting category for revenue and performance metrics aggregation. Used for executive dashboards, regulatory filings, and investor reporting. Examples: Enterprise, Business, Government, Wholesale.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment for operational use and system integration. Examples: LRG_ENT, MID_MKT, GOV_FED, SME_TIER1.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the segment characteristics, target customer profile, and business rationale for segmentation. Includes criteria such as employee count, revenue thresholds, service complexity, and strategic importance.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the enterprise segment. Examples: Large Enterprise, Mid-Market, Government Federal, Small and Medium Enterprise Tier 1, Mobile Virtual Network Operator (MVNO) Host, Wholesale Carrier.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active segments are in use for account classification and go-to-market strategies. Deprecated segments are being phased out. Proposed segments are under review for future implementation.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segmentation methodology used. Revenue-based segments by Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) or Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR), size-based by employee count, vertical-based by industry, service-based by solution complexity, strategic for key accounts, or hybrid combining multiple criteria.']
  service_eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining which services and solutions are available to accounts in this segment. May include eligibility for Software-Defined Wide Area Network (SD-WAN), Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS), dedicated fiber, unified communications, Internet of Things (IoT) platforms, managed security services, and premium Service Level Agreement (SLA) tiers.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Default Service Level Agreement tier for accounts in this segment. Defines response times, uptime guarantees, Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) commitments, and escalation procedures. Mission-critical tier provides highest priority support and fastest restoration times.']
  support_channel_access varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of support channels available to accounts in this segment. May include web portal, mobile app, phone support, email, chat, dedicated hotline, on-site support, Network Operations Center (NOC) direct access, and technical account manager.']
  target_customer_lifetime_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expected Customer Lifetime Value in base currency for accounts in this segment over a standard planning horizon. Used for investment prioritization and customer acquisition cost justification. Null if not calculated for this segment.']
  upsell_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for proactive upsell and cross-sell campaigns targeting accounts in this segment. Critical priority segments receive dedicated sales resources and personalized offers. Low priority segments receive automated digital campaigns only.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining enterprise customer segmentation taxonomy — classifying corporate accounts by size, revenue band, industry vertical, and service eligibility. Segments include Large Enterprise, Mid-Market, Government, SME, MVNO Host, and Wholesale. Drives differentiated pricing, account management models, and go-to-market strategies across the B2B customer base.'
}

Table enterprise.topology_site_membership {
  topology_site_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the topology_site_membership association']
  sdwan_topology_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SD-WAN topology that this site is enrolled in.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the enterprise site that is a member of this topology.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this specific site was activated and enrolled into this specific topology. Distinct from enterprise_site.activation_date (when the site became operational) and sdwan_topology.activation_date (when the topology went live). Tracks when the site-topology membership became operational.']
  bandwidth_allocation_mbps decimal [note: 'The bandwidth slice (in Mbps) allocated to this site within this topology. This is the provisioned capacity for this site\'s participation in the topology — distinct from enterprise_site.bandwidth_requirement_mbps (the site\'s total requirement) and is governed by the topology\'s overall capacity plan.']
  cpe_device_count varchar [note: 'Total number of CPE devices (physical or virtual) deployed across all sites in this topology. Each site typically has one or more CPE devices for SD-WAN edge functions. [Moved from sdwan_topology: This is a derived aggregate count of CPE devices across all enrolled sites. It should be computed from topology_site_membership joined to enterprise_site CPE inventory, not stored as a denormalized count on the topology. Removing it eliminates a maintenance burden when sites are added or removed.]']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when this site\'s membership in this topology was or will be terminated. Enables historical tracking of which sites were enrolled in which topologies over time, supporting audit, billing reconciliation, and migration history.']
  hub_site_count varchar [note: 'Number of hub sites in hub-and-spoke or hybrid topologies. Hub sites typically host centralized services (data centers, cloud on-ramps, security stacks) and aggregate traffic from spoke sites. [Moved from sdwan_topology: This is a derived aggregate count of hub-role memberships in this topology. It should be computed from topology_site_membership records where site_role = HUB, not stored as a denormalized count on the topology. Storing it on sdwan_topology creates a data integrity risk when memberships change.]']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this site\'s enrollment in the topology. Tracks whether the site is actively participating, pending provisioning, suspended, or being migrated to a new topology. Enables phased migration tracking where a site transitions between topologies.']
  qos_profile_override varchar [note: 'Site-specific QoS profile that overrides the topology-level QoS configuration for this site. Allows individual sites to have differentiated traffic prioritization within the same topology. Belongs to the membership record because it is specific to this site\'s participation in this topology, not to the site or topology independently.']
  site_role varchar [note: 'The functional role this site plays within the SD-WAN topology. Hub sites aggregate traffic from spoke sites; spoke sites are branch endpoints; edge sites have direct internet breakout. This role belongs to the membership, not the site or topology alone — the same site can be a hub in one topology and a spoke in another.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Role-based enrollment of an enterprise_site within an sdwan_topology. It captures the operational membership record that governs how a specific site participates in a specific SD-WAN topology — including the site\'s role (hub, spoke, edge), its activation within that topology, its allocated bandwidth slice, and any site-level QoS overrides. Each record links one sdwan_topology to one enterprise_site with attributes that exist only in the context of this site-topology relationship, enabling multi-topology deployments, DR topologies, and phased migrations to be modeled accurately.. Existence Justification: In SD-WAN managed services, a topology is explicitly a multi-site construct — a single topology governs traffic engineering across many enterprise sites simultaneously. Critically, the reverse is also operationally real: an enterprise site can participate in multiple topologies concurrently (e.g., a primary production topology and a DR/failover topology, or during phased migrations where a site is enrolled in both the legacy and new topology). The business actively manages \'site membership\' in a topology as an operational concept — provisioning, activating, and deactivating individual site enrollments with site-specific roles and configurations.'
}
